steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'praising' or 'the act of praise'
Added 'al' to make it specific/definite praise
Added damma to show this word is the subject
This is the base word Allah, which is a proper name and doesn't come from a standard root pattern
Added ل at the beginning which combines with the ال of Allah, causing the first ل to get a shadda
Added vowel marks to show proper pronunciation and grammatical role. This word shows who should receive the praise (الْحَمْدُ) mentioned before it.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence, like 'who' or 'which' in English. In this ayah, it connects 'Allah' with what He did (sent down the Book).
Adding ال with shadda makes it more specific, pointing to a particular person or thing. Here it refers back to Allah mentioned in the previous word.
The elongation mark (ٓ) helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding hamza at the start makes someone cause the action - instead of something coming down by itself, someone is making it come down. Allah is doing the action of sending down the Book in this ayah.

This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else. In this ayah, it connects 'sent down' (أَنزَلَ) with 'His servant' (عَبْدِهِ), showing that Allah sent the Book down upon His servant.
Added an alif symbol (ٰ) after the ى which doesn't change the meaning but helps with pronunciation, showing that the ى is pronounced as a long 'aa' sound
Root letters got vowels to make a noun meaning 'servant' from the basic idea of serving
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by something
Added هِ at the end to show it belongs to Him (Allah). This word appears in the phrase 'upon His servant' in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels and added a long alif to make it into a noun representing the result of writing
Added ٱلْ to make it specific ('the book') and fatha at the end to show it receives the action of being 'sent down'
This is a particle that makes something negative in the past, like saying 'did not' in English
Added 'wa' at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This word negates يَجْعَل (make/put) in this ayah, changing its meaning to 'and He did not make'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb

steps.m: he makes/sets (negated by لَمْ in this ayah)
steps.n: Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start. When لَمْ comes before this word, it changes the meaning to 'did not make' in the past.
Starting with the basic direction particle that shows something is 'for' or 'to' something else
The لِ combines with ال (the) to form لَّ with a shadda, showing emphasis and meaning 'for the'
Added هُ to show this is 'for him' specifically
Added a small و (ۥ) after هُ as a pronunciation helper to show the sound is elongated slightly
Changes from verb 'to be crooked' to noun meaning 'crookedness'
Added ا at the end to show this is the object being described in the ayah - there is no crookedness in the Book
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
The و changes to ي and gets doubled with shadda to show intensity of the quality
The ًا shows this word is describing the state or quality of something (in this case, describing how the Quran is perfectly straight/correct in its guidance)

steps.m: to warn or alert about danger

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added يُ at start to show present/future tense. The middle letter lost its vowel mark to make pronunciation easier. This verb is warning about 'severe punishment' (بَأْسًا شَدِيدًا)

steps.m: in order that he warns (about بَأْسًا شَدِيدًا)
steps.n: Added لِ to show this is the purpose or reason for something

steps.m: in order that he warns (about بَأْسًا شَدِيدًا)
steps.n: Added emphasis by doubling the ل with a shadda

steps.m: in order that he warns (about بَأْسًا شَدِيدًا)
steps.n: Added a small diagonal line (fatha) at the end because the word starts with لِ (in order to)

steps.m: might/punishment (object of لِّيُنذِرَ - to warn of)
steps.n: Turns the action into a noun. This word is what's being warned about in the phrase 'to warn of a severe punishment'.

steps.m: a might/punishment (object of لِّيُنذِرَ - to warn of)
steps.n: Adding ًا shows this is what's being warned about and makes it indefinite (like saying 'a punishment' instead of 'the punishment')
Added vowel marks (fatha) to the root letters to make it mean 'he became strong/intense' in the past
Added ي in the middle to make it describe a strong quality

steps.m: severe punishment (describes بَأْسًا/might/punishment in the ayah)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is describing the punishment (بَأْسًا) mentioned in the ayah
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it connects to the word لَّدُنْهُ (His presence) to show something coming from Allah's presence.
Added a strong emphasis on the م by doubling it with a shadda. This happens because the word before it ends with a similar sound.
Becomes a noun meaning presence or nearness
Adds vowels to make it a noun meaning 'from the presence of'

steps.m: from near, from the presence of (with emphasis)
steps.n: Added shadda to emphasize the meaning
Added هُ to show possession by Him (Allah)

steps.m: to give good news, to bring glad tidings

steps.n: Added shadda to show intensive meaning
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: that he give good news (connected to لِّيُنذِرَ - to warn)
steps.n: Changed ending to َ to show this is linked to the purpose mentioned in لِّيُنذِرَ (to warn)

steps.m: and that he give good news (connected to لِّيُنذِرَ - to warn)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this verb with the previous verb لِّيُنذِرَ (to warn)

steps.m: to trust, feel secure, believe in

steps.n: Added مُ at the start to show who does the action, like changing 'believe' to 'believer' in English

steps.m: believers (object of يُبَشِّرَ - gives glad tidings to)
steps.n: Added ين to show we're talking about many believers. This word receives the action of giving glad tidings.

steps.m: the believers (object of يُبَشِّرَ - gives glad tidings to)
steps.n: Added 'the' (ال) at the start to talk about specific believers, and a mark at the end because it's receiving the action
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ال with a shadda makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين at the end makes it plural. This word connects to يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/work) in the next part of the ayah, describing who is doing the action.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
The يَ at the start and the pattern of vowels shows this is happening in the present/future

steps.m: they work/do (referring to those who believe in the previous ayah)
steps.n: The ون shows this is about a group of people (in this case, referring back to the believers mentioned before)
This pattern shows someone/something that possesses this quality. Forms the active participle meaning 'one who is good/righteous'.
The ات makes it feminine plural, commonly used for abstract concepts and actions
Adding ال makes it definite. The ص gets a shadda because it's a sun letter that assimilates with the ل of ال.

steps.m: the righteous deeds (that are being done)
steps.n: This word is what's being acted upon in the phrase 'they do righteous deeds'. The ِ shows it's connected to the verb يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/perform).
This is the basic particle that connects parts of a sentence, like saying 'that' in English
Adding the shadda (doubled letter) and fatha makes this word stronger, showing that what comes after it is definitely true. Here it emphasizes that the believers will surely have a good reward.
This is a basic particle that shows direction or purpose

steps.m: for them/to them (referring to الْمُؤْمِنِينَ - the believers)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which means 'them' to show who the action is for. In this ayah, it refers back to the believers (الْمُؤْمِنِينَ) mentioned earlier.

steps.m: to give a reward or payment

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning the reward itself

steps.m: a reward (object of أَنَّ لَهُمْ - that they have)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what they have (the reward). This connects to أَنَّ لَهُمْ (that they have) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic adjective form
Added tanween alif to show this is describing the reward (أَجْرًا) that the believers will receive. The ًا ending matches أَجْرًا because it's describing it.

steps.m: to stay or remain in a place

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Changes verb to describe someone who does the action
Adds ين to show this describes multiple people who are staying. This word relates to the word فِيهِ (in it) in the ayah.
The doubled م adds emphasis to show they are definitely staying
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. The kasra (ِ) under ف shows it's a preposition.

steps.m: in it (referring to the reward/paradise mentioned before)
steps.n: Added هِ (hi) at the end to mean 'it'. This refers back to the paradise/reward mentioned earlier in the context. The kasra (ِ) on ه shows it's connected to something.
Added fatha to connect the letters and make it a noun

steps.m: forever (describing how long they will stay)
steps.n: Added an -an ending to make it mean 'forever' as a description (like saying 'they stayed forever')

steps.m: to warn or alert someone about danger

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Changes to subjunctive mood, often used after 'that' or 'to'
Added وَ to connect this with what came before it
This is the basic form of the word that points to someone or something and connects it to what comes next
Added ٱلَّ at the beginning to make it specific and definite
Added ينَ at the end to make it refer to multiple people. This word is connected to يُنذِرَ (warns/to warn) in this ayah, showing who is being warned.
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb
Changed from قَوَلَ to قَالُوا by flipping و to ا and adding وا at the end to show multiple people said it (they) instead of one person (he). Connected to يُنذِرَ (warns) in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
The ت combines with أ to make a stronger meaning of 'taking'
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the ل is doubled with a shadda
The word 'Allah' gets a special mark (ٱ) at the start and a double L sound (ّ). The U sound at the end (ُ) shows it's doing the action in the sentence
Adding vowel marks makes this a past tense verb
The ًا shows this is the object being taken. In this ayah, it's what some people falsely claimed Allah took.
This is the basic form of the word ما, which is a particle used to ask questions or connect ideas
The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to show this is being used as a strong negation. In this ayah, it's saying they have NO knowledge of something.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added هُم at the end to show who the preposition is referring to. This connects to the word عِلْمٍ (knowledge) in the ayah, showing they have no knowledge.
Basic pronoun form indicating 'him' or 'it'

steps.m: with/by it (referring to what they say)
steps.n: Preposition بِ (with/by) added to pronoun

steps.m: with/by it (referring to what they say)
steps.n: Added small alif (ۦ) as a pronunciation guide to show the vowel is lengthened slightly

This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'their mouths' (أَفْوٰهِهِمْ) to the verb 'comes out' (تَخْرُجُ), showing that the words are coming out from their mouths.
Forms a noun meaning 'knowledge' that is being acted upon by مِنْ (from/of) in this ayah
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is talking about knowledge in general, not specific knowledge
Added fatha to show clear pronunciation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what comes after it. This word connects to the verb كَبُرَتْ (it became great) in this ayah.

steps.m: to nurture/care for (as a father does)

steps.n: Changes from the root meaning 'to care for' into the simple word for 'father'
Changes from 'father' to its irregular plural form 'fathers' - like how 'child' becomes 'children' in English
Adds preposition لِ meaning 'to/for'
Adds kasra to prepare for possession suffix
Adds plural possession suffix هِمْ meaning 'their'

steps.m: to be/become big, great, or serious
it became great/serious (describing كَلِمَةً - a word)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) on middle letter to show this describes a quality or state changing. This word describes how serious/grievous the word/statement (كَلِمَةً) is.

steps.m: it (feminine) became grievous (describing كَلِمَةً - a word)
steps.n: Added ت to match with كَلِمَةً which is feminine. This shows the whole phrase means 'grievous is the word that comes from their mouths'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from a verb (speaking) to a noun meaning one single word

steps.m: a word (object of كَبُرَتْ - it became great)
steps.n: Shows this word is being acted upon by the verb كَبُرَتْ (it became great) in this ayah
This is the basic concept of leaving or exiting something
Added vowels to make it past tense 'he exited' - like adding -ed in English
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowel pattern
Changes from 'he' to 'she' by replacing يَ with تَ at start

steps.m: from (connects عِلْمٍ to the previous phrase)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the word عِلْمٍ (knowledge) to show they don't have any knowledge of what they're saying.
Basic root letters got vowels to make the word for 'a mouth'
Changes from single 'mouth' to multiple 'mouths' by adding alif at start and lengthening middle with alif
Added kasra because this word comes after the preposition مِنْ (from)
Added the possessive pronoun هِمْ meaning 'their'
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition or possibility. Here it connects to the verb يَقُولُونَ (they say) to show what they might say.
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense, and changed vowels to show ongoing action. The middle letter changes back to و.
Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people (they) are doing the action
This is the basic form of the word that means 'except' - it excludes what comes after it from what came before
Adding the shadda and alif makes the exception stronger and connects it to what follows. Here it emphasizes that they are saying nothing but lies.
Changes from verb 'to lie' into noun meaning 'a lie'

steps.m: a lie (object of يَقُولُونَ - they say)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is what is being said. In this ayah, it's what 'they say' (يَقُولُونَ)
This is a compound particle made up of multiple parts
The فَ shows this continues from a previous thought. Since there's no ayah before this, it connects to an implied meaning like 'Given what was mentioned before...'

steps.m: and perhaps you (are destroying yourself - بٰخِعٌ نَّفْسَكَ)
steps.n: The كَ at the end means 'you' (when talking to one male). This connects to the next word بٰخِعٌ (destroying) where 'you' is the one doing the action.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed the middle vowel from fatha to kasra to show ongoing action

steps.m: one who destroys himself (from grief for نَّفْسَكَ)
steps.n: Changed from past tense verb to active participle (doer of action). Added tanween (double damma) to show it's indefinite. Shows who is destroying themselves through grief.
This refers to one's soul or self
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun. The fatha at the end shows it's being acted upon.
Added ك at the end to show it belongs to you. This word is what's being destroyed in the phrase 'destroying yourself'.
This is a basic preposition showing position or relationship. Here it connects with آثَارِهِمْ (their footsteps/tracks) to show following
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation - fatha on ع and ل
Added alif maksura (ى with small alif above) at the end to show this is the standard written form
Added a small vertical line (called a maddah) above to show the vowel is stretched in pronunciation
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning one trace or mark
Changed to broken plural form by adding long alif at beginning and after second root letter
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added suffix هِمْ to show these traces belong to them

Added kasra (ِ) to show this is the conditional particle 'if' that introduces the condition in this ayah. The condition it introduces is 'if they do not believe', followed by the result earlier in the ayah about the Prophet's distress.
This is a negative word that makes the verb يُؤْمِنُوا۟ (they believe) into 'they did not believe'. It changes the verb to talk about the past.
Added emphasis to the ل by doubling it (shown by the ّ mark). This happens because the word before it ends in a similar sound.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and adding ؤ in middle
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
This is the basic demonstrative word meaning 'this' that points to something nearby
Added هٰ at the beginning to make it clearer and more emphatic when pointing to something
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is the means or instrument. In this ayah, it refers to 'this narrative' that the people are rejecting.

steps.m: to happen, to occur, to tell
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (to happen) to an adjective/noun meaning 'new' or 'a narration'
The word gets a kasra (i) sound at the end because another word is describing it - like saying 'the speech' instead of just 'speech'

steps.m: the narrative, the speech (being described by أَسَفًا)
steps.n: Added the definite article ال to specify this particular narrative
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters and form the noun for the feeling of sorrow

steps.m: in sorrow (describing how بٰخِعٌ/destroying is done)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this describes how the action of بٰخِعٌ (destroying/killing) is being done - with sorrow or sorrowfully
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah definitely made what's on earth as decoration.
The ا at the end shows this is about 'we/us'. In this ayah, it refers to Allah using the royal 'We' to show His majesty, connecting to the next word جَعَلْنَا (We made).

steps.m: to make something into something else, or to put/place something somewhere

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb showing one person did the action
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This connects back to إِنَّا (indeed we) at the start of the verse.
This is a relative pronoun that connects 'We made' (جَعَلْنَا) with what comes after it. It's like saying 'We made that which is on the earth...'
Added fatha mark to show clear pronunciation
This is the base form indicating height or elevation

Adding ى at the end makes it a preposition showing position above something. In this ayah, it shows the relationship between what's being placed (ما) and where it's being placed (الْأَرْضِ/the earth).
Added marks (fatha and sukun) to make it a complete word meaning 'earth'
Added 'al' to make it definite and kasra at the end because it comes after عَلَى (on/upon) in the ayah
Added kasra under first letter and connected letters to form basic noun

steps.m: a single beautiful thing (object of جَعَلْنَا)
steps.n: Added ة to show one specific instance. This word is what 'We made' (جَعَلْنَا) in the ayah.

steps.m: as a beautiful adornment (object of جَعَلْنَا)
steps.n: Added tanween (ً) to show this is what was made beautiful in the sentence 'We made what is on the earth as a beautiful adornment'
Basic particle showing direction or purpose. Here it connects to the word زِينَةً (adornment) to show what something is for.
Adding emphasis to show this is definitely or surely for something
Added هَا which refers back to ٱلْأَرْضِ (the earth) mentioned earlier in the ayah. We use 'it/her' because ٱلْأَرْضِ is feminine in Arabic.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he tested' to 'we test' by adding ن at start
Added لِ at the beginning to show purpose - this connects to the previous part of the ayah where Allah mentions making the earth's adornments as a test

steps.m: so that we may test them
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being tested (them)
Adding the vowel mark makes it readable
Added damma to show this word is the subject of أَحْسَنُ (better/best)

steps.m: which of them (asking who among them is أَحْسَنُ/best)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show we're asking about a specific group mentioned before (them)
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
This form is used to show something is the best of its kind. Here it's specifically talking about who has the best deeds.
Adding vowels makes it a complete past tense verb
Changed from a verb (did/performed) to a noun (deed/action) by adding tanween alif, making it the object being described as 'best'
This is an emphasis particle that makes statements stronger
The ا at the end means 'we'
The وَ at the start means 'and'
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the word to show who does the action instead of the action itself
Added ون to show it's multiple people doing the action
Added لَ at start to add emphasis - like saying 'surely' or 'certainly'
This is a relative pronoun used here to refer to everything that is on the earth. It connects to عَلَيْهَا (upon it) in the ayah.
This is a preposition showing location or position. Since it's a particle, it doesn't come from a root.
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns

steps.m: on/upon it/her (referring to مَا which is feminine)
steps.n: Added ها (meaning 'it/her') to show this is happening to something feminine mentioned earlier
The ي changes it from a verb (to rise) into a noun (high place)

steps.m: a barren ground (object of لَجٰعِلُونَ - those who will make)
steps.n: The ًا shows this word is receiving the action of لَجٰعِلُونَ (those who will make/render). In this ayah, it describes what the earth will be made into.
Added damma vowels to create a noun describing the quality of being barren
The ً (tanween) shows this is indefinite (like 'a' or 'an' in English) and matches the case of صَعِيدًا (ground/soil) that it describes
This is a special kind of 'or' that introduces a new possibility while questioning the previous one. Here it connects to the previous discussion, suggesting an alternative perspective.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense verb. This word follows أَمْ (or) in the ayah.

Adding تَ changes it from 'he' to 'you' while keeping the past tense. The sukun (ْ) is added before تَ to help pronunciation. This word is being used to ask if the person being addressed thought/considered something.
This is the basic form of the particle that connects statements

steps.m: that (emphasizing the fact that أَصْحٰبَ ٱلْكَهْفِ were amazing signs)
steps.n: Adding the shadda and fatha makes the word stronger, showing emphasis that what follows is definitely true
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to accompany'
Changes the verb into a noun meaning 'one who accompanies'
Forms the broken plural by adding أَ at the start and changing the middle of the word. This word is connected to 'the cave' (الكهف) in the ayah, meaning 'companions of the cave'.
Basic noun pattern فَعْل applied to root letters. The word is in genitive case because it comes after أَصْحٰب (companions/people) in a possession relationship, meaning 'people of the cave'.
Adds 'the' (ال) at the start and changes the ending to show it belongs to 'companions'
Forms a noun for something characterized by being written/inscribed
Adding definite article makes the word definite

steps.m: and the inscribed tablet/written record (connected to الكهف/the cave)
steps.n: Adds conjunction to connect this word to the previous word (الكهف/the cave)

steps.m: to be or exist in a state/condition

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak middle letter in the past tense

steps.m: they were (referring to أَصْحٰبَ ٱلْكَهْفِ - the companions of the cave)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action. This refers to the companions of the cave mentioned earlier in the verse.
Basic preposition showing that something comes from somewhere else. Here it shows that عَجَبًا (something amazing) comes from ءَايٰتِنَا (Our signs).
The root letters are connected with vowels. The first ي changes to ا due to weak letter rules.
Transforms the word into a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Changes from single 'sign' to multiple 'signs' by adding -aat ending. The middle alif stretches (alif maqsura) before adding the -aat.
Adds 'our' to show possession. This word follows مِنْ (from) in the ayah, showing these are the signs being referred to.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic noun form

steps.m: a wonder (object of كانوا - they were)
steps.n: The word 'wonder' got an -an ending to show it's being used as a predicate (what comes after 'were')

steps.m: when (referring to the time the youth took refuge in the cave)
steps.n: This is a time word that helps tell stories by pointing to specific events in the past. Here it introduces the main event of the youth seeking refuge in the cave.
This root carries the basic meaning of seeking protection or shelter

steps.m: he took shelter (subject: الفِتْيَةُ - the youths)
steps.n: Added fatha marks to show this happened in the past. The youths are the ones who took shelter.
Changed the final letter to ى which is common with words ending in weak letters
Connected the letters with vowels to form the basic noun for a single youth
Changed vowels and added ة to make it mean multiple youths instead of just one

steps.m: the young men (subject of أَوَى/took refuge)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ُ to show it's doing the action in the sentence. This word is the subject of أَوَى (took refuge).

steps.m: to/towards (connects فِتْيَةُ youth with كَهْفِ cave)
steps.n: Basic particle that shows direction or movement towards something. In this ayah, it shows the youth went towards/to the cave.
The root letters ك-ه-ف got vowel marks added (َ and ْ) to make the noun 'cave'
The word 'cave' got 'the' (ال) added to it and a kasra (ِ) at the end because it follows the word 'to' (إلى)
The middle letter و changes to ا when we talk about something that happened in the past. We also add َ (fatha) marks to show it's past tense.
Added وا at the end to show many people did the action, like changing 'he said' to 'they said'

steps.m: so they said (connected to إِذْ أَوَى - when they took refuge)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after they went to the cave. It's like saying 'then' or 'so' in English.

steps.m: Lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: The doubled letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares completely and perfectly

steps.m: our Lord (being called upon after فَقَالُوا)
steps.n: Added نا to show it means 'our Lord'. This is being used to call out to Allah directly after the youths said (فَقَالُوا).
The extra alif shows this is being used to call out to Allah with emphasis
Changed to a command form telling someone to give. This is used when speaking directly to someone.

steps.m: give us! (command to give to us, spoken by the youth to their Lord)
steps.n: Added نَا which means 'us' to show who should receive the giving. This is part of the youth's prayer to Allah in the cave.
Basic particle that shows where something comes from or its source. In this ayah, it shows the mercy comes from Allah.
Links two words together to show that mercy comes directly from Allah's presence. The word مِن helps connect رَحْمَةً (mercy) to لَّدُنكَ (Your presence).
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic word
The ل gets a stronger sound (shadda) because it combines with the word 'from' before it
Added second person masculine singular possessive pronoun to show ownership
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and make it a noun
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of mercy
Added tanween to show this is the object being requested in 'grant us mercy'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed to command form by adding sukun at end and kasra in middle
Added conjunction prefix to connect to previous phrase
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced

steps.m: for us (connected to هَيِّئْ - make easy)
steps.n: Added نَا which means 'us' to show who the action is for. This word connects to هَيِّئْ (make easy) in the ayah, showing what should be made easy for them.

This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere else. In this ayah, it connects 'mercy' (رَحْمَةً) with 'Your presence' (لَّدُنكَ), showing they're asking for mercy that comes from Allah's presence.

steps.m: to command, order, or deal with matters

steps.n: This pattern turns the verb into a noun meaning the action itself or result of the action. The word is in genitive case because it is possessed by نا (our), making it mean 'our matter/affair'.
نَا (naa) at the end means 'our', showing who the matter/affair belongs to
Adding vowel marks changes this into a complete past tense verb
Changed from a past tense verb (he was guided) to a noun with tanween to show it's the thing being asked for
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added فَ to show this happened right after something else mentioned before
Changed 'he' to 'We' by adding نَا. In the Quran, when Allah uses 'We', it shows His majesty and power, not multiple gods.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something is on top of, upon, or over something else
Added the small alif (ٰ) which shows this is a long 'aa' sound at the end
Added the small vertical line (ٓ) which helps connect this word's meaning to the word after it (their ears). In this ayah, it's describing something being done to their ears.
The damma marks make this a noun meaning a body part
Changed the word to show multiple ears using a special plural pattern for body parts that come in pairs

steps.m: their ears (referring to the people in the cave)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show these ears belong to the group of young men mentioned in the story of the cave
Basic preposition showing location or position inside something
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. In this ayah, it shows the young men were physically inside the cave.
Connected the letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a noun
ٱلْ makes it specific, and the ِ shows it's affected by a word before it
The weak letter و becomes ة to make the noun 'year'. This is a common pattern for words about time.
Changed to plural by adding ين and changing vowels to kasra (ِ ). The ين ending shows it's plural and being acted upon in the sentence.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create a noun meaning 'number' or 'count'
Added tanween to show this describes how many years they stayed. It's connected to the word سِنِينَ (years) in the ayah.
This word connects two events in sequence, showing that one happened after the other. In this ayah, it connects to the next phrase 'We raised them', showing this happened after the previous event.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb. In this ayah, it refers to Allah awakening the youth from their long sleep.
Added 'نا' to show 'we' as the doer. This is the royal 'We' referring to Allah. The word is followed by لِنَعْلَمَ (so that We may know).

steps.m: we sent forth/awakened them (the youth of the cave)
steps.n: Added 'هم' to show who received the action - referring to the young men of the cave (أَصْحَٰبَ ٱلْكَهْفِ) mentioned earlier
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from 'he knew' to 'we know' by adding ن at the start

steps.m: so that we may know (purpose for بَعَثْنٰهُمْ - we raised them)
steps.n: The لِ shows this is the purpose or reason for the action in بَعَثْنٰهُمْ (we raised them). It explains why they were raised.
Root letters أ-ي-ي became أَيّ with a shadda, making it into a question word meaning 'which'. It's asking about choosing between two groups (ٱلْحِزْبَيْنِ) mentioned in the ayah.

steps.m: which one (of ٱلْحِزْبَيْنِ - the two groups)
steps.n: Added damma to show this is the subject doing the counting (أَحْصَىٰ - counted) in the ayah. The ي changed to ى because it's at the end of the word.

steps.m: to form a group or party

steps.n: Added vowel marks to create the basic noun meaning a group or party
Added dual ending يْنِ to show exactly two groups. The ِ ending shows it's being acted upon by the word أَىُّ (which) in the sentence.

steps.m: the two groups (object of أَىُّ - which)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to show these are specific groups mentioned before

steps.m: to count or keep track of something precisely

steps.n: Added vowels (a) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he counted'

steps.m: he kept most precise count (of لَبِثُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added أ at the beginning to show doing something more completely or thoroughly. This word relates to how long they لَبِثُوٓا۟ (remained/stayed).

steps.m: he kept most precise count (of لَبِثُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added the elongated alif (ى with small alif above) at the end to show the proper pronunciation
This is a particle showing purpose or reason
Added ما which means 'what' or 'that which' to create a relative clause. This connects to لَبِثُوٓا۟ (they stayed) in the ayah.

steps.m: to stay/remain for a period of time

steps.n: Adding vowels creates the basic past tense verb meaning 'to stay'

steps.m: they remained/stayed (referring to أَصْحَٰبَ ٱلْكَهْفِ - the People of the Cave)
steps.n: Added plural masculine ending to show multiple people (the People of the Cave) did this action
Connected the root letters and added fatha marks to make it a noun
Added tanween fatha to show this is the object being counted in the phrase 'counted a period'
Basic personal pronoun meaning 'we' - it does not come from a root
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: fatha on ن, sukun on ح, and damma on final ن
Added shadda on first letter to emphasize the pronoun. This emphasis shows strong assertion, like saying 'We ourselves' or 'Indeed, we'

steps.m: to tell a story or follow tracks

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changes from past tense 'he told' to present tense 'we tell'
This is a preposition that comes from the idea of being above or over something. The و at the end changes to ى when used as a preposition.
The ى changes to يْ before adding a pronoun suffix. This is just a spelling change to make it easier to connect the letters.

steps.m: upon you (object of نَقُصُّ - we narrate)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is happening to 'you'. This word is receiving the action of نَقُصُّ (we narrate) in this ayah.
Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb

steps.m: their news (object of نَقُصُّ - we narrate)
steps.n: Added هُم to show it's their news. This word is what's being narrated in the phrase 'We narrate to you their news'
Connected the root letters with fatha and added shadda to show completeness
Changed from a verb to a noun meaning the concept itself
Added the preposition بِ to show how something is done
Added ال (the) to make it 'the truth' instead of just 'truth'. The word has kasra (i sound) at the end because it comes after بِ (with). Connected to نَقُصُّ (We narrate).
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain. The shadda (ّ) makes it stronger.

steps.m: indeed they (referring to فِتْيَةٌ - the youth)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which means 'they/them' to show who is being talked about. This connects back to the youth (فِتْيَةٌ) mentioned later in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic noun for a young person
Changed vowels and added ة to make it mean multiple youth instead of just one

steps.m: a group of youths (subject of ءَامَنُوا)
steps.n: Adds tanween dammah to show this is the subject doing the action of believing (ءَامَنُوا) in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed first letter to hamza and added alif, making the meaning stronger and more specific to faith
Added وا at the end to show a group of people did this action. This word is part of the story being told (نَقُصُّ means 'We narrate')

steps.m: to care for, nurture, or develop something to completion

steps.n: The doubled ب with fatha on ر shows this is someone who completely fulfills the action of nurturing and sustaining
Added بِ to show this is about doing something with or by means of the lord

steps.m: by their lord (connected to ءَامَنُوا۟ - they believed)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show this is their lord. This word explains what they believed in from the word ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed) in the ayah.
Basic command form. The middle letter ي is dropped because it's a weak letter in command form.
Added 'نا' to show 'we' did this action, changing it from a command to a past tense verb we did
Added conjunction prefix وَ to connect to previous phrase

steps.m: and we increased them (referring to their guidance mentioned in هُدًى)
steps.n: Added هُمْ meaning 'them'. The alif in نَا changes to ٰ when connected to هُمْ. This word connects to هُدًى (guidance) in the next part of the verse.
Changed from a verb meaning 'to guide' into a noun meaning 'guidance' by adding the vowel marks ُ and ْ
The ي changes to ى at the end to show it's complete or perfect guidance (not just regular guidance)
Adding tanween (ً) shows this guidance is complete and can spread to others
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding vowels (fatha) and a sukun at the end
The suffix نا shows 'we' did the action. In this ayah, Allah is strengthening the hearts of the youth of the cave.
Adding و at the start shows this action happened along with other things Allah did to help the youth of the cave
This is the basic form of the word that carries the meaning of being high or elevated
Adding ى turns this word from meaning 'high' into meaning 'upon' or 'on top of'. Here it shows something being placed upon their hearts.
The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation guide to show the long 'aa' sound at the end
Connected the root letters with basic voweling to form singular noun
Changes vowel pattern and adds و to make plural form
Adds kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Adds هِمْ to show the hearts belong to them

steps.m: when (referring to the time they stood up)
steps.n: This is a time particle that connects to the verb قَامُوا۟ (they stood up) in this ayah. It shows when something happened in the past.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action
Added past tense vowels. The middle و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Added وا to show multiple people did the action
Added فَ to show this happened after something else. In this ayah, it shows they said this after they stood up (قَامُوا)

steps.m: one who cares for and develops (as the subject)
steps.n: The root letters were combined and vowels added to make 'Lord/Master' - someone who cares for others

steps.m: our Caretaker and Developer (speaking to others)
steps.n: Added نَا to show we're talking about our Lord. This word is being used by the youth in the cave when speaking to others about their Lord.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, raise up

steps.n: Adding fatha and doubling the middle letter turns the action into the one who does it intensively
Adding damma shows this is the subject (doer) in the sentence. Here it's part of what they said after قَالُوا۟ (they said)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb meaning 'to rise' into a noun meaning 'sky' by adding ء at the end
Added feminine plural ending وات to show multiple heavens
Added ال with shadda on س to make it definite
Changed to 'of the heavens' by adding kasra (-i) sound at the end to show it belongs to رَبُّ (Lord)
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters
Adding conjunction prefix و to connect with previous phrase
Added kasra at the end to show connection to previous word السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens). The ٱ is a spelling variant of اَ.
A particle that negates future actions

steps.m: will never (negates نَّدْعُوَا۟ - we will call/invoke)
steps.n: Changes 'not' into 'never' in the future - makes نَّدْعُوَا۟ (we will call) into 'we will never call'. The fatha mark shows this strong meaning.

steps.m: to call upon or pray to someone/something

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added نَ at start to show 'we' are doing the action, and changed vowels for present tense
Added fatha at end to show this is part of a longer phrase

steps.m: that we call upon (subject of قَالُوا۟)
steps.n: Added alif and small circle as writing variants that don't change meaning. This word is what they said after قَالُوا۟ (they said).

Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects with the verb نَّدْعُوَا۟ (we will call/invoke) to show what they won't call upon - anything from besides Him.
Root letters د-و-ن became the word دُون by adding a damma mark, changing it from 'being low' to mean 'below/besides'
Added kasra to connect it to the next word
Added هِ with lengthening marks to show it means 'Him' (referring to Allah)

steps.m: to worship or take as a deity

steps.n: Changed root letters to make the word mean 'something that is worshipped' (like turning 'worship' into 'god')
Added tanween fatha to show this is the object being called upon in the phrase 'we will not call upon any god besides Him'
Added vowel marks to show this is an emphasis word that confirms something happened
Added لَ at the beginning to make the meaning stronger, like adding 'surely' or 'definitely'
Added shadda on ل to make the emphasis even stronger, like saying 'most certainly' instead of just 'certainly'
Added an alif (ا) between the first two root letters and vowels to make it mean 'he said' in the past
Changed vowels and removed the weak letter ا to make it shorter for the command form
Added the long alif (آ) at the end for emphasis and proper pronunciation
Basic form of the word that means 'when' in time expressions

steps.m: in that case, then (shows result of what was said before)
steps.n: Adding tanween alif (ًا) changes this from a simple time word to showing that something happens as a result of what came before
Added fathas to connect the letters together and make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (action) to a noun (thing or concept)

steps.m: an outrageous statement (object of قُلْنَآ - we said)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being said. This word is what 'we said' in the phrase 'we would have said an outrageous thing'
This is the attention-getting particle that starts many demonstrative pronouns. The alif is written as a small mark above the ه.
The small vertical line (ٓ) shows that we should stretch the sound a bit for emphasis
The short attention-getter هٰٓ (ha) became the full word for 'these ones' by adding ؤُلَآءِ. Like pointing your finger and saying 'these people right here!'

steps.m: to stand firmly or rise up

steps.n: Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Added damma to show this word is doing the action in the sentence
Added 'our' to show possession. This word is the subject of اتَّخَذُوا (they took/adopted) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the word to show that someone is taking something specifically for themselves

steps.m: they took for themselves (subject: قَوْمُنَا - our people)
steps.n: Added وا to show that 'our people' (multiple people) are doing the action
This is a preposition that connects with the word دُونِ after it to show something coming from a lower position or status. In this ayah, it's talking about taking gods from below/besides Allah.

steps.m: to be beneath or lower than something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'beneath/lower'
Added kasra to show this word is affected by the preposition مِن that comes before it
Added هِ ('his/its') with a long vowel to show 'besides Him/it' - often comes after مِن
Changes from verb 'to worship' into noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed from 'god/deity' to 'one who possesses godly qualities' - like changing from 'king' to 'kingly'
Added feminine ending to specify a female deity
Added a small mark (tanween) at the end to show these gods are what is being taken/chosen in the sentence
Combined two particles: لَوْ (if) and لَا (not) to create a new meaning. This combination is used to express wishes or gentle suggestions.

steps.m: if not, why don't/why not (with emphasis)
steps.n: Added emphasis by doubling the first letter with a shadda. This makes the suggestion or wish stronger.
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now, with hamza getting sukun and kasra under ت
Added ون and fatha at the end to show many people are doing it, changing the kasra to damma before ون
This is a combination of the preposition 'upon' (عَلَى) with the pronoun 'them' (هِم)

steps.m: to have power or authority over something

steps.n: Changes from basic root to noun meaning authority/power

steps.m: a clear authority/proof (being used with another word)
steps.n: Added tanween (two kasras) to show this is indefinite (like 'a/an' in English) and is being used with another word

steps.m: with any clear authority/proof (connected to يَأْتُونَ - they bring)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this is describing how they should bring their proof. This connects to the verb يَأْتُونَ (they bring) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic word
Added shadda (doubling) on the middle letter and changed its vowel to kasra to show intensity
Added tanween kasratayn at the end to show this is describing something in the sentence

steps.m: who (asking about the subject of أَظْلَمُ)
steps.n: Basic interrogative word that asks about people. Here it's asking who is more unjust.

steps.m: so who (asking about the subject of أَظْلَمُ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this question follows from the previous statement about people taking other gods
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: more/most unfair (compared to others mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to show comparison, added ُ at the end to show this is the subject, and added ْ (sukun) on ظ to help pronunciation
Combined the two words مِن (from/than) and مَن (who) together
The ن of مِن combines with the م of مَن to make a doubled م (shown by the shadda mark ّ)
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to connect this word grammatically with the verb افْتَرَىٰ (he invented/fabricated) that follows it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he invented falsely (subject of أَظْلَمُ - more unjust)
steps.n: Added ٱ at start and ت after first letter to show doing something to oneself, changing meaning from simple 'cutting' to 'making up/inventing' something false
Added ى at the end to show the word ends with a long 'aa' sound
This is a preposition showing opposition or hostility in this context. It connects with the word كَذِبًا (a lie) to show that the lie is against Allah.
Added ال to specify The One God, and the first letter merged with the ل, creating the unique word Allah
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word عَلَى (upon/against). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form.
Added vowels to make it mean 'he lied' in the past tense
Adds ًا (an) at the end to make it a direct object (the thing that was invented) instead of the verb 'to lie'
This is a time particle that refers to past events
Changed the sukun to kasra because it's connected to what comes after it. This word introduces the time when 'you separated from them' (اعْتَزَلْتُمُوهُمْ).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it

steps.m: to separate or withdraw from something

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added اِعْتَ at the beginning to show doing the action by oneself - like choosing to separate yourself from others
Added تُمْ to show that multiple people (you all) did the action
Added و as a connecting letter and هُمْ to show who you withdrew from
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' or 'that which'. In this ayah, it refers to the things that the people worship.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This connects to يَعْبُدُونَ (they worship) in the next part of the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show this happens in present/future
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
This is a basic exception word that shows something is left out. It's made up of إِ (which starts it) and لّ (which is doubled with shadda).
Adding ا at the end makes it connect better with the next word. Here it connects to اللَّهَ (Allah) to show He is the exception to what they left behind.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being worshipped in يَعْبُدُونَ)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a special mark (shadda) on the ل to make it stronger, and a small mark at the end (fatha) to show it's receiving the action of worship
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding و at the end - a common way to make plural in Arabic
Adds conjunction prefix to indicate sequence/result
This is a preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to الْكَهْفِ (the cave) to show where they should go.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a proper noun
The kasra at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to something
Adding ال makes the cave specific - we're talking about a particular cave
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes to present tense and shows connection to 'mercy' mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: he will spread out (mercy from رَّحْمَتِهِۦ)
steps.n: The sukun (stop mark) at the end shows this is connected to 'if' - when something happens, then He will spread out mercy
Added fatha to show this is a preposition showing who receives or benefits from something

steps.m: for you all (receivers of يَنشُرْ - spreads out)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this refers to multiple people being spoken to. This word shows who receives the action of Allah spreading out His mercy in the ayah.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, and help develop/grow

steps.n: Adding vowel marks creates the basic past tense verb form
The shadda makes the meaning more complete and permanent, showing someone who fully embodies the quality of nurturing
Adding damma to indicate nominative case

steps.m: your (all of you) lord/master (who will spread mercy for you)
steps.n: Adding كُم shows this lord belongs to or is connected to multiple people. In this ayah, this lord will spread mercy for them, as mentioned in the words يَنشُرْ لَكُمْ (spread for you) that come right before.
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or its source

steps.m: from (showing source of رَّحْمَتِهِۦ - His mercy)
steps.n: Added shadda on م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel sound. This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to show kindness and care for someone

steps.n: Adding vowels to create the basic noun meaning 'mercy'

steps.m: a specific act of kindness and care
steps.n: Adding ة makes it into a specific instance of mercy
Added 'ت' and kasra at the end to show this mercy belongs to someone

steps.m: His kindness and care (referring to Allah)
steps.n: Added emphasis mark (shadda) and 'His' (هِ) at the end to show this is Allah's mercy
Added ۦ after هِ to show the elongated pronunciation of 'his'
Added shadda to show intensive preparation
Added يُ at start to make it 'he' in present tense, middle letter doubled with ّ stays to show strong action
Added 'and' (و) at start to connect with previous phrase, and added a stop mark (sukun) at end because it follows a command
Starting with the basic preposition that shows purpose or direction
Added the plural 'you' pronoun كُم to show who the action is for. This word is receiving the action of يَنشُرْ (spread out/extend) in this ayah.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'
The ن in مِنْ changes to match the ر sound that follows it, making it easier to say together with رَّحْمَتِهِۦ
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create the basic noun form
Added kasra at the end to show possession relationship
Added كُم at the end to show it belongs to multiple people being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Added مِ at start and changed vowels to make a noun meaning the place or result of being gentle - in this case, a convenience or facility
Added marks to show this word is receiving the action - the shadda joins it to the previous word and the tanween shows it's the object
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The ي changes to ى at the end because it's a weak letter at the end of a word.
Added ت at start to show 'you' are doing the action. The أ is dropped because it's between two vowels.

steps.m: and you see (the sun إِذَا طَلَعَت)
steps.n: Added و at start to connect this to the previous phrase. This word is followed by 'the sun' (ٱلشَّمْسَ) which is what is being seen.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and form the basic noun
Adding ال makes it specific and doubles the ش
The fatHa at the end shows this is the object being seen in the phrase 'you see the sun'
This is a time word that introduces when something happens. In this ayah, it introduces when the sun rises (طَلَعَت). It's like saying 'at the time when' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word describes what the sun (الشَّمْسَ) did.

steps.m: it rose/appeared (referring to الشَّمْسَ - the sun)
steps.n: Added ت at the end to show this is about the sun (which is feminine in Arabic)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb

steps.m: it/she turns aside (subject of الشمس - the sun)
steps.n: Added ت at beginning to show present tense and feminine subject (the sun), changed last vowel to ُ to show present tense
Added emphasis by doubling the ت with shadda, and lengthened the first vowel with alif
The alif is written with a vertical alif (ٰ) which is a common Quranic orthographic variation
This is a preposition that shows separation or distance from something. In this ayah, it shows the sun moving away from their cave.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added the genitive case ending ِ to show this noun is possessed by something
Added هِمْ at the end to show the cave belongs to them
This is a noun used to indicate direction or side. It doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: towards the direction of (الْيَمِينِ - the right)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is being used to point to a direction. In this ayah, it's used twice - once for 'towards the right' and once for 'towards the left'.

steps.m: to be blessed, fortunate, or on the right side

steps.n: Added 'the' at beginning and kasra ending because it follows ذَاتَ (towards/having) in the ayah
Basic time word that introduces what happens at a specific moment. Here it's talking about when the sun sets.
Added وَ (and) to connect this time phrase to what came before it. This links the sun's setting to its previous rising mentioned earlier in the verse.
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت to show the subject is feminine (الشمس/the sun) which is feminine in Arabic

steps.m: to cut across or pass by

steps.n: Added vowels (a) to make it past tense 'he passed by'

steps.m: it passes by (referring to الشَّمْسَ - the sun)
steps.n: Changed to present tense with feminine subject (referring to the sun)

steps.m: it passes by (referring to الشَّمْسَ - the sun)
steps.n: Added shadda on ت due to assimilation with previous word

steps.m: it passes by them (the sun passing by the sleepers)
steps.n: Added pronoun suffix meaning 'them' referring to the sleepers in the cave
This is a special word used to show that something (feminine) has or owns something else. It's like saying 'having' or 'with' in English.
Added fatha at the end to show direction. In this ayah, it's describing which way the sun moves - towards the right side of their cave.
The root meaning of gathering/containing transforms into the word for 'left' - this is a common pattern in Arabic where abstract roots become concrete nouns
The root letters take the فِعَال pattern to form a concrete noun meaning 'left'
Adding ال makes it specific and causes the ش to get a shadda
Adding kasra at the end because it follows ذَاتَ (meaning 'towards' or 'in the direction of')

steps.m: they/them (referring to the youth in the cave)
steps.n: This is a basic pronoun meaning 'they' (for a group of males or mixed group). In this ayah, it refers back to the youth (أَصْحَابَ الْكَهْفِ) who were in the cave.

steps.m: and they (while they were in the cave)
steps.n: The و connects this phrase to the previous description of how the sun would pass by their cave. It shows these things were happening while they were in a specific state.

steps.m: in/within (location in فَجْوَةٍ - a spacious recess)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows the cave dwellers were located inside a spacious recess of the cave.

steps.m: in/within (location in فَجْوَةٍ - a spacious recess)
steps.n: The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters together. The fatha and sukun pattern creates a basic noun from the root concept.
Added ة at the end to make it a specific instance of a space or gap
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is indefinite (a/an) and follows the preposition في (in)
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from'
The ن is assimilated into the م (shown by shadda), which is a pronunciation change due to the following word

steps.m: from him/it (referring to the cave mentioned in كَهْفِهِمْ)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show this means 'from it', referring back to the cave mentioned earlier in the verse
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' for something close by
The alif is replaced with a long alif (ٰ) to show distance, changing 'this' to 'that'
Added ل with kasra (لِ) which helps connect to the next part
Added كَ at the end which emphasizes the distance, like saying 'over there'
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the word فَجْوَةٍ (spacious area) with هُ (it), showing the spacious area is from/of the cave.

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: Adding ة changes it from a verb root into a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Makes the word plural using the feminine plural ending ات
The word gets a kasra (small diagonal line) at the end and a longer alif to show it belongs to Allah
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of مِنْ (from). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
This is a special word that introduces a conditional clause, meaning 'whoever' or 'anyone who'. In this ayah, it's connected to the verb يَهْدِ (guides), showing who might receive guidance.

steps.m: to guide to the right path

steps.n: Added vowels to make it mean 'he guided' in the past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Final ي is dropped because this is in a conditional statement starting with مَن (whoever)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
The word for 'god' (إِلَه) became the name 'Allah' by adding AL- in a special way, doubling the L sound, and adding a U sound at the end to show it's doing the action
This is a basic pronoun that points to someone or something. Here it refers to the person who is guided (ٱلْمُهْتَدِ).
The فَ at the start shows this follows from what was said before - whoever Allah guides. It connects the two ideas with 'so'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Added reflexive pattern showing the person is both doing and receiving the guidance
Changed from a past tense verb (he was guided) to a noun meaning 'a guided person'
Added definite article to specify this particular guided one
This is a special word that means 'whoever' when used with a verb that comes after it. Here it connects to the verb يُضْلِلْ (makes astray) to mean 'whoever He makes astray'

steps.m: and whoever (subject of يُضْلِلْ - makes astray)
steps.n: The وَ at the start connects this to the previous statement about guidance, contrasting 'whoever Allah guides' with 'and whoever He makes astray'
Added hamza at start to show making someone else do the action

steps.m: he causes to go astray (in a conditional statement)
steps.n: Changed to present tense with yu- prefix. The sukun at the end shows this is in a conditional statement (after مَن 'whoever')
This is a compound particle made of فَ (then/so) and لَن (never/not). It's used to strongly negate future actions.
This is the basic meaning of finding or discovering something
The weak letter و is dropped because it comes at the start of a present tense verb
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' (masculine singular) are doing the action
Added fatha at the end to show this is in the subjunctive mood (used after certain words)
Starting with the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose

steps.m: for him (connected to وَلِيًّا - a protecting friend)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُ meaning 'him' to show who the action is for. This word connects to وَلِيًّا (protecting friend) in the next part of the ayah, showing who would not have a protecting friend.

steps.m: to protect, guard, be close to
steps.n: The doubled ي (shadda) intensifies the meaning to indicate someone who fully protects or guards
Changed the root letters into a noun pattern that means 'someone who does the action' (like protector)
The doubled ي (shadda) makes the meaning stronger to show someone who is very close in protection

steps.m: a close protector/guardian (object of تَجِدَ - you will find)
steps.n: Added tanween and alif to show this word is receiving the action of 'finding' in the phrase 'you will not find for him a close protector'

steps.m: to guide correctly, to show the right way

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show who does the action
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more intense

steps.m: a guide (object of تَجِدَ - you will find)
steps.n: Added tanween fatHa to show this is the object being found in the phrase 'you will not find for him a guide'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning and changed vowels to show present tense 'you' form
Added هُمْ at the end to show the object (them) of the thinking

steps.m: and you think/consider them (while they are أَيْقَاظًا/awake)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this to the next word أَيْقَاظًا (awake) in the ayah
Root letters got vowels (َ ِ) to make a word meaning 'being awake'
Changed to plural form by adding أَ at start and ا after second root letter

steps.m: awake ones (as the object of تَحْسَبُهُمْ - you think them)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is the object of تَحْسَبُ (you think/consider), telling us what 'you think they are'
This is a pronoun meaning 'they' for a group of males. In this ayah, it refers to the people of the cave who appear awake but are actually sleeping.
و means 'and', connecting this to أَيْقَاظًا (awake) from the previous part, showing contrast with رُقُودٌ (asleep) that follows
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he slept' verb to plural noun meaning 'sleepers' by using the fu'ūl pattern
Added two dots (tanween) to show this is the main doer in the sentence - it refers to the sleeping youth that people thought were awake

steps.m: to turn over/flip back and forth

steps.n: The shadda (ّ) shows this action is done back and forth multiple times
نُ at start shows 'we' are doing the action
و connects this to the previous action

steps.m: and we turn them over repeatedly (referring to the sleeping youth)
steps.n: Adding هُمْ shows this action is done to them (the sleeping youth mentioned in the verse)
This is a special word that shows direction or side of something. It's often used with other words to say which way something is facing.

steps.m: towards the direction (of الْيَمِينِ - the right)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence. Here it's used with 'the right' (الْيَمِينِ) to mean 'towards the right direction'.
Changes root into a concrete noun meaning 'right side'
Added ال to show we're talking about a specific right side

steps.m: of the right side (connected to ذَاتَ meaning 'towards')
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to ذَاتَ which means 'towards' - together they mean 'towards the right side'
This is a special word that shows possession or direction. Here it's used to show direction.
Adding the fatha at the end shows this word is being used to point in a direction. Here it's specifically showing the direction of ٱلْيَمِينِ (the right)
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this to the previous phrase, showing this is another direction they were turned

steps.m: to gather, contain, or be on the left side

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowel pattern and added alif to create a noun for direction
Added kasra at the end to show it's being used as object of preposition
Added the definite article ال (written as ٱل) to make it specific
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to the word before it (ذَاتَ)
Basic noun formation from root letters. This word is the subject of بٰسِطٌ (stretching) in this ayah, describing the dog stretching its forelegs.
The هُم suffix means 'their'
The وَ means 'and'
This pattern shows who/what is doing the action. Here it describes their dog (كَلْبُهُم) stretching out its limbs.
The tanween (ٌ) at the end shows this word is describing their dog - like saying 'a dog spreading out'
Adds vowels to make a noun meaning the part of the arm from elbow to wrist
Shows there are exactly two forearms

steps.m: his two forearms (belonging to the dog)
steps.n: Shows the forearms belong to the dog mentioned in the verse

steps.m: to close or block an entrance

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Changed to describe something that is closed/blocked, specifically referring to a threshold or entrance
Added ال (the) to make it 'the threshold' instead of just any threshold, and added kasra (ِ) at the end because it comes after a preposition
Added بِ to show location/position

This is a helper word that introduces an imaginary situation that didn't actually happen. In this ayah, it connects to لَوَلَّيْتَ (you would have turned away) to say 'if you had seen them, you would have turned away'
Added kasra at the end because it's followed by a word starting with a connecting hamza (ٱطَّلَعْتَ)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
The ت merges with ط creating a shadda, showing he did the action for himself
Added تَ at the end to change from 'he' to 'you'
Changed initial اِ to ٱ as a common spelling variation
Starting with the basic preposition على with proper vowel marks
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns
Added هِمْ at the end to show it's happening to them
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Intensifies the meaning of turning away
Changes 'he' to 'you' (for a male) in past tense by adding تَ at the end

steps.m: you would have certainly turned away
steps.n: Adds 'la' at start to emphasize that this definitely would have happened
Added sukun to show where the word pauses before connecting to the next part

steps.m: from them (the sleeping people in the cave)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُم (them) to show who is being referred to. This connects to the previous mentions of the sleeping people in the cave.
Changed the verb into a noun by adding ِ on first letter and ا after second letter. Like changing 'run' to 'running'.

steps.m: in running away (from them) (describes how وَلَّيْتَ/you would turn away happens)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes how the action of turning away happens - like saying 'you would turn away in flight'
Changes from doing the filling to receiving the filling
The تَ shows 'you' (masculine) received this action in the past

steps.m: you would have been filled completely
steps.n: The لَ shows this is the result of a condition (if this happened, then you would have been filled)

steps.m: and you would have been filled completely (result of لَوِ ٱطَّلَعْتَ - if you had looked)
steps.n: Adds و to connect with previous phrase in the conditional statement
This is a basic preposition showing movement away from something or someone. In this ayah, it shows moving away from the sleeping people in the cave.

steps.m: from them (refers to أَيْقَاظًا/the sleeping people)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being moved away from - in this case, the sleeping people in the cave that looked awake (أَيْقَاظًا). The word connects to فِرَارًا (fleeing) to show what kind of movement away is happening.
Added the vowels ُ (u) and ْ (silence) to turn the action into a noun meaning the feeling itself

steps.m: with terror (object of مُلِئْتَ - you would be filled)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is the object being filled with. Like saying 'filled WITH terror'
Starting with the demonstrative particle ذا (this). The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this form.
Added ل with kasra to extend the meaning to 'that'
Added ك at the end to make it mean 'that' (pointing to something far away)
Added ك ('like') at the start to mean 'like that' or 'similarly' - just as we say 'like this' in English
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added هُمْ at the end to show who received the action (them). The alif in نا becomes ٰ when connected to هُمْ.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he asked' (past) to 'he asks' (present) by adding يَ at start
Adding تَ and ا shows people asking back and forth to each other
Adding وا at the end makes it a group of men asking each other

steps.m: so that they ask one another
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose ('so that/in order to') and lengthened the alif for emphasis

steps.m: to be separate/distinct from one another
between (used to show they are speaking with each other)
steps.n: Changed from a verb about separating into a word meaning 'between' that shows people interacting

steps.m: between them (referring to the people speaking with each other)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is involved in the conversation
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form before the weak letter change

steps.m: he said (speaker in this ayah is one of the companions of the cave)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it comes between two fathas. This is a common change in hollow verbs (verbs with و in the middle)

steps.m: one who says (speaker in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ا after ق and changed و to ئ to show who does the action. This word refers to one of the people speaking in the cave.
Added ٌ (tanween) at the end to show this is any speaker, not a specific one. Also wrote the long alif as آ which is just a writing style.
This is a basic word that shows something coming from somewhere or someone. Since it's a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.
The letter م gets doubled (shown by ّ) because it comes after the word فَلْيَأْتِكُم in the ayah. This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: from them (referring to the food/provisions mentioned in فَلْيَأْتِكُم بِرِزْقٍ)
steps.n: Adds هُمْ at the end to show who/what is being referred to. Here it refers back to the food/provisions (رِزْق) mentioned earlier in the ayah.

steps.m: how much/many (asking about لَبِثْتُمْ - how long you remained)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask about quantity or duration. In this ayah, it's asking about how long the people of the cave slept.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb showing someone stayed somewhere
Added ت with sukun to show 'you' did the action instead of 'he'
Changed تَ to تُمْ to make the verb plural, addressing multiple people
Added fatha (َ) after each root letter to show this happened in the past. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels - this is a common change in words with و in the middle.

steps.m: they said (responding to كَمْ لَبِثْتُمْ - how long did you remain)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people are speaking. This matches the context where the sleepers are responding to the question about how long they remained in the cave.
Added vowels to make past tense verb showing one person did the action
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. Put ْ (sukun) on ث because it comes before نَا.
Added vowel marks (fatHa and sukoon) to the root letters to form the common noun meaning 'day'

steps.m: a day (object of لَبِثْنَا - we remained)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is receiving the action of the verb لَبِثْنَا (we remained). It answers the question 'how long did they remain?'
This is a particle that gives choice between options. In this ayah, it connects 'a day' with 'part of a day' as the people are unsure how long they slept.
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make it into a noun meaning 'a part'
Added a fatha at the end to show this word is connected to يَوْمٍ (day) in the phrase 'part of a day'
Connected the letters with fatha-sukoon pattern to make the noun for a single day

steps.m: of a day (acted on by stayed)
steps.n: Added two dots under the م to show this word means 'of a day' - it's being acted upon by the word 'stayed'
Adding the basic vowel pattern for past tense verbs

steps.m: they said (responding to كَمْ لَبِثْتُمْ - how long did you remain)
steps.n: Changed middle letter و to ا and added وا at the end to show many people (they) did the action. This is their response to being asked how long they stayed.

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop
one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this person does a lot of caring and helping

steps.m: the one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: Adding the ُ shows this is the main subject being talked about

steps.m: your Lord (the one who cares for and helps you all grow)
steps.n: The كُمْ at the end shows this Lord belongs to or is connected to multiple people being spoken to
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: I know better (than you about how long you stayed)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning and ُ at the end to show comparison. In this ayah, Allah is saying He knows better than the people of the cave about how long they stayed there.
Basic preposition indicating accompaniment or means

steps.m: with what (about what you have stayed)
steps.n: Added ما which is a relative pronoun meaning 'what'. In this context, it's asking about the duration of their stay.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to stay'
Changed the final vowel to a sukun to prepare for adding endings
Added تُمْ at the end to show that multiple people are being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes from 'he sent' to 'send!' - making it a command to one person
Adds plural masculine suffix to make command apply to group
Added فَ at beginning to show this action follows from what came before. The alif changes to hamza-alif (ٱ) after فَ.
Connected the letters with fatha marks to create a noun meaning a single person or thing

steps.m: one of you (plural) (object of فَٱبْعَثُوٓا۟ - send)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show this refers to 'you all'. This word is the object of the verb فَٱبْعَثُوٓا۟ (send) in this ayah, meaning 'send one of you'

steps.m: to produce silver coins/currency, to mint money

steps.n: Changes from root meaning into specific noun for currency
Added كُمْ to show the silver coins belong to multiple people being addressed

steps.m: with your silver coins (object of فَٱبْعَثُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added preposition ب to show how the silver coins will be used. This word is the object of the command فَٱبْعَثُوٓا۟ (send forth)
Basic demonstrative word meaning 'this'
Added ذا to make it specifically point to something nearby
Changed ending to هِ to make it feminine, showing it points to a feminine word
Added pronunciation helpers (ۦٓ) that don't change the meaning
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to الْمَدِينَةِ (the city) to show where they should go.
Added basic vowel pattern to connect the root letters
Adding feminine marker ة changes the word from describing a state to naming a place

steps.m: to the city (connected to إِلَى meaning 'to')
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite (the city) and ِ because it comes after إِلَى (to)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changes past tense to present/future tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to ُ
Adds فَ to show this action follows from something before it
Adds ل after فَ to show this is a command/suggestion
Adds sukun at the end to show it's a command form
