The root letters أ-ي-ي combine with a fatha and shadda to make the word 'which' that we use to ask questions
Adding the damma (ُ) at the end shows this word is in the nominative case, like when it's the subject of a sentence
Adding ها changes this from 'which' to a way of calling out to someone

steps.m: to be pure, to grow in goodness
steps.n: Basic root meaning showing the concept of becoming pure or growing in goodness
Added fatha vowels between the letters. The و at the end turned into ا because it's at the end of the word - this is a common pattern in Arabic verbs.
Added أَ at the beginning to show comparison. The middle letter gets a sukun. The ا at the end changes to ى because it's a comparative form.
Added the small alif (ٰ) after ى to show it's a long vowel sound
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the thing that is eaten
The ًا ending shows this word is the direct object - it's the food that is being acted upon
Changes to present tense by adding ي
Adds 'so' to connect this action with what came before
The final ي is dropped because it's a command, but the meaning stays the same

steps.m: so let him bring to you all
steps.n: Added كُم to show who receives the action (you all)
Changed the verb 'to provide' into the noun 'provision' by adding the kasra (ِ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on middle letter. This pattern is common for making nouns from verbs.
Added بِ at start to mean 'with/by' and tanwin kasratin (ٍ) at end to show it's indefinite (any provision). This word shows how the food should be brought in فَلْيَأْتِكُم (let him bring to you).
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or its source
Added emphasis mark (shadda) on م because it's followed by another م sound (assimilation)

steps.m: from him/it (referring to the food طَعَامًا)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show this means 'from it', referring back to the food (طَعَامًا) mentioned in the verse

steps.m: to be gentle, kind, or gracious

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels
Doubling the middle letter changes it from 'being gentle' to 'making something gentle'
Added تَ at start shows doing the action to yourself - being gentle rather than making others gentle
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at the start
The word changes to show 'let him' instead of just 'he' - like giving gentle advice
Added ل prefix to indicate imperative/command
Added conjunction 'and' to connect with previous command (فَلْيَأْتِكُم - 'then let him bring') in the verse
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يُشْعِرَنَّ (make aware/inform) that follows it
Added وَ (and) to connect this phrase with the previous commands in the verse. This continues the series of instructions being given.
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels
Makes the verb causative - changing from doing the action yourself to making someone else do it
Changes from past to present/future by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel

steps.m: he should definitely not make (anyone) aware (of وَلَا...بِكُمْ أَحَدًا - you all...anyone)
steps.n: Added نَّ for emphasis. This verb is part of a negative command (وَلَا) telling them not to let anyone know about them.
Basic preposition meaning 'with' or 'by' that connects to the verb يُشْعِرَنَّ (make aware/inform) in this ayah
The بِ (with/by) got attached to كُمْ (you all) to show who is being affected by the action. Like adding 'you all' to complete 'with you all'
Connected the root letters together with fatha to create the basic word for 'one'
Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يُشْعِرَنَّ (inform/make aware). The full meaning is 'do not let anyone know about you.'
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain
Adds هُمْ to show this refers to the group of people who might appear/find them (يَظْهَرُوا۟) in the cave. This connects to the next part of the ayah which warns about what these people might do.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a possible future situation. It connects two parts: the condition (if they appear) and the result (they will stone you).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changes from past to present/future by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they appear/overcome (referring to the disbelievers in the story)
steps.n: The وا shows multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to the disbelievers potentially gaining power over the believers.
This is a basic preposition showing something happening on or upon something else. Since it's a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.
The ى changes to ي with a sukun to prepare for the upcoming suffix

steps.m: upon you all (object of يَرْجُمُوا - they stone)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. This word is receiving the action of stoning (يَرْجُمُوا) in this ayah.

steps.m: to throw stones at/to drive away with stones

steps.n: Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, and ُ after last root letter to show basic present tense form

steps.m: they throw stones at (present tense, plural)
steps.n: Added و at the end to make it plural - means multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: they will stone you all (warning in response to إِن يَظْهَرُوا۟ - if they find you)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show who receives the action. This is a threat being made, connected to the condition 'if they find you' (إِن يَظْهَرُوا۟)

This is a conjunction that gives a choice between two possibilities in this ayah: either they will stone you OR return you to their religion. It connects the two verbs يَرْجُمُوكُمْ (they will stone you) and يُعِيدُوكُمْ (they will return you).
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changes to present tense by adding يُ at start, and vowel changes make it mean 'to make something/someone return'
Adds و to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: they return you all (object of يَرْجُمُوكُمْ)
steps.n: Adds كُمْ to show who is being returned. This word is connected to يَرْجُمُوكُمْ (they stone you) with أَوْ (or) in this ayah.
This is a preposition showing something is inside or within something else. Here it connects to مِلَّتِهِمْ (their religion/way) to show being 'in their religion/way'.
Added kasra on first letter and shadda on second letter to create basic noun form
Added ة (taa marboota) at the end to make it mean a complete system/way of life
Added 'i' sound at the end to show it belongs to/connects with another word
Adding هِمْ shows this belongs to them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the verse)
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Added لَن which makes the following verb negative in the future. Here it connects to the verb تُفْلِحُوا (succeed) to say they will never succeed.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense base form
Changed to present tense 'you' form by adding تُ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end
Added plural masculine ending وا to show multiple people. This verb is negated by لَن earlier in the ayah, showing it won't happen in the future.
This is the basic form meaning 'when' or 'at that time'

steps.m: then/in that case (consequence of يَظْهَرُوا۟ - if they discover)
steps.n: Adding tanween alif (ًا) changes the meaning from just 'when' to show that something is a result or consequence. Here it shows what will happen if the people discover them.
Connected the root letters and added fatha to create the basic noun form

steps.m: forever (describing how long they will not succeed)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word describes how the action happens in the sentence وَلَن تُفْلِحُوٓا۟ (and you will never succeed)
Starting with the basic demonstrative word ذا meaning 'that'
Added لِ to give more emphasis to the demonstrative meaning
Added كَ (ka) at start and end to change 'that' into 'like that/in that way'
Added و at beginning to connect this phrase to the next verb أَعْثَرْنَا (We caused them to be found)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Adding أَ at the beginning makes someone else do the action
Adding نَا shows 'we' did the action
The base preposition عَلَى changes its final ى to يْ when adding pronouns after it

steps.m: upon them (referring to the people they discovered)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this means 'them' (referring to the people they found)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why something was done
This is the basic particle that connects parts of a sentence and introduces a new fact or statement

steps.m: that (emphasizing what follows is definitely true)
steps.n: Adding the shadda (doubled letter) and fatha marks makes the meaning stronger and more certain
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb

steps.m: promise (object of لِيَعْلَمُوا - so they would know)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'promise' by adding sukun on middle letter. This word is what they would know (object of لِيَعْلَمُوا) in this ayah.
Changed to the proper name for God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession, changed alif to wasl-alif, and added shadda for emphasis. This word shows who the promise belongs to in the phrase 'promise of Allah'.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and make it a past tense verb
The second letter gets a shadda (doubling mark) to make it stronger, and we remove the last vowel to make it a noun instead of a verb

steps.m: a truth (part of Allah's promise that is حَقٌّ/true)
steps.n: Adding tanween (double damma) shows this is any truth, not a specific one. In this ayah, it's describing Allah's promise as being true.
This is a particle that emphasizes what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes the truth of Allah's promise.

steps.m: and indeed/and that (connects to وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقٌّ)
steps.n: The و connects this to the previous statement about Allah's promise being true
Changed و to ا and added ة to make it mean a specific period of time

steps.m: the Hour (referring to the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and doubled the س letter. In this context, 'the Hour' specifically refers to the Day of Judgment.

steps.m: the Hour (object of أَنَّ - that the Hour)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because it comes after أَنَّ (that) in the ayah
This is a negative particle that denies what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates رَيْبَ (doubt), making the phrase mean 'there is no doubt'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and turn the action into a noun. The ْ (sukun) shows the middle letter is silent.
The fatha (َ) at the end shows this word is being affected by لا (no/not) that comes before it. When لا is used to completely deny something exists, it puts a fatha on the end of the word after it.
Basic preposition showing location or containment

steps.m: in it (referring to السَّاعَةَ - the Hour)
steps.n: Added هَا which refers back to السَّاعَةَ (the Hour) mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: in it (referring to السَّاعَةَ - the Hour)
steps.n: The alif is elongated for emphasis in recitation, but doesn't change the meaning
This is a time particle that connects two events in the past. Here it introduces what happened at the time they were disputing (يَتَنٰزَعُونَ) about their matter.
Form III gives reciprocal/mutual action meaning. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يتنازعون (they dispute).
Added تَ prefix to create reflexive/reciprocal meaning showing mutual action between participants
Added ي prefix to show this is something 'he' does
Masculine plural suffix changes subject from 'he' to 'they'

steps.m: to be separate/distinct from something else

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'between'. This word connects to يَتَنٰزَعُونَ (they disputed) to show what they were disputing about.

steps.m: between them (the people who were disputing)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who was involved in the dispute - it refers back to the people mentioned earlier in the story
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'matter' or 'affair'. This word is what they are disputing about in the phrase 'when they disputed among themselves'.
Added هُمْ to show the matter belongs to them, and َ (fatha) before it because it's needed when connecting هُمْ to the word
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb. This is a common pattern in Arabic for these types of words.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this action follows from what happened before
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
Connected the root letters to make a command meaning 'build!'
Added 'u' sound at start to make it a command to one person
Changed the ending to وا to show it's addressed to multiple people
This is a preposition without a root
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns to make it easier to connect the parts

steps.m: upon them (connected to مَّسْجِدًا - a mosque)
steps.n: Adds هِم to show who the action affects. In this ayah, it refers to the people upon whom a mosque will be built.
Changed from root 'to build' into 'a building' by adding damma and sukun marks
The ان ending makes it mean a complete building rather than just construction
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being built. This connects to the command ابْنُوا (build) earlier in the verse.
Doubling the middle letter makes it stronger - shows someone who does a lot of nurturing/caring
The ُ (damma) shows this is the main subject doing something in the sentence
Added هُمْ at the end to show this belongs to them (the people being discussed in the story)
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic past tense verb

Changed from 'he knew' to 'most knowing' by adding أَ at the start and changing the vowels. Like saying 'He is the most knowing one' instead of just 'he knew'. This describes Allah (their Lord) in the ayah.
Added kasra to the letter ب to show it's a preposition meaning with/by
Added هِمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them' (referring to the people being discussed in the ayah)
Added short vowels 'a' to make it mean 'he said' in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas
Base demonstrative pronoun that points to something specific
Adding ال with shadda makes it more specific and definite
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people. This word refers to the people who won in the discussion mentioned in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added وا with damma before it to show multiple people did the action. This word is connected to قَالَ (said) in the ayah, showing who said something.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else. In this ayah, it connects to أَمْرِهِمْ (their matter/affair) to show what they are deciding upon.
Added an alif that shows this word ends with a long 'aa' sound. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.
Added a small alif (ٓ) above the word to show that the sound is stretched slightly when reading. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
The root 'command' became a noun meaning 'matter/affair' by adding marks (fatha and sukun). Like turning 'command' into 'the thing being commanded about'.
Added kasra to show this word is being possessed by something
Added هِمْ at the end to show this matter belongs to them (the people arguing)
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to Form VIII to show doing action for oneself. The ت combines with خ making a shadda.
Changed to present tense with 'we' as subject
Added لَ to show emphasis and future tense
Added نَّ at the end for extra emphasis
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to pronouns

steps.m: upon them (referring to the people they're discussing building over)
steps.n: Added هِم at the end to show this applies to 'them' (the people being discussed in the ayah)

steps.m: to prostrate, bow down in worship

steps.n: Adding مَ at the start and ِ in the middle creates a noun for the place where prostration happens
Adding ًا shows this is what they will take/establish - it's the object of the verb لَنَتَّخِذَنَّ (we will surely take/establish)

steps.m: a mosque (object of لَنَتَّخِذَنَّ, with emphasis)
steps.n: The shadda on م adds emphasis to show this is definitely a mosque/place of worship they will establish
Added vowels to connect letters and make past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Made plural by adding ونَ at the end
Added future tense marker سَ at beginning
Adding basic vowel marks to make the word readable
Adding alif to make it a number noun

steps.m: three (subject of سَيَقُولُونَ - they will say)
steps.n: Changed from the number 'three' to 'three' as a countable noun by adding ة and two dots on top (tanween), so it can be the subject of 'they will say'
Changes from basic root to ordinal number pattern فَاعِل
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing something
Added هُمْ at the end to show it belongs to them
Added shadda on first letter for pronunciation after previous word
Basic root letters get vowels to make the common word for 'dog'
Added u-sound at end to show this is the subject (doer) of the sentence
Added attached pronoun هُمْ to show possession

steps.m: to say something, to speak words

steps.n: Adds يَ at the start to show someone is doing this now, and adds vowels to connect the letters
Adds ونَ at the end to show many people are doing it together

steps.m: and they say/are saying (about the number of people in the cave)
steps.n: Adds وَ (and) at the start to connect this with the previous statement about what people say
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the basic number word
Adding feminine marker to create the number 'five'

steps.m: five (as a subject in the sentence)
steps.n: Adds two dots (tanween) at the end to show this is a complete subject in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed from verb 'to make six' into the number 'sixth'
Adds the nominative case marker to indicate this word's grammatical role as a subject or predicate

steps.m: their sixth one (referring to a person in the group being discussed)
steps.n: Adds attached pronoun هم meaning 'their' to show possession. This word is part of a discussion about how many people were in the cave, with their dog being mentioned as an additional member.
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject (doer) in the sentence. This dog is being described as 'their fourth one' (رَّابِعُهُمْ).
Adds possessive pronoun 'their'
Connected the root letters together with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make it into a noun meaning the act itself
Added tanween alif to show this describes how they are speaking - they're just making guesses without real knowledge
Changes from verb 'to be hidden' to noun meaning 'something hidden'
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it's being acted upon by the بِ that comes next
Added بِ at the beginning to show how something is done. The ا in ال changes to ٱ after بِ
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels. The و stays because it's a weak letter.
The ونَ ending shows this is being done by three or more people (all male or mixed group)
Added 'و' at start to connect this with what came before it
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together

steps.m: seven (as a complete number in this counting sequence)
steps.n: The ة makes it a complete number form, and the tanwin (ٌ) shows it's part of a sequence (they are counting up: five, six, seven...)
Changes the root 'eight' into 'eighth' by adding alif and kasra, with tanween to make it indefinite (like saying 'an eighth one')
The suffix 'هُمْ' means 'their'. It shows that the eighth one belongs to them.
The prefix 'وَ' means 'and'. It connects this phrase to something that came before it.
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun form. This word is being described as their fourth companion in the phrase رَّابِعُهُمْ كَلْبُهُمْ (their fourth one was their dog).
Added ُهُمْ at the end to show possession - 'their dog'. The ُ before هُمْ connects it to the word.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb

Changed to a command form. The middle letter و drops out because it's a 'weak' letter that often disappears in commands. This command is from Allah to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in response to what others were saying about the People of the Cave.
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly
The kasra ending shows this word is connected to something coming after it
Adding ي at the end makes it mean 'my lord' - shows possession/belonging
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic past tense verb

steps.m: most knowing (in response to يَقُولُونَ - what they say)
steps.n: Changed from 'he knew' to 'most knowing' by adding أ at start - shows Allah knows best compared to others. Links to 'they say' (يَقُولُونَ) in the verse.
Root letters get vowels added to make a noun meaning 'how many' of something
Added shadda (doubling mark) on middle letter and feminine marker ة to create a specific instance noun
Added the preposition بِ (with) to the beginning of the word
The ة at the end changed to تِ to connect with a word showing ownership
Added possessive pronoun هِم to show ownership/possession
This is the basic negative particle 'ma' that negates what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates يَعْلَمُهُمْ (knows them) to mean 'does not know them'.
The shadda (double letter mark) adds emphasis to the negation, making it stronger - like saying 'definitely not' or 'absolutely not'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The يَ at the start shows it's happening now (present tense)

steps.m: he knows them (referring to the number of companions of the cave)
steps.n: هُمْ means 'them' - here referring back to the people being discussed in the verse about the companions of the cave

steps.m: except (what follows from what came before)
steps.n: This is a special word that creates an exception - it tells us that what comes after it is different from what came before. In this ayah, it connects to مِرَآءً (argument/dispute) to say you should only argue in a certain way.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Changed the word to describe a quality. In this ayah, it's describing how few people know about the people of the cave.
Basic negation particle that tells us not to do something. Here it's telling us not to argue/dispute (تُمَارِ).
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence - because of what was mentioned before, therefore do not argue about it.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Added تُ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action in the present tense
Combines preposition في (in/concerning) with pronoun هم (them)
This starts with two separate particles that join together: إِنْ (if) and لَا (not)
The ن merges with ل creating a shadda (ّ). The meaning shifts from 'if not' to 'except'. In this ayah, it shows that only a few (قَلِيلٌ) know their number.
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the act of disputing
The ً (tanween) at the end shows this word is receiving the action 'do not dispute'. The alif is written longer (آ) because it comes at the end of the word.
Added vowels (َ) to make it past tense meaning 'he/it became visible'
Changed the pattern to make it describe something that has this quality
Added tanween alif to make it describe how something is done. In this ayah, it describes how not to argue about them except openly/visibly.
Added fatha to show this is the basic negative particle لا that stops or prevents something
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) between the root letters and an alif at the end to make 'he guided' in the past tense
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or requesting guidance from someone
Changed to present tense 'you' form by adding ت at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'you seek guidance' to 'seek guidance!' by removing the last letter
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it connects to the verb تُمَارِ (argue) to show what is being argued about.
Added هِ to show who/what is being referred to (singular masculine)
Changed هِ to هِم to show it refers to multiple people (the companions of the cave) instead of just one
This is a basic preposition showing source or origin
Added emphasis to the م with shadda for stronger pronunciation after connecting with previous word
Added هُمْ at the end to show this refers to 'them' (a group of males or mixed group)
This root relates to the concept of oneness or being singular
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it a complete word meaning 'one'
Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of asking in the phrase 'don't ask anyone'
This is a negative particle that tells us the action تَقُولَنَّ (saying) should not happen
The و at the start connects this with what came before it, like saying 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ
Added نَّ at the end to show emphasis and certainty about the future action
Root letters ش-ي-أ form the noun شَيْء meaning 'thing'. The hamza (ء) is part of the root.
Changed the spelling to show the long 'aa' sound with ا and ى, but pronunciation stays the same
Added لِ at the beginning to show this thing is being directed to or used for something. This connects to the word فَاعِلٌ (doer) later in the ayah.
Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is any general thing, not a specific thing. This word is connected to فَاعِلٌ (doer) in the ayah.
This is a word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain, like adding 'definitely' in English
Added ى at the end to show 'I' am doing the action. This word connects to فَاعِلٌ (doer) later in the ayah, showing that 'I' am the one who will be doing something.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
This form shows who does the action. The word comes after إِنِّى (indeed I am) in the ayah.
Adding tanween dammah makes the word indefinite
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is written as a small mark above the ذ to show emphasis
Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)

steps.m: that (pointing to something not close by)
steps.n: The كَ at the end turns this into a complete word meaning 'that over there' (pointing to something far away)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the base noun form
Added tanween alif to make it mean 'tomorrow' as a time when something will happen. Like saying 'I will do it tomorrow'
This is a basic exception word that shows something is left out from what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it connects to يَشَآءَ (wills/wishes) to mean 'except that Allah wills'
The alif at the end is written as آ instead of ا. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning or pronunciation

This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it connects the exception (إِلَّآ - except) to what follows (يَشَآءَ - he wills), showing that something will only happen if Allah wills it.
The root letters change to make 'he wanted' - the middle letter becomes a long aa sound. This is a past tense verb pattern.

steps.m: he wants/wishes (in this ayah: unless Allah wills/wishes)
steps.n: Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now or in the future. The long alif (آ) shows emphasis in the meaning.

steps.m: to worship, to be devoted to
steps.n: This is the three-letter root that gives us the idea of worship and devotion
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special divine name
Added emphasis by doubling the ل letter

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يَشَآءَ - He wills)
steps.n: Added ُ to show Allah is doing the action of willing in this ayah. The alif at the start is written with a special mark (wasl) showing it connects with the previous word.
Added sukun on first letter and damma on second letter to make it a command form
Added 'alif' at start makes it a direct command meaning 'you remember!'

steps.m: and remember! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: The و at the beginning means 'and'
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does the caring in a complete way
The extra shadda on the first letter comes from the word before it ending in a vowel. It doesn't change the meaning.
This is the basic word that refers to a point in time
Adding ا makes it refer to a conditional future time. In this ayah, it introduces what to do when you forget something.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make the past tense form
The تَ at the end shows this is talking about 'you'
Changed to a command form by adding ُ on first letter and ْ on last letter. The middle letter و is dropped because it's a weak letter.

steps.m: and say! (connecting to previous command وَٱذْكُر)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this command with the previous command in the ayah (وَٱذْكُر - and remember)

steps.m: it is hoped (that يَهْدِيَنِ will happen)
steps.n: The root letters form a special word meaning 'hopefully' or 'maybe'. It connects to يَهْدِيَنِ to express hope that the guidance will happen.

steps.m: it is hoped (that يَهْدِيَنِ will happen)
steps.n: Added small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced like a long aa sound
Added hamza above (ٓ) to show a slight pause in pronunciation
This is a basic connecting word that introduces what someone wants, thinks, or hopes to do

steps.m: to guide, to show the right way

steps.n: Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, with هْ (sukun) to connect smoothly, and ِ (kasra) before final ي
Added نِ at the end to mean 'me' - changes from 'he guides' to 'guide me'. The ي at the end is dropped before adding نِ

steps.m: Lord (one who cares for and nurtures)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly
Adding the kasra (small diagonal line) at the end shows this word 'Lord' connects to what comes after it, like saying 'Lord of...'

steps.m: my Lord (subject of يَهْدِيَنِ - guides me)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this is 'my' Lord. This word is the subject doing the action in يَهْدِيَنِ (guides me) in this ayah.
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'more' or 'most'

steps.m: so that I may be nearer to (connected to يَهْدِيَنِ - guide me)
steps.n: The لِ at the start shows purpose - 'in order to' or 'so that'

steps.m: from/than (used here to compare with هٰذَا)
steps.n: A basic preposition showing comparison in this ayah - comparing something to 'this' (هٰذَا)
This is the base attention-getting part that starts many pointing words
Added ذ which specifies pointing to something near
Added ا at the end to make it a complete demonstrative pronoun for masculine singular things

steps.m: to guide, to lead in the right way
he guided/led in the right way
steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Changed from a verb (guided) to a noun (guidance) and added tanween to show it's the object being guided to
Connected the root letters together with vowels to make a past tense verb
Added وا to show multiple people did the action, and changed the middle vowel to damma

steps.m: and they stayed (in كَهْفِهِمْ their cave)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it. This word describes what happened in their cave (كَهْفِهِمْ) as mentioned right after this word.
Basic preposition showing location or containment
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how to pronounce it correctly

steps.m: in (location preposition for كَهْفِهِمْ - their cave)
steps.n: Changed the final ي to ى which is a common spelling variation. This preposition connects to كَهْفِهِمْ (their cave) to show where they remained.
Connected the root letters together with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
Added kasra to show this cave belongs to someone

steps.m: their cave (referring to the people of the cave mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show the cave belongs to them

steps.m: three or relating to the number three

steps.n: Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make the basic word for 'three'
Added long 'aa' sound between second and third letters to make it a number noun
The ة makes this a feminine noun meaning 'a hundred'
Added tanwin kasra to show this word is possessed by the number ثَلٰثَ (three) before it, forming 'three hundred'
Added vowel pattern for numbers/quantities
Root س-ن-و means passing time, adding vowels and ة makes it mean 'one year'
Changed vowels to kasra and added ين to make it plural. This word follows 'three hundred' (ثَلٰثَ مِا۟ئَةٍ) in the ayah, showing how many years they stayed.
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of the number three hundred in the phrase 'three hundred years'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Added اِ at start and changed ز to زد to show the action happens by itself
Added وا to show multiple people did the action
Added و at start to connect with previous phrase. The اِ changes to ٱ after و
Added the standard vowel pattern for numbers with kasra and sukun

steps.m: nine (as the amount increased in وَٱزْدَادُوا۟)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object of the verb 'increased' - they increased by nine years
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he said' to a command 'say!' by dropping middle letter و and changing vowels
Adding a kasra at the end helps connect the word to what comes after it
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to elative form (comparative/superlative) to show Allah's supreme knowledge. This word is the predicate (describes Allah) in the sentence.
This combines the preposition بِ (with/by) and the relative pronoun ما (what)

steps.m: to remain or stay somewhere for a period

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make the basic past tense form showing one person did the action
Added وا to show multiple people did the action, with small circle above to show proper pronunciation
Added fatha to show the basic preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'
Added the pronoun هُ (him/it) to show who the preposition is directed to. This refers back to اللَّهُ (Allah) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added a small و mark (ۥ) after هُ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it into a noun meaning 'the thing that is unseen'
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the verb أَبْصِرْ (see) in this ayah
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense. The و changes to ا at the end.
Adding اء at the end turns the action into a noun for the place above us
The ء changes back to و when making it plural, then we add ات to show there are many skies/heavens
Adding ٱل makes it specific and doubles the س to connect the sounds
The ِ at the end shows it's owned by something (the word غيب/unseen). The alif is written as ٰ in this style.
Connected the root letters together with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make it a proper word
Added ال to make it specific
Added و at beginning to connect it to the previous word السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens)

steps.m: and the earth (possessed by/connected to something)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it's being possessed by or connected to something else in the sentence

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb 'he saw' by adding vowel marks
Changed from past tense 'he saw' to command 'see!' by changing vowels
Added 'a' sound at start (hamza) to make the command easier to say
Added kasra to the letter ب to show it's a preposition meaning 'with'
Added the pronoun ه with kasra to show it means 'him/it'

steps.m: with him/it (referring to Allah's knowledge mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ۦ) after ه to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he heard' to 'listen!' by making it a command form
Added conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'

steps.m: that which (relates to لَبِثُوا - they remained)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that links to the time period they remained. In this context, it's not asking a question but rather referring to the amount of time.
Added fatha to the letter lam to show its pronunciation
Added هُ (him) to show who the preposition is directed to
Changed هُ (him) to هُم (them) to show it refers to multiple people
Added kasra (ِ) to show this is a preposition meaning 'from'
Added shadda (ّ) because this word connects with the previous word دُونِهِۦ in speech, causing the 'n' sound to be emphasized
Adding damma (ُ) gives the word more emphasis
Added kasra to show this connects to the next word
Added هِۦ to show this means 'besides Him' referring back to Allah in this ayah
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it belongs to. In this ayah, it connects with دُونِهِۦ (besides/other than Him) to show what they don't have.

steps.m: to be close to and take care of
he was close to/took care of
steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed from a past-tense verb (he protected) to a noun meaning protector, with shadda showing strong protection
Added kasra tanween to show this is any guardian, not a specific one. This word comes after مِن (from/of) in the ayah.
Starting with the basic negation word لا which means 'not' and is used to negate what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation with what came before it. This وَلَا negates the verb يُشْرِكُ (associates/shares) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changes to present tense by adding يُ at start, changing internal vowels, and adding ُ at end

steps.m: in (connects حُكْمِهِ with what comes before)
steps.n: Basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects 'His judgment' (حُكْمِهِ) to the previous part of the verse.
Root ح-ك-م became a noun meaning 'judgment' with damma and sukun, then kasra was added because it follows the word في
Added هِۦٓ at the end to show this is His judgment. The ۦٓ marks are just for proper pronunciation.
Added short 'a' sounds (fatha) between letters to make it a complete word meaning 'one'
Added ًا at the end to show this word is receiving the action of يُشْرِكُ (associates/partners) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا when it has a fatha.
Changed from 'he recited' to 'you recite!' command form by adding u- at start and changing vowels

steps.m: and recite! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to recite what follows)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning. This connects it to previous instructions. The alif gets a special mark (waṣla) showing it merges with the و.
This is a basic word that can't be broken down into smaller parts. It's used to ask questions or refer to things.
Added fatha (َ) on م to show how to pronounce it correctly.
Added the long alif (آ) to show it's pronounced with a longer sound. In this ayah, it refers to the revelation that was given to the Prophet.

steps.m: to reveal or inspire (especially divine knowledge)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels

steps.m: it was revealed (object of تَجِدَ - you will find)
steps.n: Changed to passive voice to show the revelation was received rather than given. This word is what 'you will find' (تَجِدَ) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
Added ك at the end to show this is directed 'to you'. This word shows the Prophet ﷺ is receiving revelation in this ayah.
This is a preposition that connects words by showing where something starts, comes from, or what it's made of. In this ayah, it connects 'book' (كِتَابِ) to what follows.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the vowel pattern and added alif to transform the verb into a noun meaning 'book'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by رَبِّكَ (your Lord) in the phrase 'book of your Lord'

steps.m: Lord (one who cares for and develops)
steps.n: The doubled letter ّ (shadda) shows this is someone who does this action perfectly and completely
The kasra (ِ) shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else that comes after it
Adding كَ shows this Lord belongs to the person being spoken to. This whole phrase رَبِّكَ belongs to كِتَابِ from earlier in the ayah, forming 'book of your Lord'
This is a negative particle that makes something not true. In this ayah, it negates مُبَدِّلَ (changer), saying there is no one who can change.
Doubling the middle letter shows the action is done thoroughly or multiple times

steps.m: one who changes/alters (something that cannot be changed)
steps.n: Adding مُ at the start and changing the vowels makes this describe someone who does the action. In this ayah, it's saying no one can be a changer of Allah's words.
Added vowels and ة to change from 'speaking' to 'one word'
Changed from one word to many words by adding ات at the end
Added هِ to show possession by Allah
Added لِ at beginning to show purpose or direction, and ۦ is just a pronunciation mark
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it future tense and says it will never happen
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra. The و drops because it's a weak letter at the start of a present tense verb.
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ at the start
Changed the final vowel from ُ to َ because it comes after لَن which means 'will never' and requires this change

steps.m: from (connecting كِتَابِ to what comes before it)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'book' (كِتَابِ) to what should be read/recited.

steps.m: from (connecting كِتَابِ to what comes before it)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word is connecting to what comes after it

steps.m: to be lower or beneath something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'beneath/lower than'
Adding kasra shows this word connects to something after it
Adding هِۦ shows this refers to Him/it

steps.m: to turn aside or seek refuge

steps.n: Adding fatha vowels creates the basic past tense verb form
Adding مُ at beginning makes this a place noun (where something happens) rather than showing who does the action
Adding ت shows this is a place you go to for protection/refuge
The tanween fatha (ًا) shows this is the object of the verb تَجِدَ (you will find) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he was patient' to 'be patient!' by adding alif at start and changing vowels to make it a command

steps.m: and be patient! (connected to previous command)
steps.n: Added و at start to connect with previous command, with alif changing to hamza-wasl (ٱ)
Connected the root letters with fatha-sukun pattern to make it a noun. This word is what's being acted on by the verb اصْبِرْ (be patient with).
Adds possessive pronoun to indicate 'your self'. This word is the direct object of the verb 'be patient' (وَٱصْبِرْ), showing who should be patient.
This is the basic form of the word that shows being together with something or someone. Since it's a preposition, it doesn't come from a root.
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly. In this ayah, it connects 'be patient, yourself' with 'those who' to show who you should be patient with.
Starting with the basic demonstrative word and changing it to make a relative pronoun
Added ن to make it refer to multiple people or things
Added ال to make it specific and turn it into 'those who'

steps.m: those who (referring to the people who call upon their Lord)
steps.n: Added vowel marks and changed ال to ٱ for proper pronunciation. This word connects to يَدْعُونَ (they call upon) which comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added يَ at start to show present tense. The ا at the end changed to و because it's a special verb that ends in و in present tense.

steps.m: they call out (referring to those who call out to their Lord)
steps.n: Added نَ to show multiple people are doing the action. This word connects to رَبَّهُم (their Lord) in the ayah.
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to show complete mastery
Added هُم at the end to show it belongs to them. This word is what they are calling upon in يَدْعُونَ (they call upon)

steps.m: to go or do something early in the morning

steps.n: Changed from a verb root (doing something in morning) to a noun (morning time) by adding vowel marks
Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance and changed middle vowel to sukun
Added ال (the) at the start to make it specific
Added preposition ب with kasra to show 'in/during'
Changed spelling to standard form: 'al' is written with ٱ, added a small alif (ٰ) above, and ends with kasra (-i sound)
Adds vowels and shadda to make a noun meaning evening/nighttime
Added the definite article ال to specify a particular evening

Added و to connect with the word بِٱلْغَدَوٰةِ (in the morning) mentioned earlier in the ayah. The ال changes to ٱل after و, and added kasra at the end because it's connected to what comes after it in the sentence.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Added يُ at start to show present tense. The middle و changes to ي because it's between two vowels (this is a common pattern with hollow verbs)

steps.m: they want/seek (referring to those who call upon their Lord)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This refers back to الَّذِينَ (those who) in the ayah
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a noun meaning 'face'
Added fatha to show this is the object being acted upon by يُرِيدُونَ (they seek)
Added pronoun هُ to show possession - this is what they are seeking
This is the basic negative particle that means 'not' or 'do not'

steps.m: and not (connected to تَعْدُ - let stray)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before. This connects to the verb تَعْدُ (let stray) to form the complete phrase 'and do not let stray'.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense

steps.m: you go beyond (negative command in this ayah)
steps.n: Changed from 'he' to 'you' form and made present tense by adding تَ at start. When used after لا it becomes 'do not go beyond'

steps.m: you go beyond (negative command with لا)
steps.n: The final و is dropped because it's a weak letter at the end of the word
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added alif at the end to show exactly two eyes

steps.m: your two eyes (subject of لا تَعْدُ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show these are your eyes. This word is the subject (doer) of لا تَعْدُ (do not let turn away) in this ayah.
Added sukun to show the end of the preposition before adding pronouns

steps.m: away from them (referring to those who call upon their Lord)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being moved away from. This refers back to the people mentioned earlier who call upon their Lord morning and evening.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Changed from past tense to present tense by adding ي and changing vowels. The و became ي in the middle.
تُ at start shows it's either 'you' (male) or 'she' doing the wanting
Changed from a verb (to beautify) into a noun (beauty) by adding the kasra sound
Added ة at the end to show one specific instance of beauty/decoration. This word is what is being sought (object) in the phrase 'seeking the decoration'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb تُرِيدُ (you seek/want)
Added vowel marks (fatha) between the root letters to make the basic word meaning 'life'
Changed from 'living' to 'life' by adding و and ة at the end - like changing 'alive' to 'life' in English
Added 'ال' (the) at the start to mean 'the life', and the middle و became a long ā sound
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by زِينَةَ (adornment), forming the phrase 'adornment of the life'
The root letters form a word meaning 'nearest/closest' by adding ي and ا at the end, making it feminine. Used to mean 'this life' since it's closest to us
Adds 'the' to make it specific. The ل merges into د making it doubled (shown by ّ). This word describes 'life' (الْحَيَوٰةِ) in the phrase 'adornment of worldly life'.
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation of the negation particle
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This connects to the verb تَعْدُ (stray) in the ayah.
Changed the middle root letter و to ا (alif) and added fathas to make 'he obeyed' in the past tense
Added تُ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: obey! (as a command with لا meaning 'do not obey')
steps.n: Changed the end of the word to make it a command form. When used with لا before it, it means 'do not obey'
This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of the sentence. Here it introduces the person who Allah has made heedless, connecting to the next word أَغْفَلْنَا (We made heedless).
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Adding أَ at the beginning makes it mean 'to cause someone to do something' instead of just doing it yourself
Adding نا at the end changes it from 'he' to 'we'
Root ق-ل-ب became 'heart' (قَلْبَ) with fatha at end showing something is happening to this heart
The suffix هُۥ means 'his'. This word is the direct object (accusative case) of the verb أَغْفَلْنَا (We made heedless).

steps.m: away from (the people mentioned in عَيْنَاكَ عَنْهُمْ)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows movement or attention going away from something. In this ayah, it's used with عَيْنَاكَ (your eyes) to mean 'turning your eyes away from them'.
Changed the action into a noun by adding ِ (kasra) on first letter and ْ (sukun) on middle letter
Added نَا at the end to show it belongs to 'us'. The word follows عَن (from) in this ayah, which is why it has the ِ (kasra) at the end.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added alif in front and doubled the middle letter to show doing something with more effort and purpose
Added و at beginning to connect to previous statement, with hamzat wasl (ٱ) replacing regular hamza
Adding vowel marks to make it a complete past tense verb
Added هُ at the end to show possession (his). This word is being acted upon by the verb اتَّبَعَ (followed) in the ayah.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak middle letter in the past tense
Adds conjunction و meaning 'and'

steps.m: to command/order, to deal with matters

steps.n: The root letters أ-م-ر that mean 'to command' become a simple noun أَمْر meaning 'matter/affair'. Like changing 'command' to 'a command'.
Added ُ to show it's doing the action of كَانَ (was), and هُۥ meaning 'his'
The dammas change this into a plural noun showing multiple instances of excess
Added tanwin alif to show this describes how excessive 'his affair' was. This word connects to أَمْرُهُۥ (his affair) in the ayah, describing it as being wastefully excessive.
Command form made by adding 'u' sound on top and dropping middle letter
Added kasra at the end to connect it to the next word
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this command to what follows (ٱلْحَقُّ - the truth)

steps.m: to be true, real, or right

steps.n: The doubled letter shows this is a strong, definite truth
Added 'the' at the beginning and ُ at the end to show this is the main subject of what is being said
This is a particle that shows where something comes from or starts. Here it connects to the word رَّبِّكُمْ (your Lord) to show the truth is coming from your Lord.
Added kasra at the end to show possession
Added كُمْ at the end to show the lord belongs to multiple people being addressed
Added shadda on first letter because it comes after a word ending in a vowel
This is a question word that can mean 'who' when asking a question, but here means 'whoever' because it's being used in a conditional statement

steps.m: so whoever (connects to يُؤْمِن - let him believe)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence. This word connects to يُؤْمِن (let him believe) later in the ayah to mean 'so whoever... let him believe'
Added fathas to make it mean 'he wanted' in the past
The weak letter ي changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs
The kasra (ِ) in the middle shows this describes being in a state of safety
Added يُ at start to make it present tense. The root أمن gained a hamza (ء) to make it mean 'believe/trust' instead of 'feel safe'
Added لْ to make it a command/request form
Added فَ to connect this command with what came before it in the verse
This is a basic interrogative/conditional particle that means 'whoever' or 'who'. In this ayah, it's used as a conditional particle meaning 'whoever'.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This word is the subject of the verb شَآءَ (willed/chose) in this ayah.
This is the basic meaning showing the concept of wanting or wishing
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he wanted' in the past tense
Changed to a hollow verb pattern by adding long alif between first and second root letters
This is the basic meaning showing rejection or denial of something
Connected the letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he disbelieved) to present tense (he disbelieves) by adding يَ at start and shortening the end
Adds 'so/then' to connect this action with what came before it
Makes it into a command/imperative form meaning 'let him do it'
Adding shadda changes this from a word meaning 'if' to a word that emphasizes what comes after it, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly'
Adding نا at the end shows this is about 'we/us', like changing 'indeed' to 'indeed we'
The ا is written as آ when it comes at the end of إِنَّ. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: to cause to prepare/make ready (doer after إنّ)
steps.n: Form IV (أفعل) transformation making the verb causative/transitive. This verb is part of أعتدنا which is the subject of the particle إنّ (indeed/verily) at the start of the clause.
Added نا to show 'we' did the action. This word follows إِنَّآ (indeed we) in the ayah, emphasizing that Allah is stating what He has prepared.
Changed from root letters to past tense verb 'he wronged' by adding vowel marks
Changed the word to describe someone who does the action
Made the word plural to refer to multiple people who do wrong
Added ال to point to specific wrongdoers
Added لِ at the beginning to show 'for' and َ at the end because it follows لِ
The middle letter و changes to ا and we add fatha on the first letter to make the noun for 'fire'

steps.m: fire (object of أَعْتَدْنَا - we have prepared)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being prepared in the phrase 'we have prepared for the wrongdoers a fire'

steps.m: to surround or protect something by enclosing it

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha) to the root letters to make it mean 'he surrounded' in the past
The و changes to ا because it's between two fathas

steps.m: he completely surrounded (the fire surrounds the wrongdoers)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning makes the surrounding more complete and intense. In this ayah, it describes how the fire will completely surround the wrongdoers.
Added kasra to the letter ب to show it's a preposition meaning with/by
Added ه with kasra to show this refers to 'him/it'

steps.m: with them (refers to سُرَادِقُهَا - its walls/enclosure)
steps.n: Added م with sukun to change 'him' to 'them', referring to the walls/enclosure mentioned right after this word

steps.m: to enclose or surround with walls
steps.n: Forms the base noun meaning 'enclosure' or 'pavilion'
Changed the root letters into a noun by adding vowels to make a word meaning a large tent or covering
Added damma to show this is the subject doing the surrounding in relation to أَحَاطَ (it surrounded)
Added هَا to show this tent/canopy belongs to the Fire mentioned earlier (نَارًا)
Basic conditional particle that introduces a condition. Here it introduces what happens if the people in Hell ask for help.
Adds و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. The و shows this is part of a longer description of what happens to wrongdoers.
Basic meaning of helping or rescuing someone
Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Changed to passive form by changing first vowel to kasra and middle weak letter to ي
Added يَسْتَ (yasta) at start to mean 'seeking/asking for' help, and changed ending to show it's happening now
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Forms passive verb meaning 'to be helped'
Changed root letters غ-و-ث into past tense verb by making the middle letter و into ا (alif)
Changed from 'he helped' to 'he is helped' by adding يُ at start and ُ at end to make it passive
Added plural masculine ending to show multiple people receiving the action
This root relates to liquids and flowing things
The middle letter و changes to ا and we add ء at the end to make the noun 'water'
Adding the long آ makes the word stronger/more emphatic

steps.m: with water (used to describe الشَّرَابُ - the drink)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'with' and tanween kasra at the end to show it's indefinite. This describes what kind of drink is being talked about in the ayah.
This is the basic concept of melting or flowing slowly
Changes the verb concept into a noun for the molten substance itself
Makes the word specific by adding 'the'

steps.m: like the molten metal (that will scald faces)
steps.n: Adds 'like' to compare something to molten metal. In this ayah, it's describing the terrible drink given to wrongdoers that will scald their faces.
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he roasted' in the past tense
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This verb acts on 'the faces' (الْوُجُوهَ) in this ayah.
The ى at the end shows this is a present tense verb that ends in a weak letter
This is the basic concept of facing or turning in a direction
Forms the singular noun 'face' from the basic concept
Changes singular 'face' to plural 'faces' by adding و and changing vowels
Shows we're talking about specific faces, not just any faces
Shows that the faces are receiving the action of scalding from the word يَشْوِى (scalds/burns)
Changes from basic root to a word expressing strong disapproval
Added alif after second letter to make it into a noun meaning the thing you drink
Added ال at beginning to make it specific and shadda on ش because it comes after ال
Added damma at end to show this is the subject. Also changed initial alif to wasl alif since it connects with previous word.
Root letters joined together and middle letter و changed to ا to make past tense verb meaning 'it became bad'
Added ت at end to show the subject is feminine
Added sukoon to show this is the end of the word

steps.m: and it (feminine) became bad/evil (describing مُرْتَفَقًا - a resting place)
steps.n: Added و with fatha to connect this word to what came before it. This word describes how bad the resting place (مُرْتَفَقًا) is.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and ت in middle to turn 'being comfortable' into 'a comfortable place', like turning 'rest' into 'resting place'

steps.m: a place of comfort/rest (as the object being described)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is being used as a description of something specific
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger. The shadda (ّ) doubles the ن sound. In this ayah, it emphasizes the description of those who believe and do good deeds.

steps.m: those who - points to specific people being talked about
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun used to describe a group of people
This is the basic meaning of safety/security
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he made others feel safe/he believed
steps.n: Adding long alif at start changes meaning to 'making someone else do the action'
Adding وا at the end shows that a group of people (they) did the action. This word comes after إِنَّ (indeed/verily) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding وا makes it refer to a group of men in the past

steps.m: and they worked/did (connected to ءَامَنُوا۟ - believed)
steps.n: Added و to connect this action with the previous word ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed). This shows these people both believed AND did good deeds.
This is the basic meaning of being good/proper
This pattern shows who or what has this quality
Adding ات makes it plural feminine, referring to deeds/actions rather than people
Adding ال makes it definite and causes the ص to get a shadda
The kasra ending shows this is the object of the verb عَمِلُوا (they did/performed)

steps.m: indeed/truly/verily (emphasizes لَا نُضِيعُ - we do not let go to waste)
steps.n: This is an emphasis particle that makes the following statement stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'surely' in English. Here it emphasizes that Allah will definitely not let good deeds go to waste.

steps.m: indeed we (subject of لَا نُضِيعُ - we do not let go to waste)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end shows this is about 'we/us'. This connects to the next words لَا نُضِيعُ (we do not let go to waste), showing who will not waste the good deeds.

steps.m: not (negates نُضِيعُ/we let go to waste)
steps.n: This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it mean the opposite. In this ayah, it makes نُضِيعُ (we let go to waste) mean 'we do not let go to waste'.

steps.m: not (negates نُضِيعُ/we let go to waste)
steps.n: Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Added نُ at start to mean 'we', changed vowels to show present tense. This word is negated by لَا (not) in the ayah, so it means 'we do not waste'
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning the reward itself rather than the act of rewarding

steps.m: reward/payment (object of نُضِيعُ - we do not lose)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb نُضِيعُ (we do not lose/waste) in the ayah
This is a special word that refers to any person who does something. In this ayah, it connects to the word أَحْسَنَ (did good) to mean 'whoever did good'.
This is the basic meaning of being good
Added the vowels 'a' and 'u' to make it mean 'he/it was good' in the past

steps.m: he did (something) in the best way (describes عَمَلًا)
steps.n: Added أ at the beginning to show doing something in the best way. This word is describing how the person did their عَمَلًا (deed/action).
This is the basic meaning of doing work
Adding the vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb meaning 'he did something'

steps.m: a work/deed (object of أَحْسَنَ - did well)
steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this is the thing being done well in the phrase 'did good work'. It's the object of the verb أَحْسَنَ (did well/excellently).
This is the basic form of the demonstrative pronoun that points to multiple people or things
The alif with line (ٰ) shows distance - like pointing to something far away
Added small circle (۟) and small alif (ٓ) marks to help with pronunciation

steps.m: those (pointing to people far away) (who will have جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ)
steps.n: Added ئِكَ at the end to show we're specifically pointing to people. This word points to the people who will have gardens of Eden (جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ) mentioned right after.
Added fatha to the letter lam to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for them (who will have جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هم (them) to show who the word 'for' refers to. This word connects to جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ (Gardens of Eden) which comes right after it in the ayah, showing these gardens are for them.

steps.m: to cover or hide (like a garden's shade)

steps.n: Adding vowel marks to make it a basic verb meaning 'to cover'
From the root meaning 'to cover/hide', added ات to make it plural 'gardens'. The ن is doubled with a shaddah. Often paired with عَدْنٍ to mean 'Gardens of Eden'
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing something in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create a noun meaning a place of permanent dwelling
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this word describes the gardens (جَنَّاتُ) mentioned before it
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb

steps.m: it flows (referring to الْأَنْهٰرُ - the rivers)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start and changing vowels. This shows the rivers are continuously flowing in Paradise.
This is a preposition that shows what something is made from or comes from. Here it shows the bracelets are made of gold.
Becomes an adverb showing location
Added vowel marks to show exact location underneath something
Changed final vowel to kasra to show possession
Added هِمُ to show 'them'. This word connects to تَجْرِى (flows) in the ayah, showing rivers flow beneath them.
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun form for a single river
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple rivers
Added ال to make it definite ('the rivers'). The ُ shows it's doing the flowing (subject of تَجْرِى - flows/flowing). The long ٰ is just a spelling variation of ا.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda shows the action is done with extra effort or care
Adding يُ at the start changes it from 'he decorated' to 'being decorated' - like changing from active to passive voice

steps.m: they are being decorated (with gold bracelets)
steps.n: Adding ون shows this is happening to many people. This refers to the believers being adorned with gold bracelets in Paradise.
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else
Added ها at the end to show this is happening inside the gardens (جَنَّاتُ) mentioned earlier in the verse
This is a preposition showing where the rivers flow from beneath them. In this ayah, it connects تَجْرِى (flows) with تَحْتِهِمُ (beneath them).
Root meaning 'wrap around' becomes a bracelet - something that wraps around your wrist
Changes one bracelet to many bracelets by adding أ at the start and changing the middle letters
Adds fatha at the end to show this is the object being adorned with (after مِنْ in يُحَلَّوْنَ فِيهَا مِنْ أَسَاوِرَ)
This is a basic preposition showing where the rivers flow from. It connects the verb تَجْرِى (flow) with تَحْتِهِمُ (beneath them).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun meaning 'gold'
Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this describes what the bracelets are made of
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, changed vowels to show continuous action
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: and they wear (connected to يُحَلَّوْنَ - they are adorned)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this action with being adorned with bracelets mentioned before
This root represents the basic concept of clothing or dressing
Changed the root into a noun for a single piece of clothing
Changed the word to show many pieces of clothing by adding ي and ا between the root letters
Added ًا to show these are the clothes being worn in the sentence يَلْبَسُونَ ثِيَابًا (they wear clothes)
This makes it a plural adjective describing color
Added tanween alif to show this word describes the garments (ثِيَابًا) mentioned in the verse
This is a basic preposition that shows what something is made from or comes from. Here it connects to سُندُسٍ (fine silk) to show what the clothes are made of.
The shadda (ّ) appears because this word comes after a word ending in ن or م, making the م sound stronger

steps.m: fine silk (a type of luxurious fabric)
steps.n: This is a borrowed word from Persian (sundus) referring to a specific type of fine, delicate silk fabric. In this ayah, it describes the luxurious clothing of people in Paradise.
The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this word is describing what the green garments (ثِيَابًا خُضْرًا) are made from. It's like saying 'garments of fine silk' in English.

steps.m: thick silk brocade (a type of luxurious fabric)
steps.n: This is a Persian loanword (إستبرق from استبره) that entered Arabic to describe a specific type of thick, luxurious silk brocade woven with gold or silver threads. It's mentioned in descriptions of Paradise's rewards.

steps.m: thick silk brocade (connected to سُندُسٍ - fine silk)
steps.n: The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this word is connected to سُندُسٍ (fine silk) mentioned just before it in the ayah, describing two types of silk garments in Paradise

steps.m: and thick silk brocade (connected to سُندُسٍ - fine silk)
steps.n: The و adds the meaning of 'and'
Added vowel marks to make it mean 'he reclined' in the past tense
Changed to active participle form showing who does the action
Added emphasis by doubling the ت
The م has merged with another letter to make a stronger sound, showing more relaxation

steps.m: those who are reclining (plural, in Paradise)
steps.n: Added masculine plural ending to show this describes multiple people in Paradise who are reclining on thrones (الْأَرَآئِكِ)
Basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'within'. This is a particle word that doesn't come from a root.
Added ها which refers back to جَنَّاتُ (gardens) mentioned earlier in the ayah. This shows where the action of being adorned (يُحَلَّوْنَ) takes place.

Basic preposition showing position above something or resting upon it. In this ayah, it connects the word 'reclining' (مُّتَّكِـِٔينَ) with 'thrones' (الْأَرَآئِكِ) to show where people are resting in Paradise.
Forms the basic noun for the object used for reclining
Changes from single couch (areek) to multiple couches (ara'ik) by rearranging letters in a special plural pattern
Adds the definite article to specify these particular couches mentioned in Paradise

steps.m: on the couches/thrones (in Paradise, following على)
steps.n: Adds genitive case ending because it follows the preposition على (upon/on) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the first vowel to kasra and added sukun on middle letter to make it a word used for praising
Added a shadda (double mark) on the middle letter to show doing something multiple times
Changed to a noun by adding alif after the second letter. This word is what's being praised by نِعْمَ (how excellent) in this ayah.
Added tanween damma to make the noun indefinite
Added ال (the) at the beginning and ُ at the end to show this is the main thing being talked about
The pattern فَعُلَ shows that something naturally has or develops a quality. Here it shows Paradise naturally becoming beautiful.
Adds 'and' to connect this description with the previous blessings of Paradise mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: and it (Paradise) became an excellent (place of rest)
steps.n: The ت makes it feminine to match with Paradise (جنة) which is feminine in Arabic. This concludes the description of the rewards in Paradise.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
The ا and ت together show doing something for one's own comfort or benefit
The مُ at the beginning shows this is a place where the action happens

steps.m: a comfortable resting place (describing how excellent it is)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is describing what is excellent in the phrase 'how excellent is this as a resting place'

steps.m: to strike or give an example

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he hit' to 'you hit!' by adding i- at start and changing middle vowel to make it a command

steps.m: and give (as an example)! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added و (and) at start. In this ayah, it's specifically commanding the Prophet ﷺ to give an example about two men.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound

steps.m: for them (referring to the two men mentioned in مَّثَلًا رَّجُلَيْنِ)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show who the preposition is referring to. This connects to the two men being discussed in the example (مَّثَلًا رَّجُلَيْنِ) that follows.

steps.m: to be like or to give an example

steps.n: Root letters connected with 'a' vowels (fatha) to make the word mean 'example'
Added emphasis by doubling the first letter

steps.m: a clear example (object of وَٱضْرِبْ - strike/give)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being given in the phrase 'give them an example'
The vowels make it into a proper noun meaning 'man'
The doubled ر (shown by ّ) makes the meaning stronger
Added يْنِ at the end to show exactly two men
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding نا changes it from 'he made' to 'we made'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the number 'one'. This refers to one of the two men mentioned in رَّجُلَيْنِ.
Adds the preposition 'for/to'
Changes the last vowel mark to match 'for/to' (li-) at the start
Added pronoun suffix to show possession
Added dual suffix to show it refers to two people
Added vowels and connected the letters to make a past tense verb
Added ة to create a noun meaning a single garden - a place that provides cover with plants

steps.m: two gardens (object of جَعَلْنَا - We made)
steps.n: Changed to show exactly two gardens. This word is what Allah made/gave in the phrase جَعَلْنَا (We made)
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. It's one of the most common words in Arabic.
Added the kasra (ِ) under the م to help with pronunciation

steps.m: from/of (describing what أَعْنٰبٍ 'grapes' are made of)
steps.n: Added the sukun (ْ) on ن to show where to stop. In this ayah, it's used to show what the gardens are made of - gardens of grapes.
Basic noun pattern with kasra-fatha vowels
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple grapes

steps.m: grapes (as part of what's in the gardens)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is part of what's being described in the gardens

steps.m: to surround or encircle something completely

steps.n: Connected the root letters ح-ف-ف with vowel marks (َ) to make the past tense verb 'surrounded'
Added نَا to show 'we' did the action
Added هُمَا to show the action was done to two things (referring to the two gardens mentioned in the ayah)

steps.m: and we surrounded them both (referring to the two gardens)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this action with what came before it in the ayah
Basic root letters were connected with vowels to make the word for palm trees
Added the two dots below (tanwin) to show it's receiving the action
Added the preposition بِ meaning 'with' to show how the palm trees are used in the garden
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Adding نا at the end changes 'he' to 'we' and shows we did this action in the past

steps.m: and we made (connected to جَعَلْنَا before it)
steps.n: Adding وَ at the start connects this with the previous جَعَلْنَا in the ayah, showing this is another thing that was made

steps.m: to be separate or distinct from something else

steps.n: Adding vowels changes it from a root into a usable word meaning 'between'

steps.m: between them both (referring to the two gardens)
steps.n: Added هُمَا at the end to show this is about two things (the two gardens mentioned in the ayah)
Root meaning 'plant' becomes the noun 'crops' by adding marks (fatha-sukun pattern)
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being grown in the garden mentioned in the ayah
Added vowel marks to make the base word that means 'all' or 'whole'

steps.m: both (of the two feminine things) (describes الْجَنَّتَيْنِ)
steps.n: Added تَا to make it mean 'both' instead of 'all', specifically for two feminine things (like the two gardens in this ayah)
Adding vowel marks changes it from a root into a basic past tense verb
Added ة to make it mean 'a covered place' which came to mean 'garden' because gardens were typically enclosed spaces
Added ين at the end to make it mean exactly two gardens (dual form)
Added ال at beginning to mean 'the' and ِ at end to show these gardens belong to كِلْتَا (both)
Changes from basic 'to come' to causative 'to make come/give'

steps.m: she gave (subject of أُكُلَهَا - its produce)
steps.n: Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine singular. In this ayah, this refers to each garden (جَنَّة) giving its produce.
The ُ marks make it a plural noun meaning multiple types
Added هَا which shows this is talking about "its" produce. This word is what was yielded (object) in ءَاتَتْ (yielded).
Added vowel marks to show this makes verbs negative in the past. When لَمْ comes before a present tense verb, it changes its meaning to a negative past tense.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects the negative statement to the previous positive one.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he wronged) to present tense (you/she wrongs)
Basic preposition meaning 'from' with kasra and sukun marks to show correct pronunciation
Added shadda to strengthen pronunciation after assimilation with previous word
Added pronoun هُ (meaning 'it/him') to show what is being referred to. Here it refers back to the produce/fruit (أُكُل) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun. The ء (hamza) at the end comes from the root letter أ.
