steps.n: Changed from action to noun by adding vowel marks. This makes it mean the concept of creation itself rather than the act of creating.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it (the heavens). This shows what was created.

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Adding fatha marks creates the basic past tense verb pattern
Changed from a verb (to rise) into a plural noun (heavens) by adding 'ات' at the end and changing the middle letter to 'ا'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س
Changed to 'the heavens' with a kasra ending because it's receiving the action of witnessing
Added vowel marks to connect the letters into a word
Adding ال makes it specific

steps.m: and the earth (connected to خَلْقَ السَّمٰوٰتِ - creation of the heavens)
steps.n: Adding conjunction و connects this to previous phrase

steps.m: and the earth (object of خَلْقَ - creation)
steps.n: The word gets a kasra (i) ending to show it's connected to 'creation' (خَلْقَ), like saying 'creation of the earth'
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This word negates خَلْقَ (creation) in this ayah.

steps.m: creation (the act or process of creating)
steps.n: Changed from basic meaning to show the action itself as a noun. This is what is being witnessed in the phrase 'I did not make them witness the creation of the heavens and earth'
The fatha (small diagonal line) at the end shows this word is being acted upon - it is what was witnessed
Basic noun form meaning self/soul
Changes the word to mean more than one self/soul

steps.m: their selves (object of خَلْقَ - creation of)
steps.n: Adds هِمْ to show these selves belong to them (a group). This word is being acted upon by خَلْقَ (creation of).
This is a negative particle that negates the verb كُنتُ (I was) that comes after it
Adds 'and' to connect this negation with the previous statement. The full phrase وَمَا كُنتُ means 'and I was not'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Command form of 'to be' - middle letter drops out and vowels change to make it shorter and snappier like a command
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this action in the past

steps.m: to grasp or take hold of something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and merged ت with خ to show someone who does the action. The ِ under خ shows it's happening now or as a regular action.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Adds مُ at the beginning to show who does the action, and adds ِ (kasra) on middle letter with shadda to show intensity

steps.m: those who persistently lead others astray
steps.n: Sound masculine plural ending for multiple doers

steps.m: the ones who persistently lead others astray
steps.n: Adds 'the' (ال) to make it specific about which misleaders we're talking about
Adds َ (fatha) at the end to show this is in the accusative case (object form)
This pattern is used for body parts and physical traits

steps.m: a helper/support (object of كُنتُ مُتَّخِذَ - I was taking)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this word is the object being taken/used. In this ayah, Allah is saying He didn't take the misleaders as helpers/supporters.
Connected the root letters with fatha and sukoon to make the basic noun form
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added fatha at the end to show this is when the action يَقُولُ (he says) happens
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense and changed middle vowels to show it's happening now
Changed the vowel to ُ (dhamma) to match the standard present tense pattern
Added alif after first root letter to form Form III verb pattern
Added plural suffix وا and changed middle vowel to damma
Final weak letter ي drops when plural suffix وا is added; و indicates plurality while ا is for pronunciation
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (to share) into a plural noun meaning 'partners' by adding alif at the end
Changed alif to alif madd for pronunciation
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added ى with fatha to show these are 'my' partners
This is the basic form meaning 'who' or 'which' used to describe someone or something
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people instead of just one

steps.m: those who, the ones who (subject of زَعَمْتُمْ - you claimed)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning and a shadda to make it specific. This word is the subject of زَعَمْتُمْ (you claimed) in this ayah.

steps.m: to claim/assert something without being certain

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added sukun on م to prepare for attaching endings
Added تُمْ to show that multiple people being spoken to did this action in the past

steps.m: to call out to someone or invite them

steps.n: Connected the root letters د-ع-و and added vowels to make past tense 'he'. The و at the end turns into ا because it's at the end of the word.
Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding وْ (waw with sukun) at the end
Added هُمْ at the end to show who was called
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after they were told to call. This connects to the word نَادُوا (call out) that came before in the verse.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of this negation particle that makes verbs past tense
Added فَ at beginning to show this follows from previous events. This connects to يَسْتَجِيبُوا۟ (they responded) showing they did not respond.

steps.m: to answer or respond to something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added است to show seeking or trying to do the action
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added هُ (him/it) to show who the action is for
Changed هُ (him) to هُمْ (them) to show it applies to multiple people

steps.m: to make, to put, to set

steps.n: Adding fathas creates the basic past tense active verb form
Added conjunction prefix
Added نَا (meaning 'We') at the end to change from 'he made' to 'We made'

steps.m: to be separate or clear from one another

steps.n: Adding vowels changes it from a root into a preposition meaning 'between'

steps.m: between them (referring to the false partners mentioned in شُرَكَآءِىَ)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show this refers to 'them' (the false partners that were mentioned)
Changed from basic root to a noun meaning 'place of' with meem prefix and shadda for emphasis

steps.m: a place of destruction (object of جَعَلْنَا)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what is being made/placed between them. This word is what Allah made/placed (object of جَعَلْنَا - We made/placed)
Added fathas to make it mean 'he saw' in the past. This word shows what happened before they thought (فَظَنُّوٓا۟) in this ayah.
Added alif at the end to show this word is affected by something before it
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Adding مُ at the start and changing vowels makes it mean 'someone who commits crimes' (like changing 'steal' to 'thief')
Adding the sound masculine plural ending ون
Adding ال (the) at the start to talk about specific criminals, not just any criminals
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'fire' (which gives light and heat)
Added 'the' (ال) at the start, which made the ن double in sound
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object that the criminals saw in the ayah

steps.m: to think or believe something without being completely sure

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a proper past tense verb
Added وا at the end and merged the double ن into one ن with a shaddah to show many people did the action

steps.m: so/then they thought/believed (connected to رَءَا/saw in previous part)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of what came before (they saw the fire, so they thought). Also added the long vowel mark (ـٓ) and small و (۟) to show proper recitation
Adding the shadda makes it stronger and more emphatic, showing certainty

steps.m: indeed/certainly that they (referring to الْمُجْرِمُونَ - the criminals)
steps.n: Adding هُم shows this refers to 'they/them' - specifically referring back to the criminals (الْمُجْرِمُونَ) mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: to fall into or encounter something

steps.n: Changes from verb (to fall) into noun (one who/what falls)
Added مُ at the start to show 'the one who' does the action

steps.m: one who falls into (something) - nominative case
steps.n: Added dhamma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added و to make the word refer to multiple people instead of just one

steps.m: those who will fall into it (referring to النَّارَ - the Fire)
steps.n: Added ها to show what they will fall into, referring back to the Fire mentioned earlier in the verse
This is a negative particle that makes verbs past tense. It's like adding 'did not' in English.

steps.m: and not (in the past) (connects to فَظَنُّوٓا۟ - and they thought)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous phrase 'and they thought'. It shows this happened after that.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people (they) and changed ending because it comes after لَمْ (not)
This is a preposition meaning 'away from' or 'about'

steps.m: away from it (referring to النَّارَ - the Fire)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to النَّارَ (the Fire) mentioned earlier in the ayah. The ها is feminine because النَّارَ is a feminine word.

steps.m: to turn away or change direction

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added مَ at start and changed vowels to show this is a place where the action happens

steps.m: a place of escape (object of يَجِدُوا)
steps.n: Added tanween fatHa to show this is the object being searched for in the phrase 'they did not find (يَجِدُوا) a place of escape'
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of this word that shows certainty
Added لَ at the beginning to add extra emphasis, like saying 'most certainly' instead of just 'certainly'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Connected the letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on ر shows doing something thoroughly or repeatedly
Doubling the ر shows the action is done thoroughly/repeatedly
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it connects to 'this Quran' to show where Allah explained things.
The ي is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Basic demonstrative pronoun form that points to something nearby
Added alif above ه to show proper pronunciation
Added ا at the end with fatha to show this points to a masculine singular thing (in this case, it points to ٱلْقُرْءَانِ - the Quran)
The root 'read' becomes 'Quran' by adding 'ان' at the end and a hamza (ء) in the middle, making it mean 'that which is read'
Adding ال makes it specifically refer to THE Quran
Adding kasra at the end shows this is the object of a preposition

steps.m: to be close to others, to feel comfortable with people

steps.n: The hamza is dropped and an alif is added after the first letter to make it mean 'people' - those who are close to and comfortable with each other

steps.m: people (being described or owned by something)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is connected to another word that describes or owns it
Adding ال makes it talk about specific people rather than just any people. The ن gets doubled (ّ) because it comes right after ال
Adding لِ at the start shows these people are receiving something or something is being done for them

steps.m: from/of (connects كُلِّ to what comes before it)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing where something starts or what it belongs to. Here it connects 'every' (كُلِّ) to what was mentioned before about the Quran.
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
The kasra (ِ) shows this word is describing the 'example' (مَثَلٍ) that comes after it in the ayah
Added vowels to make a noun meaning 'example' or 'comparison'
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this is any general example, not a specific one. This word is part of the phrase 'from every example' (مِن كُلِّ مَثَلٍ) in this ayah.

steps.m: he/it was (past tense verb in this ayah describing الْإِنسٰنُ - mankind)
steps.n: Added fatha marks to show it's past tense. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the middle of the word.

steps.m: and he/it was (connecting to the previous statement about examples in the Quran)
steps.n: Adds و with fatha at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before it
Root meaning 'to be social' becomes a noun meaning 'human' by adding i-n at start and aa-n at end

steps.m: the human being (subject of كَانَ - was)
steps.n: Added 'the' at beginning to make it specific. The ُ at the end shows this is doing the action of كَانَ (was) in this verse
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to change from 'became abundant' to 'more than'. Like saying something is bigger or smaller than something else. Here it tells us humans argue more than anything else.

The root letters ش-ي-أ form the common noun شَيْء meaning 'thing'. The vowels (fatha then sukun) help change it from a verb meaning 'to want' into a noun meaning 'thing'. It's connected to the word 'most' (أَكْثَرَ) in the sentence.
Adding the -in ending shows this word is 'of a thing' (like saying 'of' in English)
The tanwin kasra (ٍ) was already there in both words - the only change is spelling شَيْءٍ as شَىْءٍ which is just a writing variation with no change in meaning or grammar

steps.m: to argue back and forth with someone

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fathas to make it into a noun showing the act of arguing

steps.m: in argument/dispute (describing كَانَ أَكْثَرَ - was most)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how humans were 'most' in terms of arguing. This connects to كَانَ أَكْثَرَ (was most) in the ayah.
Basic particle that can either negate something or ask a question
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before. In this ayah, ما negates the verb مَنَعَ (prevented).

steps.m: he prevented (subject of prevented the people from believing)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is connected to 'the people' (الناس) who are being prevented.
Changed the root into a noun for people by adding a long 'aa' sound
Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the ن letter for pronunciation
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of the verb مَنَعَ (prevented)
This is a particle that introduces what prevented people from believing. It connects to the verb يُؤْمِنُوا (they believe) to show what action was prevented.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and putting a ؤ in the middle
Added plural marker وا with elongation mark to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: when (in the past, referring to جَآءَهُمُ - came to them)
steps.n: This is a particle that shows something happened at a specific time in the past. Here it connects to when guidance came to them.
Basic past tense verb pattern with fatha marks
Added هُمُ at the end to show who the action was done to - them (a group of people)

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the first vowel to damma to make it passive (something was done to it)
Added 'ال' (the) to make it 'the guidance' instead of just 'guidance', with a special long ending
Added basic past tense vowels
Added است at beginning to show seeking or requesting forgiveness
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Added و at beginning to connect with previous phrase

steps.m: Lord (the one who cares for everything)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does this caring completely and perfectly

steps.m: their Lord (the one being asked for forgiveness in وَيَسْتَغْفِرُوا)
steps.n: Adding هُم shows this Lord belongs to or is connected to them. This word connects to وَيَسْتَغْفِرُوا (and they ask forgiveness) showing who they're asking forgiveness from.

steps.m: except (introduces what comes after أَن تَأْتِيَهُمْ as an exception)
steps.n: Starting from the negative particle لا, we add إِ at the beginning and double the ل to create the exception particle. This changes the meaning from negation to exception.
The regular alif is written as a long alif (آ) at the end. This is just a writing variation and doesn't change the meaning or pronunciation.

This is a connecting particle that links two parts of the sentence. Here it connects what prevented people (مَنَعَ) with what they were prevented from doing (believing). It's like saying 'prevented them from believing' in English.
Added يَ at the beginning to show this is happening in the present/future
Changed يَ to تَ because the subject سُنَّةُ (way/tradition) is feminine

steps.m: it comes to them (سُنَّةُ comes to them)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being come to (them)
Added ُ on first letter and doubled the middle letter to show something established

steps.m: a specific established way or practice
steps.n: Adding ة (ta marboota) at the end makes it mean 'a specific established practice' instead of just 'established way' in general

steps.m: the established way (subject of تَأْتِيَهُمْ - comes to them)
steps.n: The ُ shows it's the subject. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb تَأْتِيَهُمْ (comes to them).

steps.m: to be first or come before something

steps.n: Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to make it mean 'first' instead of just the idea of being first
Added 'the' to make it specific
Added ين to make it refer to many people who were first

steps.m: the first ones (plural, acted upon by تَأْتِيَهُمْ)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb تَأْتِيَهُمْ (comes to them) in this ayah
This is a basic conjunction that gives a choice between two alternatives. In this ayah, it connects two possibilities: that something comes to them or that punishment comes to them.
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now or in the future. Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce it correctly.

steps.m: comes to them (object of أَن in the ayah)
steps.n: Added هُمُ at the end to show who receives the action. This word comes after أَن (that) in the ayah, showing it's part of what was prevented.
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun meaning 'punishment'. This word is what's doing the action in يَأْتِيَهُمُ (comes to them).
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific punishment, while ُ shows this word is doing the action in the sentence (it's what 'comes to them')

steps.m: to face or be in front of something

steps.n: Adding the vowel marks ُ changes this into a plural noun meaning 'fronts'

steps.m: face to face, directly (describes how العَذَابُ comes)
steps.n: Adding tanwin alif makes it describe how something happens - in this case, how the punishment (العَذَابُ) comes to people: directly, face to face
This is a basic particle that can mean either 'what' or 'not' depending on context. Here it means 'what' because it's referring to the warnings (أُنذِرُوا) that were given.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

Added نُ at start to show 'we' are doing it, changed vowels to show present tense, and added ُ at end to show it's the subject. This word connects to 'المرسلين' (the messengers) which comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to send or deliver a message

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who is sent with a message
steps.n: Added مُ at start to show someone receiving the action (like 'sent one'), similar to adding '-ed' in English

steps.m: those who are sent with messages
steps.n: Added ين to make it plural, showing multiple messengers
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and َ to show it's the object of the verb نُرْسِلُ (We send) in this ayah
This is a fundamental particle that shows exception. The kasra (ِ) under the إ helps connect it to what follows.
Adding the shadda (ّ) and alif (ا) strengthens the exception meaning and makes it a complete word. This word is used to exclude what comes after it from what came before it.

steps.m: one who gives good news (subject of نُرْسِلُ)
steps.n: Adding مُ at beginning and ِ on middle root letter shows who does the action. This word is one of the objects being sent in the phrase 'We do not send...'

steps.m: one who regularly gives good news
steps.n: Adding shadda makes it mean someone who does this as their main job or role

steps.m: those who regularly give good news (plural, object of نُرْسِلُ)
steps.n: The ين ending makes it refer to many people who give good news. This group is what is being sent in the phrase 'We do not send...'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to show someone who warns as their role or characteristic
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people who warn

steps.m: and warners (connected to مُبَشِّرِينَ - bearers of good news)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect it with مُبَشِّرِينَ (bearers of good news) in the verse, showing these are two roles of the messengers

steps.m: to argue or debate with someone
to argue back and forth with someone
steps.n: The ا shows two people are arguing with each other

steps.m: he argues back and forth (happening now)
steps.n: Adding يُ at the start changes it from past tense (he argued) to present tense (he argues now)

steps.m: he argues back and forth (happening now)
steps.n: The ُ at the end shows 'he' is doing the action

steps.m: and he argues back and forth (with the disbelievers)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this with the previous phrase. This word describes what the disbelievers do, as mentioned in the next part of the verse.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ال with a shadda makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to multiple people. This word connects to كَفَرُوا۟ (they disbelieved) in this ayah.

steps.m: to reject, deny, or cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb. This word shows who is doing the arguing (يُجٰدِلُ) in this verse.
Added وا to show multiple people did this action. The ُ before وا is needed for proper pronunciation.
Added alif after first letter and kasra after third letter to make it a noun describing something false
Added ال to specify a particular falsehood

steps.m: with falsehood (used to argue against الحَقَّ)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to show they use falsehood as a tool in their arguing. This word is connected to الحَقَّ (the truth) which they are trying to disprove.
Added ا after ب to make it follow the active participle pattern, showing something that is actively false/wrong
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changes it to present tense and shows the action is being done to something
Adds وا to make the verb plural masculine, changing from singular 'he refutes' to plural 'they refute'

steps.m: in order that they disprove (the truth)
steps.n: Adds purpose/intent meaning. In this ayah, they are trying to disprove the truth (الْحَقَّ) using falsehood.
Added kasra (ِ) to show how the letter is pronounced
Added the pronoun هِ (it) to show what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to الْبٰطِلِ (falsehood) which the disbelievers use to argue.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters
The middle letter is doubled to add emphasis to the meaning

steps.m: the truth (that they try to reject)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to the specific truth that the disbelievers are trying to reject with falsehood (بِٱلْبٰطِلِ)

steps.m: the truth (that they try to reject with falsehood)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this truth is being acted upon - they are trying to reject it using falsehood as mentioned in the ayah (بِٱلْبٰطِلِ لِيُدْحِضُوا۟ بِهِ)

steps.m: to grasp or take hold of

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The ت combines with the first letter to make a shadda, showing more intensity in the taking/adopting action
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: and they took for themselves/adopted (referring to ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect with previous phrase. This word refers back to 'those who disbelieved' mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: Adding ة changes it from a verb root into a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Added the feminine plural ending ات to show multiple signs
Added kasra at the end to show it's being used after a preposition or as a possessive
Added ي at the end to show possession - 'my signs'
Different spelling of same word - ى is another way to write ي at the end
This is a question word that can also mean 'that which'. It's a built-in word that doesn't come from a root. In this ayah, it's used with the meaning 'that which' to refer to what was warned about.
Added conjunction 'and' at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he warned' to 'was warned' by adding أُ at start and putting a kasra ( ِ ) in middle - this makes it passive
Added وا to show multiple people were warned
The ُ marks change this from a verb into a noun meaning the act of mocking
The ًا ending shows this word is being used as an object in the sentence
Changed hamza (ء) to waw (و) - both spellings mean the same thing and show this word is being used as the object (what is being mocked)
This is a combination of two particles: و (and) + من (who/whoever)
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: more unfair (than others mentioned in وَمَنْ)
steps.n: The أَ at the start and ُ at the end make this word mean 'more' of something. Here it means 'more unfair' than the people mentioned before in وَمَنْ (and who)
Starting with the two separate words: مِن (from) and مَن (whoever)

When مِن and مَن come together, the ن from مِن combines with the م from مَن, shown by the ّ (shadda). In this context, مِن means 'than' because it's used for comparison after أَظْلَمُ (more wrong/unjust).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice by adding damma at start, kasra with shadda in middle, and fatha at end. This shows the person was reminded by something (in this case, by his Lord's signs)
Basic root meaning related to signs and clarity
The root letters combine with a long 'aa' sound (آ) and end with 'ah' (ة) to make a noun meaning 'sign'
Makes the word plural to show multiple signs

steps.m: by/with the signs (of رَبِّهِ - his Lord)
steps.n: Adds بِ at the beginning to mean 'by/with' and ِ at the end because it's connected to 'his Lord' after it

steps.m: to care for, raise, nurture to completion
steps.n: Root meaning related to nurturing and development
The root ر-ب-ب becomes رَبِّ (Lord) and the kasra at the end shows it belongs to something else - like saying 'Lord of'
The هِ at the end shows this belongs to him. The ۦ is just a pronunciation mark.

steps.m: to turn aside or present something broadly
steps.n: Root meaning related to width or presenting
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added hamza with fatha at start and sukun on first root letter to show deliberate turning away. This verb connects to آيَات (signs) in the ayah through عَنْهَا (from them) that follows it.

steps.m: and so he turned away (from آيَات)
steps.n: Added فَ at start to show this happened as a result of what came before. This connects to being reminded of Allah's signs in the previous part of the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows movement away from something or turning away from something
Added the feminine pronoun هَا to show what is being turned away from. In this ayah, it refers back to the signs/verses (آيَات) mentioned earlier.
Basic root meaning related to forgetting
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb. The kasra on the middle letter shows this is about a mental state or feeling.
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this with the previous action in the verse (فَأَعْرَضَ - and he turned away)

steps.m: what (refers to قَدَّمَتْ - what his hands sent forth)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb after it (قَدَّمَتْ). It's like saying 'what' when asking about something that happened.
Added vowel marks and doubled middle letter (shadda) to show he did it with extra force/emphasis

steps.m: she put forward (intensively) (subject of يَدَاهُ - her hands)
steps.n: Added ت with sukun at the end to show a female did this action. This word connects to يَدَاهُ (her hands) which comes right after it.
Added fatha to create the basic noun form
Added alif at end to make it dual (showing two hands)
Adding possessive pronoun suffix هُ meaning 'his'. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb قَدَّمَتْ (sent forth) in this ayah.
This is a particle that emphasizes the verb جَعَلْنَا (we made/placed) that comes after it
Added ا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action in جَعَلْنَا (we made/placed)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb meaning 'he made/put'
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. The last fatha becomes sukun before نَا.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else. Here it connects to قُلُوبِهِمْ (their hearts) to show what has something upon it.
Added an alif symbol (ٰ) after the ى which helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning
Changed the internal structure to make it plural by adding damma vowels and و, which is a common pattern for making plurals of body parts and important objects
Added هِمْ at the end to show these hearts belong to them (referring back to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the verse)
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show something that does the covering
Added أَ at start and ة at end to make it plural, with ِ after first letter to match the plural pattern
Added tanween (ً) to show this is the object of جَعَلْنَا (We made/placed)

steps.m: that (connects to يَفْقَهُوهُ - they understand it)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence together. Here it connects to the verb يَفْقَهُوهُ (they understand it), showing what they are prevented from doing.
Adding vowels to make it a complete past tense verb meaning 'to understand'
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and adjusting vowels
Changed from singular 'he understands' to plural 'they understand' by adding و and ا at the end
Added هُ to show what is being understood
Basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'within'
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing
Added elongation mark (ٓ) to show the long vowel sound
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'
Changes from verb 'to permit/hear' to the noun 'ear' by adding damma vowels
Changed to broken plural form showing multiple ears

steps.m: their ears (referring to those who turn away from Allah's signs)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show possession by the group mentioned in the ayah who turn away from Allah's signs

steps.m: to be heavy or blocked (especially about hearing)

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to be heavy' into noun meaning 'heaviness/deafness'
Added ًا to show this is what's being placed. In this ayah, Allah is saying He placed deafness in their ears.
Added fatha to show how the word is pronounced

steps.m: and if (connects to تَدْعُهُمْ - you call them)
steps.n: Added إِن (if) to create a conditional phrase. This combination introduces a condition about what happens if 'you call them' (تَدْعُهُمْ)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Adding تَ changes it from 'he called' to 'you/she call(s)'
Adding 'them' (هم) to the end of 'you call' to make 'you call them'
This is a preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to ٱلْهُدَىٰ (guidance) to show 'towards guidance'

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he guided' in the past tense
Changed the first vowel to damma to show something was guided by someone else
Added 'ال' (the) to make it 'the guidance' instead of just 'guidance', with a special long ending
Basic connecting particle that shows what happens next or as a result

steps.m: so [they] will never (connected to يَهْتَدُوٓا۟ - be guided)
steps.n: Added لَن which is used to strongly negate something in the future. Here it connects to the next word يَهْتَدُوٓا۟ (be guided) to say they will never be guided.

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Added vowels to make past tense verb meaning 'he guided'
Added pattern that shows doing something to oneself
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing ending to ي
Added plural marker to show many people doing the action
This is a basic particle that shows time or consequence

steps.m: then, in that case (showing consequence of فَلَن يَهْتَدُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Adding ًا makes this word show a result or consequence. Here it shows the result of 'they will never be guided'
Adding ًا makes it mean 'forever' as an adverb
Doubling of middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and sustains consistently
The ُ shows this word is doing something in the sentence - it's being described by الغفور (the Most Forgiving)
Added كَ to show this Nurturer belongs to or relates to you. This word is being described by الغفور (the Most Forgiving) in the ayah.

steps.m: and your Lord (who is described as Most Forgiving)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to what came before. The whole phrase tells us about Allah who nurtures and sustains us, and is being described as Most Forgiving (الغفور) in the next word.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Pattern فَعُول indicates someone/something that does the action extensively

steps.m: The Most Forgiving (describing رَبُّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: The word gained 'Al' (ال) at the start to mean 'The' Most Forgiving, making it a title for Allah (your Lord) instead of just the quality of being forgiving
This is a special word that shows ownership or possession. Here it connects 'the Most Forgiving' (الْغَفُورُ) with 'mercy' (الرَّحْمَةِ) to mean 'possessor of mercy'.
Changes from verb 'to show mercy' into the noun 'mercy'
Added ة to make it a specific instance of mercy
Added kasra to show this mercy belongs to or is connected to ذُو (possessor of)
Added ال to make 'mercy' specific/definite

steps.m: if (introducing an impossible situation in يُؤَاخِذُهُم)
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces a hypothetical situation that didn't happen. In this ayah, it's connected to يُؤَاخِذُهُم (He would take them to task), showing what would have happened, but didn't.

steps.m: to grasp or take hold of something

steps.n: Added يُ at start for 'he' in present tense, and extra letters in middle changed the meaning from 'grasp' to 'hold accountable'
Added هُم at the end to show who is being held accountable
Starting with the basic preposition that shows cause or means
Added kasra to show proper pronunciation

steps.m: because of what (they did in كَسَبُوا)
steps.n: Added مَا which means 'what' to specify what is being referred to. This connects to the word كَسَبُوا (they earned/did) that comes right after it in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This is the basic past tense verb pattern.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action, with a small mark (۟) on top to show proper recitation

steps.m: to hurry or do something quickly

steps.n: Basic root letters got vowels to make 'he hurried' in past tense
Adding shadda makes the action stronger and shows someone is making something else happen

steps.m: he would surely hasten (the punishment mentioned in لَهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابَ)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to show this is the result of a condition (mentioned with لَوْ earlier in the verse)
Added fatha to show this is the preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'

steps.m: for them, to them (connected to ٱلْعَذَابَ - the punishment)
steps.n: Added هُمُ which means 'them'. The ُ (damma) at the end helps connect it to the next word ٱلْعَذَابَ (the punishment).
Changed from action to the result of that action (like changing 'punish' to 'punishment')
The َ shows this word is receiving the action of hastening in لَعَجَّلَ (would hasten)
ٱلْ makes it refer to the specific punishment being discussed

steps.m: rather/but rather (introduces a correction to what was mentioned before)
steps.n: This is a particle that changes the direction of the discussion. In this ayah, it transitions from discussing Allah's mercy to emphasizing that these people still have an appointed time for punishment.
Added fatha to show the basic vowel sound
Added shadda to show emphasis, making it mean 'surely/definitely'
Added هُم at the end to show who it's for (them)

steps.m: to promise or set a time/place to meet

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning and ِ after ع to make it mean 'place/time where something happens'
Added shadda on م and tanween dammah at the end to show this is any appointed time/place, not a specific one
Basic negative particle that makes verbs after it negative in the future tense. Like saying 'will not' in English.

steps.m: will never, absolutely not (negates يَجِدُوا۟ 'they will find')
steps.n: Adding shadda (doubled L sound) makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic. In this ayah, it strongly negates that 'they will find' (يَجِدُوا۟) any escape.
Added يَ at the start to show 'he' doing the action now, vowels connect the letters
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is happening in the present
Added و to show multiple people are doing the action
Added ا to complete the plural ending وا that shows multiple males doing the action
This is a basic particle that shows where something starts from, comes from, or is made from. In this ayah, it's used with دُونِهِۦ to mean 'from other than Him'

steps.m: to be below or beside something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'below/without'
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because it follows the word مِن (from/of)
Added هِ with an elongation mark (ۦ) to show it means 'him/it'

steps.m: to take shelter or find refuge

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning to show where something happens, like how 'sleep' becomes 'bedroom' (a place for sleeping)

steps.m: a place of refuge (object of يَجِدُوا/they will find)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what they will find (the object). In the ayah, Allah is saying they won't find any refuge besides their appointed time.
This is a demonstrative word used to point to things that are far away

steps.m: and that/those (pointing to the towns mentioned after)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'. This word points to القُرَىٰٓ (the towns) that are mentioned right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to settle or gather in a place

steps.n: Changes from base word to plural noun form
Added ال to make it specific and added alif maksura (ى) at the end. This word is what was destroyed in the phrase 'We destroyed them'
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning makes it mean 'cause something to happen' instead of just 'happen'
Adding نا changes it from 'he destroyed' to 'we destroyed'
Adding هُمْ shows who received the action ('them'). The alif in نا changes to ٰ when هم is added.
This letter adds emphasis to what follows
Adding مَّا changes this into a word that connects two events in time. In this ayah, it connects to the word ظَلَمُوا۟ (they wronged), showing when something happened.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb. This word describes an action that happened before أَهْلَكْنٰهُمْ (We destroyed them).

steps.m: they wronged (referring to the people of those towns)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This refers to the people of the towns (ٱلْقُرَىٰٓ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding نَا changes 'he' to 'we' and shows we did the action
Adding وَ connects this to the previous statement, like saying 'and'
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding مَ at the start and changing the vowels makes it mean where or when something happens
Adding هِم at the end shows it belongs to them
Adding لِ shows purpose - this is connected to جَعَلْنَا (We made) in the ayah, showing what was made/set

steps.m: to promise or set an appointment

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to promise' into noun meaning 'meeting place/time'
Adds tanween fatHa to show this is the object of the sentence
This is a time particle that shows when something happened in the past. Here it introduces when Moses spoke to his young companion.

steps.m: and when (connecting to قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: The و connects this story to what came before it in the Quran, showing this is another event being narrated
Added fathas to make it past tense. This is the theoretical form before the weak letter change.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is very common in hollow verbs

steps.m: Moses (the subject of قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: This is a proper noun of non-Arabic origin, believed to come from the Egyptian word meaning 'child' or 'son'. In this ayah, Moses is speaking to his young companion.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the noun form meaning 'young man' or 'servant'
Added the alif maksura (ى) at the end to show this is a noun
Added هُ to show possession - this young man belongs to Musa (Moses)
Added لِ at the beginning to show direction/purpose - Musa is speaking to his servant
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation. This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it.
The alif is lengthened to add emphasis to the negation. This emphasizes Musa's strong determination when speaking to his young companion.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense form
Changed to first person present/future tense. The word is negated by لَآ before it, making the complete meaning 'I will not leave'
This is a primitive particle that shows a limit or endpoint. It has no root.
Added shadda on ت to emphasize the meaning
Added ىٰٓ at the end to connect it to the next word أَبْلُغَ (I reach). This shows that 'until' is leading to a goal or endpoint.

steps.m: to reach or arrive at a destination

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning to show 'I' am doing the action. Changed vowels to show this is happening after 'until' (حَتَّىٰٓ)

steps.m: to join or bring things together

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added مَ at the beginning to show where something happens. In this ayah, it refers to where two seas come together.

steps.m: to split, cut through (like water through land)

steps.n: Added fatha and sukun to make it into a noun meaning 'sea', which comes from how water splits or cuts through land
The َيْنِ ending shows this means exactly two seas

steps.m: the two seas (possessed by مَجْمَع/meeting place)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific. This word is part of the phrase مَجْمَعَ ٱلْبَحْرَيْنِ (meeting place of the two seas), where it is owned by the word مَجْمَع (meeting place)

This is a basic conjunction particle that connects two options. Here it connects 'I will not cease' with 'or I will spend' to show Musa's determination to either reach his goal or spend a long time trying.
Added fatha on alif and sukun on waw to show correct pronunciation
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense meaning
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from third person (he) to first person (I) by replacing يَ with أَ
Added fatha at end to show this is the subjunctive mood (used after 'that', 'so that', etc.)
Adding the double damma pattern creates a noun meaning an extended period of time

steps.m: ages/a very long time (as object of أَمْضِىَ - I will spend)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action of the verb أَمْضِىَ (I will spend)

steps.m: when (connects to بَلَغَا - they both reached)
steps.n: This is a time particle that shows when something happened. It connects to the verb بَلَغَا (they both reached) to show when that action occurred.

steps.m: Then when (connects to بَلَغَا - they both reached)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after something else. It's like saying 'then' or 'so' in English to connect events in order.

steps.m: to reach or arrive at a destination

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha marks. This word is connected to نَسِيَا (they forgot) in this ayah, showing what happened before they forgot.
The alif at the end shows this refers to two people (Musa and his companion) who reached somewhere together

steps.m: to gather or join things together

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Added مَ at the beginning to show this is a place where gathering happens. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) to show it's a place noun.
Forms the noun 'between' from the root meaning 'to separate'. This word is in the genitive case because it is possessed by مجمع (meeting place), forming the phrase 'meeting place between them'.
The word gets a kasra (ee sound) at the end because it comes after another word
Added هِ to show possession or connection to a single masculine thing

steps.m: between them both (referring to موسى and فتى)
steps.n: Added ما to show this refers to exactly two people (Moses and his young companion)

steps.m: to forget or leave something behind

steps.n: Added vowels to make past tense. This word connects to فَٱتَّخَذَ (then he took) in the ayah, showing these actions happened one after another.

steps.m: they both forgot (referring to موسى and فَتَى)
steps.n: The alif at the end shows this is about two people (Moses and his young companion) doing the action together
Adding the damma (ُ) vowel mark turns the root into a proper noun meaning 'fish'
Added fatha to show this is the object being acted upon
Added هُمَا to show the fish belongs to both of them
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
The ت combines with first letter to show doing something with effort/purpose

steps.m: so he took for himself (connected to نَسِيَا - they forgot)
steps.n: The فَ shows this action happened as a result of them forgetting their fish

steps.m: to make a way or path

steps.n: Adds vowel marks to show this is describing a path or way
Adding ي makes this a permanent path/way rather than temporary condition
Added هُ to show possession and َ to show this is the object of the verb اتَّخَذَ (took/made)
This is a preposition that shows where something is located. In this ayah, it connects 'their fish' (حُوتَهُمَا) with 'the sea' (ٱلْبَحْرِ) to show where the fish went.

steps.m: to be vast like a sea/ocean

steps.n: Added vowels to make the basic noun meaning 'sea'
Added ال to make it specific ('the sea') and kasra at the end because it follows the word في (in)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic noun
Added tanwin alif to show this is the object being acted upon. The fish took this tunnel as its path through the sea.
This is a basic time particle that shows when something happened. It often connects two events where one leads to another.

steps.m: Then when (connects to جَاوَزَا - they passed)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened right after something else. In this ayah, it connects to what happens when they passed by.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding alif makes the verb more intense, meaning to really pass by or go beyond something
Adding alif at the end shows two people did the action together (referring to Moses and his young companion in the story)
Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows someone (he) did the action in the past.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
Changed from root letters to a noun meaning 'young man' by adding vowel marks
Added لِ (li) meaning 'to/for' at the start to show who is being spoken to

steps.m: to his young man (who was with موسى)
steps.n: Added هُ to show possession - this refers to the young man who was traveling with Moses
Changes into a command form telling someone to bring something

steps.m: bring to us! (command to bring something to the speaker and companions)
steps.n: Adds 'us' as the recipients of the bringing action. In this ayah, Musa is asking his servant to bring their food.
Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding vowels
Changes from action to noun meaning the morning meal
The word is now in accusative case, showing it's the object of a verb
Added نَا at the end to show possession
This is a basic emphasis word that shows something definitely happened
Adding لَ at the beginning makes the emphasis even stronger. This extra-strong emphasis connects to the verb لَقِينَا (we met/found) in this ayah, showing they definitely experienced something.
Adding vowel marks to show this is a past tense verb meaning 'he met'

steps.m: we met/encountered (in this ayah, describing our journey)
steps.n: Adding نَا changes the subject from 'he' to 'we'. In this ayah, it's used when Musa and his companion are talking about their journey.
This is a simple preposition that connects to the word after it (سَفَرِنَا) to show where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it's specifically talking about their journey.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun meaning 'journey'
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the preposition مِن which requires this case ending
Added نَا at the end to show the journey belongs to 'us'
This is the basic attention-drawing part that starts many demonstrative pronouns
The alif is replaced with a small alif above to show it's a quick attention marker
Adding ذَ makes it specifically point to something nearby
The final alif shows this is for masculine singular things

steps.m: to set up, establish, or cause fatigue

steps.n: Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) between the root letters to make a past tense verb meaning 'he became tired'

steps.m: tiredness/exhaustion (as a state they found in سَفَرِنَا/our journey)
steps.n: Changed from 'he became tired' (verb) to 'tiredness' (noun) by adding tanween at the end
Basic root letters got vowels added to make it past tense 'he said'
The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb 'he saw' by adding the vowel marks َ (fatha)
Added ت at the end to show 'you' did the action
Added أَ at the beginning to make it a question
This is a basic word that shows when something happened in the past. It connects to the next part of the story about when they took shelter at the rock.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. The آ is just a written form of ا that appears at the end of words.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. Here it connects to 'the rock' (الصَّخْرَةِ) to show where they went.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form basic noun
Adding ة at the end makes it mean 'one rock' instead of just 'rock'
Adding the definite article ال makes the noun definite
The kasra ending is added because this word comes after the preposition إِلَى (to/towards) in the ayah
Starting with the emphatic particle إِنَّ which adds emphasis to what follows
Adding فَ at the beginning shows this follows from what was said before

steps.m: so/then indeed I (subject of نَسِيتُ - I forgot)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this is about me (I). This word connects to نَسِيتُ (I forgot) which comes right after it in the ayah.
Root letters got vowels added to make past tense 'he forgot'
Changed from 'he forgot' to 'I forgot' by adding تُ at the end. This word appears in the story of Musa's young companion explaining that he forgot about the fish.
Connected the letters and added damma to create the basic noun form
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific fish that was mentioned before

steps.m: the fish (object of نَسِيتُ - I forgot)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this is what was forgotten. It's the object of the verb نَسِيتُ (I forgot)
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation of the basic particle ما which can mean either 'not' or 'what' depending on context
Added alif with madda (آ) to show long vowel sound. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: and not/and what (connects to نَسِيتُ - I forgot)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. Here it connects to the word نَسِيتُ (I forgot), showing what else the speaker is saying about forgetting.
Basic past tense verb meaning 'he forgot'. The root letters get vowels to make it a complete word.
Adding أ at start changes meaning from 'forgot' to 'made forget'
Added نِي at the end to show who was made to forget (me)

steps.m: he made me forget it (referring to الْحُوتَ/the fish)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show what was forgotten (the fish mentioned earlier)
This is a particle (not derived from a root) used to exclude something from what came before it
Added kasra under إ to show correct pronunciation
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly

steps.m: to be far away from good/truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding ي changes it from a basic 3-letter verb to a 4-letter verb meaning 'to act like/become something'
Adding ا and ن to شَيْطَ turns a verb (acting like a devil) into a noun meaning 'devil' or 'one who is very rebellious'
Adding ٱل makes it refer to the specific devil, and causes the ش to get a shadda
Adding ُ shows this word is doing the action in the sentence. The ا becomes ٰ when the ُ is added.

steps.m: that (connects to أَذْكُرَهُ - I remember it)
steps.n: Added fatha on أ and sukun on ن. This particle connects to the verb أَذْكُرَهُ (I remember it) to show what was forgotten. It's like saying 'I forgot that I should remember it.'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed to first person present tense by adding أ at start and changing vowels
Changed to subjunctive mood by adding fatha at the end
Added هُ to show what is being remembered (it refers to the fish mentioned earlier)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added ت with shadda and changed first vowel to kasra to show reflexive meaning (doing something for oneself)

steps.m: and he took for himself (subject: الحوت - the fish)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect to previous phrase, with hamzat wasl (ٱ) replacing regular alif

steps.m: to make a path or way

steps.n: Adds vowel marks to show this is describing a path or way
Added ي to make it mean a permanent path/way instead of a temporary one
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb اتخذ (took/made)

steps.m: its way (being acted upon by اتخذ)
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end to show possession (its)
Basic preposition showing location or containment. Here it shows where the path (سَبِيلَهُۥ) was - in the sea.
The ي changes to ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Adding definite article to make the noun definite
The kasra ending shows this word comes after the word فِي (in) in the ayah
Changes from verb 'to be amazed' into the noun 'amazement'

steps.m: in an amazing way (describes how the fish took its path)
steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) to make it describe how something happened. This word describes how the fish took its path into the sea in verse 18:63

steps.m: he said (subject of ذٰلِكَ مَا كُنَّا نَبْغِ)
steps.n: Added fathas to make past tense verb. This word is the main verb that introduces what he said.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
The basic demonstrative pronoun ذا (this) gets an alif above it (ذٰ) to show distance
Added لِ to strengthen the meaning of distance

steps.m: that (in response to قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to complete the demonstrative pronoun. This word is used in response to what was said (قَالَ)

steps.m: that which (referring to what they were seeking)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun connecting to كُنَّا نَبْغِ (we were seeking). It refers back to what they were looking for.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to first person plural (we) by adding ا and other changes
Root letters got vowels (fat-ha) to make past tense verb meaning 'he sought'
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' and changed vowels to show present tense
Removed the final ي since it's not needed in this form
Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make the past tense verb 'he returned'
Added اِ and ت to show doing the action by oneself
Combined the double د into دّ with shadda for easier pronunciation
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of something before it

steps.m: so they both returned by themselves
steps.n: Added ا at the end to show two people (Musa and his companion) did this action together
Basic preposition showing something is on top of or above something else
Added small alif (ٰ) to show the long 'aa' sound is maintained at the end
Added vertical fatḥa (ٓ) to show connection to the next word (ءَاثَارِهِمَا - their footsteps)

steps.m: to leave a trace or mark behind

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'trace/mark' by adding the vowel marks a-a
Changed to plural by adding alif at start and alif after tha (ث) - this is a broken plural pattern
Added kasra at end to show this word is the object of a preposition

steps.m: to follow tracks or traces repeatedly
steps.n: The fathas show this is a verbal noun meaning 'the act of following'

steps.m: following of tracks (as the way they went back)
steps.n: Added tanween (double line) at the end to show this is describing how they went back, connecting to 'they returned' in the verse

steps.m: to find or come across something

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha vowels. This word shows who did the finding of عَبْدًا (a servant).

steps.m: they both found (referring to Moses and his companion)
steps.n: Adding alif at the end shows two people (Moses and his companion) did the action together

steps.m: and so they both found (connecting to their previous journey)
steps.n: Adding فَ at beginning shows this happened as a result of their journey mentioned before
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun meaning someone who serves

steps.m: a servant (object of فَوَجَدَا - they both found)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being found in the phrase 'they both found a servant'
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or its source. Here it connects 'servant' to 'of our servants'
The م gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after another word ending in a vowel
Connected the root letters together to form the singular noun for one who serves/worships
Changes from single servant to many servants by adding an alif and changing the vowels
Added نَا at the end to show possession ('our'). This word follows the preposition مِنْ (from/of) in the ayah.
Added ا after نَا to extend the final sound. This is just a pronunciation variation.
Changed from 'come' to 'give/bring' by adding an alif at the start - makes it mean 'causing something to happen'
Changed the ending to prepare for attachments
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added هُ to show who received the giving. This person is the object that they found in the verse.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together. The fatha on ر and sukun on ح make this into a noun pattern.
Adding ة at the end turns the general idea of mercy into one specific act of mercy

steps.m: a mercy (that was given to him)
steps.n: The tanween (ً) at the end shows this is describing what kind of thing was given to him in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows the starting point of something

steps.m: from (shows starting point or source, with emphasis)
steps.n: The letter م gets a shadda (doubling mark) because it comes after another word in the ayah. This makes the word stronger when pronounced.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it a preposition. The kasra (ِ) under ع and sukun (ْ) on ن are essential parts of this common preposition.
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because this word comes after the preposition مِنْ which requires this ending

steps.m: with us, in our possession (after مِنْ)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show this belongs to 'us' or is with 'us'. This word appears after مِنْ (from) in the ayah.
This form turns the action of knowing into the noun knowledge
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding a shadda (doubling mark) on the ل changes the meaning from 'know' to 'teach'
Adding نا shows that 'we' did the teaching
Adding هُ shows who received the teaching
Adding وَ connects this word to the previous statement
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it connects to عِندِنَا (our presence) to show the mercy and knowledge came from Allah's presence.
This word is usually used to mean 'from the presence of'
The vowels make it into a word meaning 'from near/presence'
The doubled letter at the end makes the meaning stronger
Added نَّا at the end to show this is from Allah's presence
Changed the verb 'to know' into the noun 'knowledge' by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter

steps.m: knowledge (object of عَلَّمْنٰهُ - we taught him)
steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this is the thing being taught. This word tells us what Allah taught him in the phrase 'we taught him knowledge'.
Changed the root letters ق-و-ل into a past tense verb قَوَلَ (he said) by adding the vowel marks. This shows Moses was the one speaking.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a 'weak' letter that often changes in Arabic verbs
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose. In this ayah, it connects to مُوسَىٰ (Moses) who is speaking.

steps.m: to him, for him (referring to مُوسَىٰ)
steps.n: Adds the pronoun 'him' to show who is being referred to. The ۥ mark after ه is just to help with pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew (משה/Moshe), not derived from an Arabic root. In this ayah, Moses is speaking to Al-Khidr, asking to learn from him.

steps.m: may I? (asking مُوسَىٰ's question to follow/learn)
steps.n: Question word used to ask for permission politely in this context. Here, Musa is asking his teacher if he may follow him to learn.

steps.m: to follow or go after something

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root letters into a complete past tense verb
Changed from 'he followed' to 'I follow' and added emphasis by doubling the ت
Added كَ (ka) at the end to mean 'you' - asking permission to follow you
Basic preposition showing position or condition. Here it connects Moses's request to learn by showing what he's asking about.
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show the long 'aa' sound
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to help with pronunciation when continuing to the next word

This is a connecting particle that joins Musa's request (أَتَّبِعُكَ - 'I follow you') to what he wants to learn (تُعَلِّمَنِ - 'you teach me'). It works like 'that' in English when you say 'I want that you teach me.'
Doubling the middle letter changes 'know' to 'teach' - making someone else know something
Changed from 'he taught' to 'you teach' by adding تُ at the start - makes it present tense and changes who is doing the action

steps.m: you teach me (Musa asking Khidr to teach him)
steps.n: Adding نِ (ni) at the end means 'teach me' - the نِ shows Musa is the one being taught
Basic preposition showing source or origin
Added ما which means 'what' to specify what is being referred from
The ن combines with م to make a doubled م (shown by ّ). This is just a pronunciation change that happens when these letters come together.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
The vowels changed to show someone received the teaching instead of doing it
Shadda doubles the middle letter to show the teaching was thorough or intensive
Added تَ to show this happened to 'you'. This word is being discussed in Musa's statement that starts with قَالَ (he said).

steps.m: to guide in the right direction

steps.n: Changed from action of guiding to the guidance itself - like changing 'guide' to 'guidance'

steps.m: right guidance (object of تُعَلِّمَنِ - teach me)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what Musa wants to be taught. It's like saying 'teach me guidance' where 'guidance' is what's being taught.
Added fathas to make it past tense. This word introduces what he said in the next part of the ayah.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain

steps.m: indeed you (subject of لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ - will not be able)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is addressing a single male person. This word emphasizes 'you' as the subject of the next phrase لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ (will not be able).

steps.m: will never (negates تَسْتَطِيعَ - you will be able)
steps.n: This is a negative particle that makes future actions impossible. In this ayah, it negates the verb تَسْتَطِيعَ (you will be able), making the meaning 'you will never be able'.
Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding 'aa' sound in middle
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or trying to do something
Changed to present tense with 'you' as the doer
Final damma changed to fatha to show this is in a special grammar position
This is a preposition that shows something is together with something else

steps.m: with me (referring to Moses speaking to Khidr)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show it means 'with me'
Added vowel marks to turn the action into a noun meaning the quality itself

steps.m: patience (object of تَسْتَطِيعَ - you will be able to)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what you won't be able to have - it's the thing being affected by the verb 'you will be able to'

steps.m: how? (asking about تَصْبِرُ - you being patient)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show pronunciation. This word is asking about the manner of being patient in the following verb تَصْبِرُ (you being patient).

steps.m: And how? (asking about تَصْبِرُ - you being patient)
steps.n: Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to connect this question to what came before it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Adds تَ at start to make it 'you' in present tense instead of 'he' in past tense
Adds a 'u' sound at the end to show this is the main verb after 'how'
This is one of the basic prepositions in Arabic that has no derivational history
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation - فتحة (َ) on ع and ل

steps.m: upon/over (indicating position or authority over مَا لَمْ تُحِطْ - what you have not encompassed)
steps.n: Added alif khanjariyah (ٰ) to show the elongated alif sound. In this ayah, it shows authority over knowledge that hasn't been fully grasped yet.
This is a question word that asks about things. In this ayah, it's asking about what knowledge hasn't been fully grasped yet.

This is the basic form of the word that negates verbs
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation. This word makes the verb تُحِطْ (you encompass) negative and changes it to past tense.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding تُ at start and changing middle vowels. The و changes to ي in present tense.

steps.m: you/she encompass (in negative context of لَمْ)
steps.n: When لَمْ comes before this word, it makes it mean 'did not encompass' and the middle letter ي drops out to make it shorter

Added هِ to show what the preposition connects to (it/him)
Added small ي (ۦ) after ه as a pronunciation helper - doesn't change meaning
Added damma (ُ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on middle letter to change from basic meaning into a noun meaning the result of knowing/informing

steps.m: knowledge/information (object of تُحِطْ - you did not encompass)
steps.n: Added tanween fatah (ًا) at the end to show this word is receiving the action of the verb تُحِطْ (you did not encompass)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from قَوَلَ by replacing weak letter و with ا
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and make a past tense verb
Attached pronoun نِى indicates direct object (me)

steps.m: you will find me (speaking to Musa)
steps.n: Added elongation mark (ٓ) to help with pronunciation. This word is Khidr speaking to Musa, telling him 'you will find me patient'.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects to the verb شَآءَ (wills/wishes) to form the phrase 'if Allah wills'
Added fathas to make it mean 'he' did the action in the past
The hamza moves to the end due to weak letter rules

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped

Added the u-mark at the end to show it's in the nominative case
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who has this quality

steps.m: patient (object of سَتَجِدُنِي - you will find me)

Added fatha to show clear pronunciation
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer

steps.m: and not (connected to أَعْصِى - I will disobey)

Added أَ at beginning to show 'I' am doing the action. The ى at the end shows it's happening now. This word connects to لَكَ meaning the disobedience is directed 'to you'.
This is a basic particle that shows direction or purpose
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for you (connected to سَتَجِدُنِىٓ - you will find me)

Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'command' by adding vowel marks
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being disobeyed in the phrase 'I will not disobey you (in any) command'
Added fathas to make it past tense and show who did the action
Changed weak letter و to ا, which is common in hollow verbs
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before
Added ِ at the end to connect it with the verb that follows (ٱتَّبَعْتَنِى meaning 'you follow me')
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Added اِتْ at the beginning to show doing something carefully and with extra effort
The ت and ب merge together with a shadda, making the action stronger
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did the action
Added نِى at the end to show the action was done to 'me', and changed the initial اِ to ٱ as it's more common in the Quran
This is the basic negation word that tells us something is not happening or should not happen

steps.m: then do not (connected to اتَّبَعْتَنِى - if you follow me)

Added نِ at the end to show that 'me' is being asked
Added ي to show this is specifically for a female speaker
Basic preposition that shows what something is about or away from. In this ayah, it's used with تَسْـَٔلْنِى (ask me) to mean 'ask me about'
Added fatha mark to show correct pronunciation
Root letters ش-ي-أ form the noun شَيْء (shay') meaning 'thing'. The fatha and sukun give it its noun form.

Added ى (alif maqsurah) at the end to complete the word meaning 'until'
Added the alif mark (ٰ) and the small alif (ٓ) to help with pronunciation. These don't change the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: it was brought up (as a topic)

Basic preposition showing direction or purpose
Added the pronoun كَ to show who is being addressed. In this ayah, Khidr is speaking to Musa.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added pronoun هُ (him/it) to show what is being referred to. This connects back to شَىْءٍ (something) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'the act of mentioning or remembering'

steps.m: a mention (object of أُحْدِثَ - I relate)
steps.n: Added tanwin alif to show this is the thing being mentioned. This word is what is being related/mentioned by the verb أُحْدِثَ (I relate/mention).
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

ف means 'and then' or 'so'

steps.m: and so they both set out
steps.n: The alif at the end shows this action was done by two people (Musa and Al-Khidr in this story)

Added a small alif (ٓ) above the ى which is just a pronunciation mark showing to elongate the sound slightly.
This is the basic form meaning a specific point in time. It's a particle, so it doesn't come from a root.
Adding ا makes it refer to future or conditional time. Here it introduces what happened when they got on the boat.

steps.m: to ride, mount, or board something

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a complete past tense verb meaning 'he rode'

steps.m: they both rode (referring to Moses and Al-Khidr boarding the ship)

This is a basic preposition that shows where something is. It's like saying 'in' or 'inside' in English.

steps.m: in (location preposition for السَّفِينَةِ - the ship)
steps.n: The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing. This doesn't change the meaning - it's just how we write it. Here it shows something is 'in the ship'.
Basic past tense verb showing the action of sailing

Added kasra to show it's connected to the word في (in) that comes before it in the ayah
Added ال at beginning to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he made a hole in it (acted on)
steps.n: Added ها which shows the feminine object being acted upon (the ship from the previous ayah)
Added fathas to make basic past tense form

steps.m: he said (introducing his response to خَرَقَهَا/he made a hole in it)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This word introduces what Musa said in response to Al-Khidr making a hole in the ship.

steps.m: to make a hole or tear through something

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Added تَ to show 'you' did the action
Added أَ at the beginning to make it a question

steps.m: did you make a hole in it
steps.n: Added هَا to show what received the action (the ship)
Changed first vowel to sukun to prepare for prefix addition
Adding تُ makes this a present tense verb for 'you' or 'she'
Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why the boat was damaged: to cause its people to drown

steps.m: to settle, inhabit, or belong to a place/family

steps.n: Forms the basic noun meaning 'people/family/inhabitants'
Adds fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Adds feminine singular pronoun suffix meaning 'its/her' to show possession. This word is the object being drowned in the phrase 'to drown its people'.
This is an emphatic particle that shows certainty or completion of an action

steps.m: certainly indeed (emphasizing جِئْتَ - you have brought)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to make the emphasis even stronger. This double emphasis connects to the verb جِئْتَ (you have brought) to show absolute certainty about what happened.
When adding تَ, the middle ا changes back to ئ with a kasra on the first letter and sukun on the middle letter
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' (masculine singular) did the action in the past
Added short vowels (fat-ha and sukoon) to make the basic noun meaning 'a thing'

steps.m: a thing (object of جِئْتَ - you have brought)
