Added tanween alif to show this is the object of جِئْتَ (you have brought). The context makes it clear this is a terrible or grievous matter.

steps.m: he said (subject of أَقُلْ in next ayah)
steps.n: Added fathas (َ) after each letter to show this happened in the past. This word connects to أَقُلْ (I say) in the next ayah where Khidr reminds Musa of what he said earlier.
The و (weak letter) changes to ا because it comes after fatha
This particle turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English
Added لَ for emphasis, making the question stronger
Added مْ which makes the meaning negative. The whole word now asks "Did not...?" in relation to the next word أَقُلْ (I say), so together they mean "Did I not say...?"
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed the vowels to make it present tense. The و (weak letter) causes special changes in pronunciation.
Added أَ at the beginning to show 'I' am doing the action
The و is dropped and final vowel changed to sukun because it comes after أَلَمْ (did not). This makes it mean 'did I not say'
Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to 'indeed'. This emphasizes what comes after it.
Added كَ at the end to show this is talking about 'you'. This word connects to تَسْتَطِيعَ (you will be able) in this ayah, showing who won't be able to have patience.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it negative in the future
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation. This particle makes the verb تَسْتَطِيعَ negative, showing that the person will not be able to do something in the future.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Added است at beginning to show seeking or acquiring ability. The و changed to ا due to being a weak letter.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing internal vowels

steps.m: you are able (after لَن meaning 'will not' in this ayah)
steps.n: Changed يَ to تَ to show 'you' instead of 'he'. Changed final ُ to َ because it follows لَن (will not) in the ayah.
Base word meaning 'with' - this is a particle that doesn't come from a root
The fatha changes to kasra to prepare for connecting with the next part
Added ى with fatha to mean 'me', showing who the word 'with' refers to
Changed the root letters into a noun that names the quality of being patient

steps.m: patience (object of 'you will not be able to have')

The و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a very common way to say 'he said' in Arabic.
This is a basic negative word that tells us the action won't happen. Here it's saying 'do not hold me accountable'
Added the fatha mark (َ) to show how to pronounce it as 'laa'

steps.m: to grasp or take hold of something

Added ت prefix to show 'you' are doing the action

steps.n: Added the preposition بِ (with/by) to show how something happened. Here it connects to the verb 'I forgot' to mean 'with what I forgot'

steps.m: to forget or leave something behind

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: I forgot (explaining to موسى why he made a mistake)

Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something (in this case, will be possessed by 'my')
Added ى to show this is 'my' matter/affair. This comes after the word مِنْ (from) in the ayah.

Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he' form

Added فَ at beginning to show this action follows something else. The اِ changes to ٱ after فَ.

steps.m: until (connecting to لَقِيَا - they both met)

steps.m: until (connecting to لَقِيَا - they both met)

steps.m: until (connecting to لَقِيَا - they both met)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ٓ) above the ى which is a pronunciation mark

steps.m: to meet or come across someone/something

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root into a proper past tense verb

steps.m: they both met (referring to Moses and Al-Khidr in the story)
steps.n: Added ا at the end to show two people did this action together. This refers to Moses and Al-Khidr meeting a young boy in their journey.
This pattern is used for young ones - here it means a young boy
The ًا ending shows this is the object of the sentence

steps.m: a boy (object of لَقِيَا - they both met)

Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after something else

Added fathas to make past tense form
Changed from قَوَلَ by replacing weak letter و with ا
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added ت at the end to show 'you' did the action
Added أَ at the beginning to turn the statement into a question

steps.m: a soul/person (object of قَتَلْتَ - you killed)
steps.n: The ًا shows this word is being acted upon. In this ayah, it's what was killed (the object of قَتَلْتَ - you killed).

steps.m: to be pure, clean, or grow in goodness

Changed the و to ي and added shadda to show intensity of the quality
Added ة to make it feminine - now describes a female/feminine noun
Added tanween to show this describes نَفْسًا (a soul) in the ayah

Added بِ at the beginning which combines with غَيْرِ to mean 'without'
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the noun form

Adding shadda (ّ) on the ل makes the emphasis even stronger, like saying 'most certainly' instead of just 'certainly'
The long ا changes to ئ with kasra and sukun when adding endings. This is a common pattern with hollow verbs (verbs with ا in the middle).
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did the action in the past
Root letters ش-ي-أ get vowels (fatha and sukun) to make the common word meaning 'thing'

steps.m: a thing (object of قَتَلْتَ - you killed)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon. In this verse, it refers to what was killed.

steps.m: to reject or disapprove of something

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to reject' into noun meaning 'a rejected/wrong thing'

steps.m: something very wrong or terrible (as a direct object)
steps.n: The ًا at the end shows this word is being used as a direct object in the sentence
Basic root letters ق-و-ل got vowels added to make قَوَلَ meaning 'he said' in the past tense. Links to 'I say' later in the sentence.
The middle letter و changes to ا, which is common for hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in middle)
This is a negative word that changes present tense verbs to past tense negative meaning
Added أَ at the beginning to turn the negative statement into a question. This word appears before أَقُل (I say) to ask 'Did I not say?'
Added vowels to make the past tense form. The و changes to ا because it's between two vowels.
Changed to command form. The middle weak letter is dropped and the first vowel changes to damma.
Added أَ at the beginning to show 'I' am doing the action. This word connects to لَّكَ (to you) in the ayah.
Starting with the basic particle ل which shows direction or purpose
The shadda (ّ) shows that this ل has merged with another ل from the previous word, making the meaning stronger
Added ك at the end to show this is directed to 'you'. This word comes after أَقُل (I say) in the ayah, showing what was said.
This is a particle that adds emphasis to what follows. It's like adding 'truly' or 'certainly' at the start of a sentence.

steps.m: indeed you (addressing Moses in this ayah)
steps.n: Added كَ (ka) at the end to show 'you', speaking directly to someone (Moses in this case)
This is a basic negative particle that makes verbs negative in the future. In this ayah, it negates تَسْتَطِيعَ (be able), making it mean 'you will not be able'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation

steps.m: to be able, to have capability

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to mean 'seeking/trying to' - shows someone working to gain the ability to do something

Starting with the basic word meaning 'with'

Added ى with fatha to mean 'me', connecting it to the word 'with' to create 'with me'

steps.m: patience (object of تَسْتَطِيعَ - you will be able)

The ًا ending shows this is the object being acted upon in the sentence. It's what you 'won't be able to have' according to the verse.
Added vowels to show this happened in the past. The middle و will change to ا in the next step because it's a special letter.
The و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change with weak letters.
This is a conditional particle that introduces the condition 'if you ask me'. The result of this condition is 'then do not accompany me' (فَلَا تُصٰحِبْنِى)
Connected the root letters together with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he' to 'I' by adding تُ at the end. The ْ (sukun) is added on ل to help pronunciation when adding تُ
Added كَ to show who was asked. This connects to the word شَىْءٍ (something) that comes next in the ayah
This is a preposition that shows what something is about. Here it connects 'I asked you' (سَأَلْتُكَ) with 'a thing' (شَىْءٍ), meaning 'I asked you about a thing'
Added vowels to the root letters to make the word mean 'a thing'
Added kasratain at the end because it comes after the word عَن (about/concerning) in the ayah
Added a small meem (called 'silent meem') to show proper pronunciation pause
Added vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to the root letters to make the word mean 'after'
Added هَا (meaning 'it') to show what comes after. Here 'it' refers back to the question that was asked.
Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a conclusion or result. Here it introduces the conclusion of the previous condition - if you ask again, then don't accompany me.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to accompany'
Adding ت at start makes it 'you' (2nd person) or 'she' (3rd person feminine)
Changed regular alif to small alif (ٰ) and made the last letter silent with sukun
Added نِى at the end to show the object is 'me'
This is a particle that adds emphasis to the past tense verb بَلَغْتَ (you have reached) that follows it. It's like adding 'indeed' or 'certainly' before a statement in English to make it stronger.
Added a sukun (ْ) mark on the د to show that it has no vowel sound after it
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' (addressing Musa) did the action. Changed the last vowel to sukun (ْ) before adding تَ.
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects to the word لَّدُنِّى (from Me) to show the source or origin.
The vowels show this is something close or personal
Double letters (shadda marks) added to make 'from' stronger and more direct/personal
Added ى at the end to show this is coming from the speaker
Changed the vowels to make it a noun meaning 'excuse' instead of an action

steps.m: an excuse (object of بَلَغْتَ - you have reached)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what is being reached/attained in the sentence. When Musa says this, he means 'you have reached/attained a valid excuse from me to end our companionship.'
Added the vowels 'fatha' (َ) to make it mean 'he moved forward' in the past
Adding اِنْ at the beginning shows the person did the action on their own without being forced
Adding فَ shows this action happened as a result of what came before

steps.m: and so the two of them proceeded
steps.n: Adding ا at the end shows two people did the action together (in this case, Moses and Al-Khidr)
This is a connecting word that shows something continued until a certain point. It connects 'so they both went on' with what happens next.
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to show emphasis
Added an alif mark (ٰ) at the end to show the sound is stretched out when reading
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to show even more stretching of the sound when reading
This is a basic particle used to show when something happens. It's like saying "at the time when" in English.
Added kasra under إ to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'i-dha'
Added fatha above ذ to show how to pronounce it as 'dha'
Extended the alif at the end to show a longer sound. In this ayah, it introduces what happened when they arrived, connecting to the next word أَتَيَآ (they both came to).
Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make it mean 'he came' in the past tense
Added the long alif (آ) at the end to show two people did the action. This is Moses and Al-Khidr in the story.

steps.m: people who belong together (like family or community)
steps.n: Changed from a verb about settling to a noun meaning the people who settle together. This word is being acted upon by the verb أَتَيَا (they both came to).

steps.m: to gather or settle in a place

Added تَ at start and ي in middle to make a noun meaning 'the process of returning something to its true meaning'
The ُ shows this word is describing what ذٰلِكَ (that) is in the sentence

steps.m: what (referring to the actions previously mentioned)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects to فَعَلْتُهُۥ (I did it) to ask about or refer to those actions. In this context, it refers back to all the actions Khidr did that Moses questioned.
This is the basic negative particle that shows something didn't happen
Adding مْ changes this from a simple 'not' to specifically mean something didn't happen in the past. Here it works with تَسْطِع (you were able) to say 'you were not able'
Added يَسْتَ at the start to mean 'he seeks/tries to be able to'
Changed the first letter to show 'you' instead of 'he'
Shortened form drops some letters but keeps same meaning. This is common in Arabic for smoother speech.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, upon, or over something else
The ى changes to يْ to help connect with the next part
Added هِ at the end to show this means 'him'
Added shadda on first letter for emphasis
Changed the root letters into a noun by adding vowel marks. This makes it name the quality of being patient.

steps.m: patience (object of تَسْطِع - you could bear)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word receives the action of the verb تَسْطِع (you could bear), meaning 'you could not bear patience'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Adding يَ at the start and changing vowels shows this is happening now
Adding ونَ shows multiple people are doing the action
Adding كَ shows you are being asked

steps.m: and they ask you (about ذِى ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to what came before it. The word is followed by 'about Dhul-Qarnayn' in the ayah
This is the basic form of the word that connects questions or statements to what they're about, like saying 'about' or 'concerning' in English. Here it connects 'they ask you' with 'Dhul-Qarnayn'
Added the fatha mark (َ) above the ع to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'an'
Added kasra to show this word connects to what comes after it. This word introduces who or what possesses something.

steps.m: possessor of (connected to الْقَرْنَيْنِ - the two horns)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to complete the word. This shows it's connecting to the word after it (القرنين meaning 'the two horns').

steps.m: horn (because it connects to an animal's head)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters. The meaning changes to 'horn' because a horn is something that joins or connects to an animal's head.
Adding ين shows we're talking about exactly two of something. In this ayah, it refers to Dhul-Qarnayn (the one with two horns), who was a powerful ruler mentioned in the Quran.

steps.m: the two horns (possessed by ذِى/owner of)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific. The ِ at the end shows it belongs to or is connected to ذِى (meaning 'owner of') that comes before it in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in response to their question)
steps.n: Changed to command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter and sukun on last letter, 2) Removing middle weak letter و. This is a command from Allah to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to respond to those asking about Dhul-Qarnayn.

steps.m: to follow or recite something in order

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Adding ت changes it from past tense (he recited) to present tense (you/she recite)
Changed تَ (you) to أَ (I) at the start to show I am the one reciting
Added سَ at beginning to show this will happen in the future
Added ا at the end to show multiple people will do this
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning

steps.m: upon you all (related to سَأَتْلُوا۟ - I will recite)
steps.n: Added كُم to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. This connects to سَأَتْلُوا۟ (I will recite) - meaning 'I will recite to you all'
Added sukun on ن to show end of basic word
The م has a shadda because it merges with the ن of the previous word
Added هُ at the end to show who/what is being referred to. Here it refers back to ذِى ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ (Dhul-Qarnayn) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to turn the root into a noun - the ِ under first letter and ْ on middle letter make it mean the thing itself rather than the action
Added ًا at the end to show this is the object being recited in the phrase 'I will recite to you a mention/account of him'
This is a special word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain, like saying 'definitely' in English
Added ا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This connects to the next word مَكَّنَّا (we established) where we are also the ones doing the action.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and shows we're giving power to someone else
The suffix نا changes 'he established' to 'we established' - showing that Allah is speaking about Himself
This is a preposition that shows something belongs to or is for someone

steps.m: for him (object of مَكَّنَّا - we established)
steps.n: Added هُ which means 'him'. This word shows who received the action of مَكَّنَّا (we established/empowered).
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows that power/authority was given within الْأَرْضِ (the earth/land).
Added ى at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make it a basic noun
Added 'al' to make it definite and kasra at the end because it comes after the word 'in' (فِى) in the ayah
Changes from basic 'to come' to causative 'to give/bring'
Added 'نَا' at the end to show 'we' did the giving. Changed from basic form to past tense.
Added هُ (him) to show who received the giving
Added وَ (and) to connect with previous phrase 'We established him in the land'

steps.m: from (shows where something starts or what it's made of)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows: 1) where something starts from, like 'from the city', or 2) what something is made of, like 'made from wood'. In this ayah, it's used with كُلِّ (every/all) to mean 'from all'.
Added damma (ُ) to make it a noun and doubled the middle letter to make it stronger
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word connects to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, making the phrase mean 'of every thing'
Root meaning 'to want' becomes the noun 'thing' - one of the most common words in Arabic
Added kasratain (two kasras) at end to show this word is affected by كُلِّ before it

The tanwin kasra (ٍ) was already there in both words - the only change is the letter ي was written as ى, which is just a writing style difference. The meaning stays the same - it's still 'of a thing' owned by كُلِّ (every/all).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create a basic noun meaning 'a way or means'

steps.m: a means (object of آتَيْنٰهُ/We gave him)
steps.n: The tanween (double marks) at the end shows this is indefinite (any way/means). This word is the object (what was given) in the phrase 'We gave him'. The accusative case shows it is receiving the action of giving.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding alif at the beginning makes this a causative verb - showing that someone is making something else follow
Added فَ to connect this action with what came before. This word is connected to سَبَبًا (a way/road) which shows what he followed.

steps.m: to make a path or connection
a path or means (object of فَأَتْبَعَ)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create a noun meaning the result of the action. This word is the object (thing being followed) of فَأَتْبَعَ (so he followed) in this ayah.

steps.m: a path/means (as the object being followed)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is an indefinite object being acted upon - something that is being followed
Starting with the basic particle form. This word has no root but comes from a base particle حت.
Adding shadda strengthens the meaning to show a definite endpoint
Adding the elongated ى (with alif above) at the end shows this word stretches to reach a limit or endpoint. The small alif (ٓ) adds emphasis.
This is the basic form of the word that refers to a specific point in time
Adding ا at the end makes it more general and conditional. In this ayah, it connects to the verb بَلَغَ (he reached) to show when something happened.

steps.m: he reached (subject of وَجَدَ - he found)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is a completed action in the past. This word describes what happened before he found (وَجَدَ) the setting place of the sun.

steps.m: to set (like the sun) or go westward

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added مَ at start and changed vowels to show this is the place where something happens. This word is connected to الشَّمْسِ (the sun) in this ayah, showing where the sun sets.
Adding vowel marks to create the standard noun pattern

steps.m: the sun (owned by مَغْرِبَ - setting place)
steps.n: Added 'the' (ال) to make it 'the sun', and doubled the ش sound because it comes after ال. The sun belongs to the setting place mentioned in the ayah.

steps.m: the sun (owned by مَغْرِبَ - setting place)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to or is connected to مَغْرِبَ (setting place)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. This word is connected to تَغْرُبُ (it sets) in the ayah, describing what he found.

steps.m: he found it/her (referring to الشَّمْسِ - the sun)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show what was found (the sun). In Arabic, the sun (الشَّمْسِ) is treated as feminine, so we use 'her/it' to refer to it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed vowels to show present tense - sukun on first letter, damma on second letter, and damma at end
Added تَ at start to make it a present tense verb meaning 'it sets'
Basic preposition showing location or being inside something. Here it shows the sun sets in a spring.
The ي at the end changes to ى when it's at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'spring' or 'source'
Added tanween kasra to show this is the object of the preposition فِي (in/into)
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic adjective
Added ة to make it refer to a specific muddy thing (in this case, a spring of water)
Added the tanween kasra to show this word describes the spring (عَيْنٍ) mentioned in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this is something that happened in the past. This word is connected to تَغْرُبُ (sets) in this ayah, showing what he found.
Added و at the beginning to connect this action with what happened before it in the story
Added vowels to make the preposition meaning 'at/near'

steps.m: at/near it (referring to الشَّمْسِ - the sun)
steps.n: Added ها to show this is happening at/near the sun mentioned earlier in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the noun meaning 'people'

steps.m: a people (object of وَجَدَ - he found)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this word is the object being found in the phrase 'he found a people'
Changed the middle letter و to ا, which is common for this type of word. Added vowels to show it happened in the past.
Changed from 'he said' to command form 'say!' - like telling someone to speak
Added نا to make it 'we' instead of just 'say'
Added fatha to show this is being used to point to someone specific (Dhul-Qarnayn in this case)
Added يٰ at the beginning to make it a call or direct address to someone
Added vowel marks to make it mean 'a single horn' (like on an animal)
Added dual ending to show exactly two horns. The ِ ending shows it's possessed by ذا (owner of)
Added the definite article ٱلْ to specify these particular two horns
Combined the conditional particle إِنْ (if) with the particle مَا (what) to start forming a new meaning
The word إِنْمَا (if what) becomes إِمَّا (either/whether) when the ن combines with م, making a stronger م sound (with ّ). This changes it to a word used when listing choices.

steps.m: either/whether (introducing options in أَن تُعَذِّبَ 'to punish' and أَن تَتَّخِذَ 'to adopt')
steps.n: Added the elongated alif (آ) at the end to emphasize the word. This word introduces two options in the verse: either to punish or to adopt good treatment.
Basic particle that introduces what someone might do. Here it connects to the word تُعَذِّبَ (punish) to show it's one of two options being given.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the punishment stronger or repeated
Added تُ at start to show 'you' doing the action, and changed middle vowel to kasra to show it might happen
The basic conditional particle that shows a choice between options. The long alif (آ) at the end shows emphasis.
Added و at the beginning to connect this choice with the previous option in the verse. This creates a structure like 'either X or Y'.

steps.m: that (introduces the choice 'either to punish' in إِمَّآ أَن تُعَذِّبَ)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that introduces what comes next. Here it shows one of two choices being given to Dhul-Qarnayn - either to punish the people or to treat them well.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
The ت merges with first letter to make shadda, showing something done to/for oneself
Adding تَ at start changes it from 'he' to 'you'
The kasra (ِ) sound in the middle makes it more passive/receiving
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. Since it's a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.

steps.m: in them (referring to the people Dhul-Qarnayn found)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show this refers to multiple people (specifically the people that Dhul-Qarnayn found near the setting sun). The م with sukun (مْ) shows it's plural.
Changed the action of being good into the quality of goodness itself

steps.m: with goodness (describing how something will be done)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how something will be done (in this case, how Dhul-Qarnayn will treat the people)

steps.m: he said (introduces his statement about punishing wrongdoers)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word introduces what Dhul-Qarnayn said about how he would deal with wrongdoers.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is very common in Arabic verbs with و in the middle
This is the basic particle that shows alternatives or options
Adding the shadda and alif changes this from a simple 'or' to a word that introduces a new topic or condition. Here it introduces what Dhul-Qarnayn said after قَالَ (he said).
This is a word that refers to any person who does something. Here it refers to anyone who does wrong (ظَلَمَ means 'does wrong'). It's like saying 'any person who...' in English.

steps.m: to do wrong or harm to someone unfairly
he wronged (subject of نُعَذِّبُهُ - we will punish him)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is something that happened in the past. This word is being referred to in the next part of the ayah where Allah says 'we will punish him'
This is a particle that shows something will happen in the future, like saying 'will' or 'soon' in English

steps.m: then soon (before نُعَذِّبُهُ - we will punish him)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after something else. Here it shows the punishment will happen after the wrongdoing mentioned before.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added shadda to show the punishment is more intense
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نُ at start and changing vowels
Added هُ at the end to show who receives the punishment
Basic particle that connects events in sequence
Added damma to help with pronunciation

Added shadda to show emphasis and indicate a time gap between events. In this ayah, it connects what happens to wrongdoers - first they are punished in this life, then they return to their Lord for more punishment.

steps.m: to send back or return something

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added shadda to show doubled letter
Changed the first vowel to show passive voice
Changed from past tense (was sent back) to present tense (is sent back) by adding يُ at the start
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. It connects the verb 'he will be returned' (يُرَدُّ) with 'his Lord' (رَبِّ)
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show proper pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with reading.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop completely

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does the nurturing action completely and perfectly
The word changes to show it is connected to something (like 'my Lord' or 'Lord of')
Adding هِۦ at the end shows this Lord belongs to 'him'. The word comes after إِلَىٰ (to) in the ayah, which is why it has the ِ ending from step 3.
The shadda (ّ) on ذ makes the punishment stronger or more intense
Added يُ at the start to make it 'he will' instead of 'he did'
Added هُۥ at the end to show who receives the punishment
Added فَ (then/so) at the start to show this happens after something else
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun by adding alif after the second root letter. This word is what will be given as punishment.

steps.m: a punishment (object of يُعَذِّبُ - he will punish)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object receiving the action of punishment in the sentence

steps.m: to reject or disapprove of something

steps.n: Changed from root meaning 'reject' to past tense verb 'he rejected' by adding vowels
Changed vowels to make it into a noun showing the intense quality of rejection
Added shadda to make the meaning stronger

steps.m: a terrible punishment (object of فَيُعَذِّبُهُۥ عَذَابًا)
steps.n: Added tanween (double line) at the end to show this is describing how terrible the punishment will be

steps.m: And as for (introduces a new topic)
steps.n: Added و (and) at the beginning to connect this new topic to what came before. This combination is commonly used to start discussing a new subject while maintaining flow with previous ideas.
This is a special word that introduces someone who does something. Here it refers to the person who 'believed' (ءَامَنَ). It's like saying 'As for whoever believed...'
Connected the root letters to show the basic concept
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it mean 'he was safe' in the past
Changed the first letter to hamza and added alif, making it Form IV which means 'to cause/make someone do something'. Here it means to put one's trust in something, leading to belief.

steps.m: he worked/did (subject of فَلَهُۥ - then for him)
steps.n: Added vowels to make past tense verb. This shows who did the action (he) and when it happened (in the past).

steps.m: and he worked/did (subject of فَلَهُۥ - then for him)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this action with the previous statement about believing (ءَامَنَ)
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a participle (describing word) by adding alif after first letter and kasra on middle letter
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb عَمِلَ (did/performed)
This is the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement about those who believe and do good deeds

steps.m: then for him (connected to جَزَآءً - reward)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who receives the reward mentioned later in the ayah

steps.m: to give back what someone deserves

steps.n: Adding vowel marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he rewarded) to a noun (reward) by adding alif and hamza at the end

steps.m: a reward (object of فَلَهُ - for him is)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is the object being given. The word relates to فَلَهُ (for him is) in the ayah.
Root meaning 'beautiful/good' becomes a noun meaning 'goodness'
Adding ى (alif maqsurah) at the end makes it feminine and means 'the best/most excellent'

steps.m: the best outcome (reward for good deeds)
steps.n: The word gained 'ال' (the) at the start to make it specific, changing it from just 'best' to 'THE best reward'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he said' to command form 'say!' by changing the vowels
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action
Added سَ to show this will happen in the future
Added وَ to connect this phrase to what came before it
This is a prefix particle that shows direction or purpose
Added هُ which means 'him' to show who receives the reward mentioned in the ayah
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. Here it connects to the word أَمْرِنَا (our command/matter) to show what something is from.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun instead of a verb
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنْ (from)
Added نَا at the end to show it belongs to us
Changed from basic root meaning 'easy' into a noun meaning 'ease' by adding the vowels u and silence marks

steps.m: ease (as the object of سَنَقُولُ - we will speak)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this word is receiving the action of 'we will speak' in the ayah
This is a connecting word that shows one thing happened after another. The shadda (double letter) makes the word stronger.

steps.m: then/next (connecting أَتْبَعَ to what came before)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ), fatha (َ), and shadda (ّ) marks to show exact pronunciation and emphasis. This word connects the action أَتْبَعَ (he followed) to previous events, showing it happened after them.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added hamza with fatha at start and sukun on first root letter to show causing someone/something else to follow. This word relates to سَبَبًا (a way/path) which is what was made to be followed.

steps.m: to cause, to be a means or way

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and make it into a noun meaning the actual path or means

steps.m: a way/means (object of أَتْبَعَ - he followed)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being followed in the phrase 'then he followed a way'
This is the basic form of the particle that shows when something ends or reaches its limit
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning and fatha for pronunciation
Added ى at the end to show reaching a limit or endpoint

steps.m: until (shows the end point of something)
steps.n: Added elongation marks (ٰٓ) which are just writing variations and don't change the meaning
This word shows when something happened in the past. In this ayah, it connects to the word بَلَغَ (he reached) to show 'when he reached'.

steps.m: to reach or arrive at something
he reached (مَطْلِعَ ٱلشَّمْسِ - the rising place of the sun)
steps.n: Added fathas (َ) after each letter to show this happened in the past. In this ayah, he reached specifically the rising place of the sun.

steps.m: to rise or appear (like the sun)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added مَ at the start to mean 'place where', changed middle vowel to make it mean 'place of rising' - this is where the sun comes up
Adding vowel marks to make the basic noun pattern

steps.m: the sun (owned by مَطْلِعَ - rising place)
steps.n: Added ال (the) to make it 'the sun' - note: it's not owned by مَطْلِعَ, they are separate words

steps.m: the sun (owned by مَطْلِعَ - rising place)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to مَطْلِعَ (rising place)
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to show this happened in the past

steps.m: he found her/it (referring to الشَّمْسِ - the sun)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show what was found. In this ayah, it refers to الشَّمْسِ (the sun) which was mentioned earlier.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding تَ prefix and changing vowels
This is a basic preposition showing something is physically or metaphorically on top of or over something else
The alif at the end is written with a special mark (ىٰ) to show it's being stretched in pronunciation. This word connects to قَوْمٍ (people) in this ayah, showing the sun rises over these people.
Changes verb 'to stand' into noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Added tanween kasra to show this is the object of the preposition عَلَىٰ (upon/over)
Basic negative particle that makes verbs show something didn't happen in the past. Like saying 'did not' in English.
Added shadda because this word comes after another ل in the ayah (نَجْعَل لَّهُم). When two ل letters meet, they merge in pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: we make/set (in response to لَّمْ before it)
steps.n: The ن at the beginning shows 'we' are doing the action. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) because it comes after لَّمْ which makes the verb present tense but changes its ending.
Starting with the basic preposition ل and adding fatha to show its basic meaning
The lam gets a shadda for emphasis because it's assimilating with another lam
Adding هُم at the end shows who the preposition is directed to - in this case 'them'
This is a basic particle that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. The kasra (ِ) is added to show it's connecting to the next word.
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after لَّمْ. This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to be beneath or lower than something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it from a root to a word meaning 'beneath'
Added kasra to show this word connects to what comes after it

steps.m: below/beneath it (referring to الشَّمْسِ - the sun)
steps.n: Added هَا to show this refers to the sun (الشَّمْسِ) mentioned earlier in the verse. This word is part of the phrase 'no protection from it (the sun)'.
Root letters turned into a noun meaning 'cover' by adding the vowels kasra (i) and sukoon

steps.m: a cover (object of وَجَدَهَا - he found it)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what was found in the ayah - it's the object that the verb وَجَدَهَا (he found it) acts upon
This is the basic demonstrative word meaning 'this' or 'that'
Changed to ذٰلِ to show something far away (like pointing to something in the distance)
Added كَ at the beginning to show similarity or comparison
Added كَ at the end to show this is being addressed to someone
This is a particle that emphasizes past actions. Here it emphasizes that they definitely had complete knowledge, connecting to the verb أَحَطْنَا (we encompassed/surrounded) that follows it.
The و connects this phrase to what came before, while قَدْ emphasizes the completeness of their knowledge that follows
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb. The و changes to ا in this pattern.
Adding أ at the start makes the meaning more complete or comprehensive
Adding نَا at the end shows 'we' did the action. The ا from the middle drops because of this change. This verb connects to بِمَا (with what) in the ayah.
This is a connecting word that links ideas together. Here it connects to لَدَيْهِ (with him/at his place) to show what was known.
The بِ shows how the action of أَحَطْنَا (we encompassed/knew fully) happened - it happened with/by what was there
This is a basic word showing location or closeness to something, like saying 'at' or 'near' in English

steps.m: with him, near him (what was known about in خُبْرًا)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show we're talking about 'him'. The يْ is added to make it easier to say. This 'him' refers back to what was mentioned in the word خُبْرًا (information/news).

steps.m: to know about something through experience
steps.n: This forms the verbal noun meaning 'knowledge/information'
Added damma and sukun to make it into a noun meaning the result of knowing/experiencing something
Added tanween alif to show this is the object of أَحَطْنَا (we encompassed) in this ayah

steps.m: then, next, afterward (connects to أَتْبَعَ - he followed)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. The ُ (damma) and َ (fatha) with ّ (shadda) give it emphasis and make it flow better in speech.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

Adding أَ at the start and changing the vowels makes this a causative verb - it means making something follow rather than following directly. In this ayah, سَبَبًا (a way/means) is what is being made to follow.

steps.m: to make a path or connection

steps.n: Connected the letters together with fatha marks to create a noun meaning the result of the action

steps.m: a path/means (object of أَتْبَعَ - he followed)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being followed in the phrase 'then he followed a way'
This is the basic form of the particle that shows when something reaches a limit
Adding the shadda emphasizes the meaning of reaching a limit

steps.m: until, up to (shows a limit or endpoint)
steps.n: The elongated alif (ىٰ) and small alif (ٓ) are added to complete the particle. These are spelling variations that don't change the meaning.
This is the basic word that shows when something happened in the past

steps.m: when (showing a future or conditional time)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end changes it from past time to future/conditional time. In this ayah, it shows when Dhul-Qarnayn reached a specific place.

steps.m: to reach or arrive at something
he reached (connected to حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا - until when)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word comes after حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا (until when) to show when something happened.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and turn the root into a preposition meaning 'between'

steps.m: between (acted on by بَلَغَ - he reached)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word is being acted on by the verb بَلَغَ (he reached) in this ayah
Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter to show it's a strong barrier
Added يْن at the end to show we're talking about exactly two barriers. This comes after the word بَيْنَ which means 'between'.
Added ٱل at the beginning to point to specific barriers, and ِ at the end because it comes after 'between'
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make the root letters into a past tense verb meaning 'he found'. Like changing 'find' to 'found' in English.
Basic preposition showing starting point or source. In this ayah, it connects to دُونِهِمَا (besides/below them) to show the location where something starts.
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show this is a preposition that connects to what comes after it

steps.m: to be beneath or lower than something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it from a root to a word meaning 'beneath'
Added kasra to show this word connects to what comes after it

steps.m: below/beneath them both (referring to السَّدَّيْنِ/the two barriers)
steps.n: Added هِمَا to show this refers to both barriers mentioned earlier (السَّدَّيْنِ)
Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands/rises together'
Added tanween alif to show this refers to any unspecified people. This word is what Dhul-Qarnayn found when he reached between the two barriers.
This is a basic negation word that shows something doesn't happen or isn't true
The shadda (ّ) shows this word was connected to something before it, making the 'not' stronger. In this ayah, it's connected to the people who could barely understand speech.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening in the present. This word connects to يَفْقَهُونَ (they understand) in the ayah.
Added ُونَ at the end to show it's a group of people doing the action. These are the same people who 'don't understand' (يَفْقَهُونَ) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters together with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they understand deeply (referring to the people mentioned as قَوْمًا in the ayah)
steps.n: Added plural ending to show many people are doing the action. This refers back to the people (قَوْمًا) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Changed from basic root meaning 'speak' to the noun 'speech' by adding the vowel pattern َوْ (fatHa then sukoon)

steps.m: a saying (object of يَفْقَهُونَ - they understand)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this word is being acted upon - it's what the people don't understand in the verse
Adding the basic past tense vowel pattern
The middle letter و changed to ا and وا was added at the end to make it 'they' instead of 'he'
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this one' - used to point to something nearby

Added يٰ at the beginning to make it a call or address. This is used to get someone's attention, like saying 'Hey you!' or 'O you!' in English. Here it's specifically addressing Dhul-Qarnayn (ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ).

steps.m: to connect or pair things together
horn or time period (owned by ذا)
steps.n: Changed the root letters into a noun meaning either a physical horn or a period of time. This word is part of the title ذا القرنين (The One of Two Horns/Ages).

Added ين to show there are exactly two of something. This is part of the famous title ذا القرنين (The One of Two Horns/Ages), possibly referring to someone who ruled two regions (east and west) or had two horn-like braids.

steps.m: the two horns/ages (part of the title ذا القرنين)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it belongs to ذا. Together ذا القرنين means 'The One of Two Horns/Ages' - a title for a powerful ruler mentioned in the Quran.
This word adds emphasis to what comes next in the sentence. It tells the listener 'pay special attention to this!' In this ayah, it emphasizes that Yajuj and Majuj are causing corruption.
This is the basic form of the proper noun referring to a group of people mentioned in the Quran, often paired with Majuj
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation and grammatical role. The fatha at the end shows this is the object of إِنَّ (indeed) in this ayah.
Added fatha at the end to show Majuj is being described as causing corruption (مُفْسِدُونَ) in the verse
Adding و connects it to يَأْجُوجَ (Yajuj) mentioned just before it, showing these are two related groups
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The مُ prefix combined with the vowel pattern shows this describes someone who does the corrupting

steps.m: those who spread corruption (subject of يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ)
steps.n: The ون ending shows this refers to a group of people. In this ayah, it's describing Yajuj and Majuj (Gog and Magog)
This is a preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it connects to the word ٱلْأَرْضِ (the earth) to show where Yajuj and Majuj are causing corruption.
Adding vowel marks to make the basic noun form
Added 'al' to make it definite and kasra at the end because it comes after the word في (in/on)
This is a question word used to ask yes/no questions

steps.m: so would you? (asking for permission from ذَا ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ)
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this question follows from their previous statement about Yajuj and Majuj causing corruption. They are politely asking Dhul-Qarnayn if he would accept their proposal.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb
Changed from 'he' to 'we' and from past to present tense. The people are asking Dhul-Qarnayn if they can make/establish something.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added the pronoun ك with fatha to show who the preposition is directed to. In this ayah, the people are addressing Dhul-Qarnayn.

steps.m: payment, tribute (object of نَجْعَلُ - we make)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning payment or tribute. In this ayah, the people are asking if they can set/make a payment for Dhul-Qarnayn.

steps.m: a payment (object of نَجْعَلُ - we make)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the direct object of نَجْعَلُ (we make/set). The people are asking if they can set a payment for Dhul-Qarnayn.
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of or dependent on something else
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is just a spelling variation of ى
Added a small alif (ٓ) above the word which helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: that (introduces what they want يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ to do)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that introduces what someone wants or plans to do. Here it connects 'we make' (نَجْعَلُ) with what they want to make - a barrier.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: you make/set (in response to هَلْ نَجْعَلُ - shall we make)
steps.n: Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' as the doer. The sukun on ج shows this is happening now/future. This is part of أَن تَجْعَلَ (that you make) following the question 'shall we make' (هَلْ نَجْعَلُ)
The root meaning of separation becomes a word meaning 'between' - the space that separates things
Added نَا at the end to show this refers to 'us', making it 'between us'. This word is the object of تَجْعَلَ (you make/place) in the phrase 'that you make between us'
The root meaning of separation becomes a word meaning 'between' - the space that separates things. This word is being used to show where something will be placed.
Added هُمْ to show who or what is being separated
Adds 'and' (wa) at the start
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Doubled the middle letter to create a noun showing the result of blocking
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being made in the phrase 'that we make between us and them a barrier'
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word is connected to مَكَّنِّى (enabled me) as he is the one speaking.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle
This is a question word asking about the thing/matter that Allah has established Dhul-Qarnayn in. It connects directly to the next word مَكَّنِّى (has established me) in the ayah.

steps.m: to give power or establish firmly

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more intense
The kasra at the end makes it into a command form
Adding ي at the end makes it mean 'me' - the person receiving the action
Adding ن with shadda makes the command stronger/emphasized
This is a preposition showing location or position

steps.m: in it (referring to what Dhul-Qarnayn was enabled with)
steps.n: Added pronoun هِ to show what the preposition refers to. In this ayah, it refers back to what Allah enabled Dhul-Qarnayn with (the power and resources).

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop
one who cares for and helps others grow (lord/master)
steps.n: The doubled ب with shadda shows this is someone who really takes care of others completely
The ِ at the end shows this word connects to something coming after it

steps.m: my lord (subject of قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this is MY lord. This word is being spoken by the subject of قَالَ (he said) in this ayah.

steps.m: better (than what Dhul-Qarnayn's people can offer)
steps.n: Basic noun pattern showing comparison. This word is telling us that what Allah has given Dhul-Qarnayn is better than what the people can offer him.

steps.m: better (than what Dhul-Qarnayn's people can offer)
steps.n: Added tanween (double damma) to show this is the main subject describing what Allah gave
The middle letter و changes to ي when it has a kasra vowel. This makes the basic command form.
Adding أَ at the start makes it a stronger command form
Adding و makes it a command to multiple people (like saying 'all of you help!')
Adding نِ shows the help is directed at me (I am being helped)
Adding فَ at the start shows this follows from something before, and ى at the end is the full writing of the 'me' ending
Root meaning 'strong' became a noun meaning 'one strength/power' by adding ّ (doubling) and ة at the end
Added بِ to show how something is done (with/by means of)
Added tanween (double dots under) to show it means 'with a strength' - describes how they should help
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed to a command form with أَ at start to mean 'let me' and sukun at end to show it's a command. This is part of Dhul-Qarnayn's response about what he will do to help.

steps.m: to be separate or distinct from something else

steps.n: Changes from verb root to noun meaning 'between'

steps.m: between you all (referring to Dhul-Qarnayn's helpers)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this refers to multiple people being addressed. In this ayah, Dhul-Qarnayn is speaking to his helpers about building a barrier.

steps.m: between (object of أَجْعَلْ - I will make)
steps.n: Forms a noun meaning 'between' from the root. This word is being acted upon by the verb أَجْعَلْ (I will make/place) in the phrase 'I will make between you'

steps.m: and between (object of أَجْعَلْ - I will make)
steps.n: Adds و which connects this to the previous phrase about 'between you', showing both sides of what will be made

steps.m: and between them (object of أَجْعَلْ - I will make)
steps.n: Adds هُمْ to show the second group that the barrier will be between. The full meaning with context is 'I will make between you and between them'

steps.m: to block or fill up a gap

steps.n: Changed the action into a thing by adding vowel marks. This is the thing that will block the gap.

steps.m: a barrier (object of أَجْعَلْ - I will make)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what will be made. In the ayah, Dhul-Qarnayn is saying 'I will make (أَجْعَلْ) a barrier between you and them.'
Changed first letter to hamza with madd and added fatha to show past tense
Adds plural masculine subject marker
Added نِى at the end to show the command is directed at me
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the first vowel to damma to transform from verb to noun meaning pieces

steps.m: to be hard/sharp or to set limits

steps.n: Added ي between the second and third root letters and vowel marks to create the noun for iron, emphasizing its characteristic hardness
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific. This word is connected to زُبَرَ (pieces) in the phrase 'pieces of iron'
Added kasra at the end because this word belongs to زُبَرَ (pieces), forming the phrase 'pieces of iron'
Basic particle form showing continuation until a point
Added fatha and doubled the ت with shadda to emphasize the meaning

steps.m: until/up to (emphasized, connects to next word)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show this connects to what comes after it
Added alif superscript (ٰ) and small alif (ٓ) as pronunciation helpers. This word connects to إِذَا (when) in the ayah to mean 'until when'

steps.m: when/if (connecting to سَاوَىٰ - he made equal)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows when something happens. In this ayah, it connects two events: 'when he made (the walls) equal' leads to what happens next.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to show intensive or mutual action, and changed final ي to ى because it's at the end
Added fatha marks to show basic past tense pronunciation
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it work as a preposition. This word connects to صَّدَفَيْنِ (the two sides) in this ayah, showing the space between them.
Connected the root letters with basic vowels to form a noun
Added يْن to show exactly two mountain sides
Added ٱل (the) and doubled the ص with shadda. The ِ at the end shows this comes after the word بَيْنَ (between)
Changed from basic root 'say' to past tense 'he said' by adding the vowel marks (fatha)
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to blow air or breathe out forcefully

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he blew' to 'blow!' by adding 'u' at start and changing vowels to make it a command to one person
Changed to plural command by adding وا at the end and changing the last vowel
This is the basic particle that shows a limit or endpoint. Added vowel marks to show pronunciation.
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning and pronunciation
Added alif maqsura with superscript alif to show this word indicates reaching a limit or endpoint
Added small alif to connect this word with إِذَا (when) that follows it, showing these words work together to mean 'until when'

steps.m: when (introduces a condition in حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا سَاوَىٰ - until when he made equal)
steps.n: This is a time word that shows when something happens. In this ayah, it comes after حَتَّىٰٓ (until) to mean 'until when' and connects two events: making the walls equal and the next action.

steps.m: when (introduces the time condition for جَعَلَهُۥ - he made it)
steps.n: إِذَا here connects two actions in time: (1) making the walls equal and (2) making it into fire. It shows that the second action happened right after the first one was complete.

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he made it (referring to the iron blocks) into something
steps.n: Adds هُ meaning 'it' to show what is being transformed. Here 'it' refers back to the iron blocks (زُبَرَ ٱلْحَدِيدِ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
The و (weak letter) changes to ا when we add fatha on the first letter to make it a noun. This is a common pattern for hollow roots (roots with و in the middle).
Added tanwin (double fatha) to show this is indefinite (like 'a' in English) and is being acted upon. This word shows what something was turned into by the verb جَعَلَ (he made/turned it into).
Added vowels to make past tense form
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ by replacing weak letter و with ا
Changed from basic root to 'I bring' form
Changed to passive voice - something is being brought
Added plural masculine ending to show multiple people/things
Changed from 'they bring' to 'bring to me!' by adding نِي (nee) at the end, which means 'to me'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Makes it passive - something being poured out rather than doing the pouring
Basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on top of'
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for connecting with the pronoun

steps.m: upon it/him (referring to the molten copper being poured)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هِ (him/it) to show what is being acted upon
Changes the action of pouring metal into the metal itself (copper)

steps.m: molten copper (object of أُفْرِغْ - I may pour)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being poured in the phrase 'I may pour upon it molten copper'
This is a question word that asks about things or introduces relative clauses

steps.m: then what/so what (introduces consequence in relation to يَظْهَرُوهُ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of the previous action. In this ayah, it connects to يَظْهَرُوهُ (climb over it) to show what happened after they tried to climb.

steps.m: to be able to do something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added اِسْ at the beginning to show trying or making effort to do something. This is a shortened form of اِسْتَ where the ت is dropped for easier pronunciation
Added وا at the end to show many people (they) did the action
Added ا (alif) at start to help pronounce the word - it's not pronounced when in middle of sentence

steps.m: that/to (connects to يَظْهَرُوهُ - they could scale/climb it)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence. Here it connects 'they were not able' with 'to climb it'
The vowel marks (َ) were added to make it past tense - 'he climbed over'
Changes from past tense (he climbed) to present tense (he climbs) by adding يَ at the start and changing vowels
Changes from singular (he climbs) to plural (they climb) by adding و at the end
Adding هُ shows what is being climbed over
Added fatha to show this is the negative particle ما that makes the verb ٱسْتَطٰعُوا۟ (they were able) negative, changing it to mean 'they were not able'

steps.m: and they were not (able to ٱسْتَطٰعُوا۟)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, showing these two things happened together - they couldn't climb over it AND they couldn't dig through it

steps.m: to be able or willing to do something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or gaining ability

steps.m: they were able to (subject of يَظْهَرُوهُ - to climb over it)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. This word is the subject of يَظْهَرُوهُ (to climb over it) in this ayah.
Added fatha to show the basic vowel of the preposition

steps.m: for him/it (referring to نَقْبًا - making a hole)
steps.n: Added pronoun هُ (him/it) to show who the action is for. Here it refers to making a hole through the barrier.
Added prolongation mark (ۥ) to indicate the extended pronunciation of the final vowel
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun describing what results from digging or piercing.
Added ًا to show this is the object being acted upon. In this ayah, it shows what they were not able to do - they couldn't make a hole in the barrier.

steps.m: to say or speak (directly to someone)

steps.n: Basic root ق-و-ل got vowels (َ) to make 'he said' - connects to next part where someone speaks
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle
This is the basic pointing word that shows something is near
Added ذا to specify this is pointing to something masculine and nearby
Added the alif mark (ٰ) and fatha mark to help with pronunciation. In this ayah, it's pointing to Allah's mercy (رَحْمَةٌ) that was mentioned right after.

steps.m: to be merciful, to show kindness and compassion

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun by adding َ (fatha) on first letter, ْ (sukun) on middle letter, and ة at the end. This makes it mean the quality or act of being merciful.
The ٌ (double damma) at the end shows this is indefinite (like saying 'a' instead of 'the'). In this ayah, it's telling us what 'this' (هٰذَا) is - it's a mercy.
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or originates

The shadda is added because this word comes before a word starting with ر. This is a pronunciation helper that makes it easier to say. In the ayah, it connects the word 'mercy' (رَحْمَةٌ) to 'my Lord' (رَّبِّى) to say 'mercy from my Lord'.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, raise up

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does a lot of nurturing
The shadda on the first letter adds emphasis, showing this is being said with strong feeling
The kasra shows this word is connected to something after it
Adding ى shows this is my Lord. This comes after the word مِن (from) in the ayah.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a time condition

steps.m: and when (connects to جَعَلَهُ - he made it)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. This introduces what happened after the previous statement, connecting to جَعَلَهُ (he made it).
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he came' in the past
Changed to hollow verb pattern by adding alif after first letter
Extended the alif to show emphasis/intensity of coming

steps.m: to promise or make a commitment

steps.n: Changed the action of promising into the thing itself (a promise)

steps.m: promise (subject of came in فَإِذَا جَآءَ)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is doing the action of 'coming' in the phrase 'when the promise came'
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares completely
Adding kasra shows this word is connected to something (like 'Lord of' or 'my Lord')
Adding ى at the end shows this Lord belongs to me. This word comes after مِن (from) in the ayah.

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Added fatha marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he made it (referring to the barrier/wall)
steps.n: The هُ shows what was acted upon - in this case referring back to the barrier/wall mentioned earlier in the story of Dhul-Qarnayn

steps.m: to crush or level something completely flat

steps.n: Connected the letters and added shadda to show strong crushing action

steps.m: completely flattened/leveled (describing the wall in the story)
steps.n: Adding آء makes it describe the state of being completely flattened. This word describes how the wall became after the promise of Allah came.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the past tense

steps.m: and he was (connected to جَعَلَهُ meaning 'he made it')
steps.n: Added وَ at the start to mean 'and'. This word connects to جَعَلَهُ (he made it) in the ayah, showing what happened after he made it.
Changes from action to the thing itself (the promise)
The ُ shows this word is doing the action of coming in the phrase 'when the promise came'

steps.m: to nurture, care for, and have authority over
lord (one who nurtures and has complete authority)
steps.n: Connected the letters and doubled the ب (shown by ّ) to show complete lordship and authority
The kasra shows this word is connected to something after it, like 'my' in 'my lord'
Adding ى shows this lord belongs to me (is my lord). This whole phrase وَعْدُ رَبِّى means 'promise of my lord'

steps.m: to be true, real, or right

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
The doubled ق (shown by ّ) makes it into a noun meaning the essence of being true

steps.m: truly/really (describes how something is done in وَعْدُ رَبِّى حَقًّا)
steps.n: Adding ًا changes it from a noun (truth) to an adverb (truly) that describes how the Lord's promise is fulfilled
Adding basic past tense voweling pattern
Adding نا at the end changes 'he left' to 'we left'
Adding و at the beginning connects this action with what came before

steps.m: to separate or divide into parts

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make a noun meaning 'some' or 'part'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Added هُمْ at the end to show this refers to 'them'. This word is being acted upon by تَرَكْنَا (we left)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun form
Added fatha to connect this word to what follows it
Added ئِذٍ to specify 'that particular day' being discussed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to ُ, and adding ُ at end
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it connects to the word بَعْضٍ (some/others) to show one group being within another.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the noun form
The tanwin kasra shows this word is indefinite (like 'a' in English) and is being possessed by or connected to something else

steps.m: to blow into something with force

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: it was blown (into الصُّورِ - the Horn)
steps.n: Changed the vowels to show this is happening to something rather than someone doing it. This refers to the Horn being blown on the Day of Judgment.

steps.m: and it was blown (into الصُّورِ - the Horn)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this to what happened before it
This is a basic preposition showing one thing is inside or within another. Here it shows how some people are surging/flowing into others.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added damma to create a noun meaning the instrument that makes the sound
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite

steps.m: in the horn, the trumpet (blown into in وَنُفِخَ فِى)
steps.n: The word gets a kasra (i) sound at the end because it comes after the word في (in/into)
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after something else
Added نا to show 'we' did the gathering
Added هُمْ to show who was gathered (them)

steps.m: to gather or bring things together

steps.n: Changed from basic root meaning 'gather' into the noun 'gathering' by adding vowel marks (fatha and sukun)

steps.m: a gathering (as the object being described)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to make it an object (something being gathered) instead of just the general idea of gathering
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added نا at the end to change 'he displayed' to 'we displayed'. The نا means 'we' and shows who did the action.
Added وَ (and) to connect this action with what came before it
This is a proper noun that doesn't come from a root. It's believed to be borrowed from Hebrew 'Gehinnom'

steps.m: Hell (the object being shown in عَرَضْنَا)
steps.n: Added vowel marks and shadda (double letter mark) to show the correct pronunciation. This word is what's being 'presented/shown' in the phrase 'We will present Hell'.
Added fatha at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it
Adding ئِذٍ changes 'day' to mean specifically 'on that day' or 'at that time'

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Changes the verb 'to reject' into 'one who rejects' by adding alif after first letter
The word changed from singular (one rejecter) to plural (many rejecters) by adding ين at the end
Adds 'for/to the' (لِلْ) at the start of the word

steps.m: for the rejecters of truth (object of عَرَضْنَا)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل and elongated alif. This word is receiving the action of عَرَضْنَا (We presented) in this ayah.
Added vowels to the root letters to make it mean 'the act of displaying' instead of 'to display'

steps.m: as a display (emphasizing how we displayed Hell)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word describes how the action was done. It's connected to عَرَضْنَا (we displayed) in the verse to emphasize we really did display Hell.
This is the base form of the relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to people or things
Adding ال (the) turns 'who/which' into 'the one who' - making it point to someone specific
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people. This word is the subject of كَانَتْ (was/were) in this ayah.
Adding the fatha mark makes this a past tense verb
Adding the second fatha completes the past tense verb pattern
Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine. This word connects to أَعْيُنُهُمْ (their eyes) which is feminine in Arabic.
Added short 'a' and 'sukoon' marks to make the root letters into the word for 'eye'
Changes the word to show many eyes using a special plural pattern
Adds ُ to show this word is the subject in the sentence
Adds هُمْ to show these eyes belong to them. This word is the subject of كَانَتْ (was/were) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition showing location. It connects غِطَآءٍ (covering) to show where something is.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in the Uthmani script. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the word from an action (to cover) into a thing (a cover) by adding alif and hamza, and changing the first vowel to kasra

steps.m: a cover (being used with the word عَن meaning 'from')
steps.n: The word gets a tanwin (double dots) at the end because it's being used with عَن (from). The alif is written longer to show proper pronunciation.

Basic form of the preposition that shows separation or moving away from something. In this ayah, it connects 'their eyes' with 'My remembrance', showing their eyes were turned away from Allah's remembrance.
Added fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation
Changed from verb (action) to noun (thing) by adding vowel marks
Added kasra at the end to show it connects to the next word
Added ى at the end to show it belongs to me. This word is what they are away from (عَن) in this ayah.
Added vowels to make past tense. The middle و becomes ا in past tense.
Added وا to show multiple people (they) instead of one person (he)

steps.m: and they were (those whose eyes were covered from My remembrance)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this to the previous description. This word refers back to the people mentioned before whose eyes were covered from Allah's remembrance.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَسْتَطِيعُونَ (they are able) that comes after it, making the meaning 'they are not able'

steps.m: to be able to do something
steps.n: Added letters at the beginning to show asking for or seeking ability
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle letter ا to ي

steps.m: they are able to (referring to those whose eyes were covered)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers back to those mentioned earlier whose eyes were covered.
Root meaning 'hear' becomes the noun 'hearing' by adding marks (fatha and sukun) to the letters

steps.m: hearing (as the object being affected in لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object of the verb يَسْتَطِيعُونَ (they were not able to). The full meaning in context is 'they were not able to hear.'
Basic past tense form showing someone did the action of thinking. This word is connected to يَتَّخِذُوا۟ (they take) in the ayah, showing what was thought about.
Added فَ which means 'and' or 'then' to connect it to what came before

steps.m: do they then think? (questioning the thinking of الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added أَ at beginning to make it a question expressing disapproval of what follows. The subject 'they' refers back to الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ (those who disbelieved) mentioned right after.
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that points to something
Changed the ending to make it point to someone or something being described
Added ال to make it specific about who or what we're describing
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people
Added vowel marks and changed ال to ٱ for proper Quranic spelling. This word is connected to كَفَرُوٓا۟ (they disbelieved) which comes right after it.

steps.m: to reject, deny, or cover up truth

steps.n: Added fatha marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the ending to show many people did this action together

This is a connecting word that introduces what the disbelievers thought - that they could take God's servants as protectors. It connects the main verb حَسِبَ (they thought) to what they thought (يَتَّخِذُوا۟ - they could take).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added اِتّ at the beginning to show doing something for oneself. The ت combines with the خ to make تّ
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changes vowel pattern to form broken plural for people who serve/worship
Adds ى at the end to show these are my servants specifically
This is a preposition that connects two words, showing that 'My servants' are being taken 'from below/besides' Me. It shows separation or distance.

steps.m: to be below or less than something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'below/beneath'
Added kasra (ِ) to connect this word to the next word in the sentence

steps.m: besides me/other than me (connected to عِبَادِى meaning 'My servants')
steps.n: Added ي with a long vowel mark to mean 'me', showing this refers to Allah's servants being taken as protectors besides Him

steps.m: to protect or be close to

steps.n: Added vowels and doubled the last letter to make it a noun for someone who protects
Changed from single to many by making it plural (like going from 'friend' to 'friends')
Added fatha at end to show this is the object of the verb يَتَّخِذُوا (they take). Also lengthened the alif for emphasis.
Starting with the emphasis particle إِنَّ that strengthens what comes after it. Here it emphasizes that Allah has truly prepared something.
Added آ (long alif) at the end to show this is Allah speaking about Himself using the royal 'We'. This 'We' is the subject (doer) of the verb أَعْتَدْنَا (We have prepared).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added hamza with fatha at start and sukun after first letter to show making something happen
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action. In this ayah, Allah is telling us what He prepared for the disbelievers.
Added vowel marks and shadda to show correct pronunciation of this proper noun for Hell

steps.m: Hell (as the object of أَعْتَدْنَا - we have prepared)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb 'we have prepared' (أَعْتَدْنَا) in the ayah

steps.m: to reject, cover up, or deny truth
one who rejects truth (acted on by أَعْتَدْنَا - We have prepared)
steps.n: Changes the action into a person who does it. This describes someone affected by the verb أَعْتَدْنَا (We have prepared) in the verse.
Added ين to show we're talking about multiple people who reject truth
Added ال to point to specific rejecters of truth, not just any rejecters
Added لِ at start to show these rejecters of truth are receiving something (in this case, Hell as hospitality)

steps.m: to come down or provide hospitality

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to come down' to plural noun meaning 'provisions'

steps.m: as hospitality (what is prepared by اللَّهِ)
