steps.n: Added tanwin alif to show this is what Allah has prepared as hospitality for the disbelievers in Hell. This word is the object of أَعْتَدْنَا (We have prepared).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he said' to 'say!' by making it a command. The middle letter و drops out and first vowel changes to make it shorter and easier to say as a command.
This is a question word that always comes at the start of a sentence to turn it into a yes/no question
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing this is important news being told
Changed from 'he told' to 'we tell' by adding نُ at start and changing vowels
Added 'you all' as the ones being informed. This connects to the next word الْأَخْسَرِينَ (the greatest losers) which is what they're being informed about.
Added أَ at the beginning to show this describes someone who has more of this quality than others, like saying 'bigger' or 'biggest' in English

steps.m: those who are greatest in loss (object of نُنَبِّئُكُم)
steps.n: The ين ending shows this refers to multiple people (more than two). This word is what/who we are being informed about in the question 'Shall we inform you?' (نُنَبِّئُكُم)

steps.m: the ones who are greatest in loss
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it specific, like adding 'the' in English

steps.m: about the ones who are greatest in loss (object of نُنَبِّئُكُم)
steps.n: Adding بِ at the start means 'about' or 'of'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (doing something) to many things that were done - like going from 'work' to 'works'

steps.m: deeds/actions (as the thing being described in أَخْسَرِينَ/greatest losers)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is answering 'in what way?' they are the greatest losers - specifically in their deeds/actions
Added ٱلّ to make it specific. This word points to someone or something we're about to describe.

steps.m: those who (subject of ضَلَّ - went astray)
steps.n: Added نَ to show we're talking about multiple people. This word describes the people whose deeds were lost, as shown by the verb ضَلَّ (went astray) that follows.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he went completely astray (describes الَّذِينَ - those who)
steps.n: The doubled ل with shadda shows this is a strong or complete going astray. This describes 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) in the ayah.

steps.m: to work hard, to put in effort

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create a noun meaning the actual effort or work someone does. This word is what 'went astray' in the verse.
Added هُمْ at the end with ُ before it to show this effort belongs to them. The ُ is needed because this word is doing the action of 'going astray' in the verse.
Basic preposition showing location or position
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. This word connects to ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ (life) to show where their efforts were lost.
This root represents the basic concept of living or being alive
Added vowels and و to make it into a noun meaning 'life'. The ة at the end shows it's one specific instance of life.
Added ٱلْ to make it specific ('the life') and ِ at the end because it's connected to the word ٱلدُّنْيَا (worldly) that comes after it
Root د-ن-و means 'near' - added vowels and ي to make the word 'worldly/lower' (feminine form). The و changed to ي because it's easier to pronounce.
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the د letter because it comes after ال. This word describes 'life' (ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ) in the phrase 'worldly life'.
Added diacritics to show the correct pronunciation: damma (ُ) shows the 'u' sound and sukun (ْ) shows no vowel after م
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before. This word is the subject (doer) of يَحْسَبُونَ (they think/assume).
Basic past tense verb form with fatha vowels
Added يَ at beginning and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action
Basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added هُمْ at the end to show this applies to 'they/them' (masculine plural)
Added the vowels fatHa (َ) and Damma (ُ) to make it mean 'it became/was good' in the past
Changed from past tense 'was good' to present tense 'does good' by adding يُ at the start

steps.m: they do well (thinking they are doing good deeds)
steps.n: Added plural masculine ending to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to those who think they are doing good deeds but are actually misguided.

steps.m: to create, to make, to craft

steps.n: Transforms verb root into noun meaning 'the quality or result of skilled making/crafting'
Adds tanween (double marks) to show this word is the object - it tells us what they are doing well
This is the base form meaning 'those'

steps.m: those (pointing to something far) (subject of كَفَرُوا)
steps.n: Added ئِكَ to show we're pointing to something far away. This word is the subject of كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved) in this ayah.
Added ال to make it specific and point to particular people or things

steps.m: those who/the ones who (subject of كَفَرُوا)
steps.n: Added ين to show we're talking about multiple people. This word connects to كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved) in the ayah by showing who did the disbelieving.

steps.m: he rejected/denied (referring to those who rejected in أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word describes what the people mentioned before (أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ) did.

steps.m: they rejected/denied (referring to those who rejected in أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ)
steps.n: Adding وا shows many people did this action together. This refers back to the group mentioned at the start of the ayah.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or demonstrate
a sign or miracle (something that shows or demonstrates)
steps.n: Changes from a verb meaning 'to show' into a noun meaning 'a sign' by adding special vowels and making it a feminine word
Makes the word plural to show multiple signs

steps.m: by/with the signs (of رَبِّهِمْ - their Lord)
steps.n: Adds بِ at the beginning to show these signs are being used or dealt with in some way. This word is connected to كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved) in the ayah.

steps.m: by/with the signs (of رَبِّهِمْ - their Lord)
steps.n: Adds kasra at the end because this word is possessed by رَبِّهِمْ (their Lord) which comes right after it

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop to completion
lord, master, one who nurtures to completion
steps.n: Connected the letters and added shadda to show this is someone who does the nurturing completely
The kasra shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else in the sentence (the signs/verses mentioned before it)
Adding هِ shows possession by one male/neutral thing
Changed هِ to هِمْ to show the lord belongs to many people (those who disbelieved). This word is possessed by آيات (signs/verses) in the phrase 'signs of their Lord'
Root 'meet' changed to 'meeting' and got an 'i' sound at the end to show it belongs to something/someone
Added هِ to show the meeting belongs to him (referring to Allah/Lord in this context)
Added ۦ as a pronunciation helper for the possessive pronoun
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: it became worthless (referring to أَعْمٰلُهُمْ - their deeds)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense. This word describes what happened to their deeds (أَعْمٰلُهُمْ).

steps.m: and so it became worthless (referring to أَعْمٰلُهُمْ - their deeds)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of their disbelief mentioned in the previous part of the verse

steps.m: and so they became worthless (referring to أَعْمٰلُهُمْ - their deeds)
steps.n: Added تْ at the end because أَعْمٰل (deeds) is feminine plural
Connected the letters together with fatha marks to make a word for one single action
Changes the word to show many deeds instead of just one deed by adding أ at the start and ا (written as ٰ) after م
Adds ُ (damma) at the end to show these deeds are the subject of what's happening in the sentence
Adds هُمْ at the end to show these are their deeds. This word is the subject of فَحَبِطَتْ (became worthless) in this ayah.
This is the basic negation word in Arabic that means 'no' or 'not'
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. This connects to فَحَبِطَتْ (and so their deeds became worthless) in the previous part of the ayah.

steps.m: to establish, set up, make stand

steps.n: Basic root letters connected with vowels to make past tense 'he' form
Middle weak letter و changes to ا in past tense
Changed to present tense 'we' form. The middle weak letter ا becomes ي in present tense
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced
Added هُ which means 'him'
Added م with sukun to change 'him' to 'them'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun. The و is a weak letter that stays as is.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of the verb نُقِيمُ (we establish) in this ayah
Changed و to ي after kasra to make it easier to pronounce. This makes the word mean the action itself instead of doing the action.
Added ة to make it mean one specific type of rising - the resurrection
Added ال to show we're talking about THE resurrection - the specific one on Judgment Day
Changed spelling to match Quran style (ٱ instead of اَ), added kasra at end to show it follows يَوْمَ (day), and wrote alif as ٰ
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the result of weighing. This word is what's being acted on by نُقِيمُ (we establish/give) in this ayah.

steps.m: any weight (being acted upon by نُقِيمُ - we establish/give)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what's being acted upon and is indefinite (any weight). In this ayah, it means Allah won't give any weight/value to the deeds of these people.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) to show it's a fixed part of the word
Adding لِ changes the word from pointing to something near to pointing to something far

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (subject of جَزَآؤُهُمْ)
steps.n: The كَ is part of the complete word ذٰلِكَ - it's not an addition. ذٰلِكَ is a single word meaning 'that' used to point to something far away. Here it points forward to their punishment (جَزَآؤُهُمْ).
Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding the vowel 'a' between letters
Changed from a verb (action) to a noun (thing)
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing something in the sentence
Added هُمْ to show this belongs to them. This word is the subject of جَهَنَّمُ (Hell) in this ayah.
Added basic vowel marks to the base word. The shadda on ن is part of the original word structure since this is a borrowed proper noun.
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence. This connects to جَزَآؤُهُمْ (their recompense) in the previous part of the ayah, showing that Hell is their recompense.
This is a relative pronoun that means 'what' - it refers to the actions mentioned after it (their disbelief and mockery)
Added بِ at the beginning to show cause/reason. This connects to the verb كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved) to explain why they deserve the punishment mentioned.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adds وا at the end to change from 'he disbelieved' to 'they disbelieved'

steps.m: to take or adopt something as one's own

steps.n: Added vowels to make it 'he took' in the past tense

steps.m: to take/adopt for oneself (Form VIII past)
steps.n: Changed to Form VIII by adding ت between first two letters, which then combines with the following letter. The initial أ changes to ٱ. This form shows doing something for oneself.
Added conjunction prefix وَ meaning 'and'
Changed from singular 'he' to plural 'they'
Added elongation mark (ـٓ) and small و above to indicate proper recitation pause

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.n: The root letters are transformed into a noun by adding the vowel 'aa' sound and changing the last 'ya' into 'ta', making it mean 'a sign'
Makes the word plural to show multiple signs/verses
Shows the word is being used as an object of a preposition
Adds 'my' to show ownership/possession
Changes the ي to ى (different spelling, same meaning)
This pattern فَعُول is used for nouns that describe someone who does something intensively or as their main role
Changed the word pattern to show multiple messengers instead of just one
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it
Added ى at the end to show these are Allah's messengers, with ِ before it to connect the sounds properly
Added damma marks to change from action to noun
Added و and tanween alif to show this is being used as the object of 'they took/made' (وَٱتَّخَذُوٓا۟). The hamza changed to و because it's easier to pronounce.
This is a special particle (حرف) that doesn't come from a root. It's one of the most basic forms in Arabic.
Adding the shadda changes this from a conditional word to one that adds emphasis. Here it emphasizes the following word ٱلَّذِينَ (those who).
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ال (the) turns 'who/which' into 'the one who' - making it point to something specific

steps.m: those who (referring to a group of people who ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed) in the ayah, describing the believers.
This is the basic meaning that all words from this root share
Connected the letters together with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Adding the long alif makes the meaning stronger, changing 'trust' to 'believe strongly'
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people did the action. This word is one of the subjects of كَانَتْ (it was) in this ayah.
This is the basic meaning of doing or working
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb

steps.m: and he did (connected to ءَامَنُوا۟ - believed)
steps.n: Added و to connect this action with the previous word ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed), showing these actions happen together

steps.m: and they did (referring to ٱلَّذِينَ - those who)
steps.n: Changed to plural form to match 'those who' (ٱلَّذِينَ) from earlier in the verse
This root is about things being good or correct
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed the pattern to show someone/something that has this quality
Added the feminine plural ending to show multiple good deeds/things
Added the definite article ٱل and doubled the ص with shadda
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of the verb عَمِلُوا (they did/performed)
This root is about existing or being
Adding the fatha mark makes this a past tense verb
Adding the second fatha completes the past tense verb pattern
Adding ت with sukun shows this refers to جَنَّاتُ (gardens) which is feminine. This word connects to جَنَّاتُ ٱلْفِرْدَوْسِ (gardens of Paradise) in the ayah.
This is a prefix particle that shows direction or purpose
Added هُمْ at the end to show this is 'for them', referring back to 'those who believed' mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: to cover or conceal (like how gardens are hidden by trees)
steps.n: This root is about things being hidden or covered
Adding the vowel marks makes it a proper past tense verb

steps.m: a garden (because it's covered by trees)
steps.n: The root means 'to cover' and adding ة turns it into 'garden' - a place covered by trees
Changes single garden to multiple gardens
The ُ shows this word is the subject of كَانَتْ (were) in this ayah
This is a special word borrowed from Persian/Greek meaning the highest level of Paradise. It doesn't have an Arabic root.
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific

steps.m: of the highest garden of Paradise
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is being described as belonging to something or following another word
This root is about going down or hosting
The damma vowels make it a plural noun meaning 'provisions'

steps.m: as a hospitality gift (describing جَنَّاتُ ٱلْفِرْدَوْسِ)
steps.n: The tanween alif (ًا) shows this word describes how the Gardens of Paradise will serve as a hospitality gift for the believers. It connects to كَانَتْ earlier in the verse.
Added vowels to make it 'he remained' in the past tense
Added alif after first letter and changed vowels to show someone who does the action
Added ين to make it plural, showing multiple people who remain

steps.m: those who remain (plural, in accusative case)
steps.n: Added fatha mark to show this is in the accusative case
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it's showing where the people will stay.
Added ها which refers back to Paradise (جنة, which is feminine in Arabic). This connects with خٰلِدِينَ (abiding forever) to show they will stay in Paradise.
Basic negative particle that makes something not true. In this ayah, it negates the verb يَبْغُونَ (they seek), showing they do not seek.
Added fatha mark to show clear pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows it's happening in the present. The ي at the end is part of the root.

steps.m: they seek/desire (referring to the dwellers of Paradise)
steps.n: Adding ون changes 'he' to 'they' and removes the final root letter ي. This word refers to the Paradise dwellers mentioned earlier in خٰلِدِينَ (those who will abide).
This is a preposition showing movement away from something

steps.m: away from it/her (referring to جَنَّاتِ - gardens)
steps.n: Added هَا which refers back to the gardens (جَنَّاتِ) mentioned earlier. This shows they don't want to leave or move away from these gardens.
Added kasra under first letter and fatha after second letter to create a noun meaning 'change' or 'alternative'

steps.m: a change/alternative (object of يَبْغُونَ - they seek)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is the thing being sought - it's what they don't want to change from (Paradise)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he said' to a command 'say!' by changing middle vowel to 'u' sound
The middle letter و is dropped because it's between two different vowels in a command form
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces hypothetical scenarios - things that could happen but haven't. It's like saying 'what if' in English.
The shadda (double letter mark) makes the 'if' stronger, like saying 'if indeed' or 'even if'. This emphasizes how impossible or unlikely the condition is.
Added fathas to make it past tense. This is the theoretical form before the weak letter change.

steps.m: he/it was (describing the sea/ocean in this ayah)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it has a fatha and comes between two other fathas. This is a common change with weak letters.

steps.m: to spread out, be vast and deep (like water)

steps.n: Added fatha and sukun to make the root into a noun. This word is the subject of كَانَ (was) in the phrase 'if the sea was ink'.
Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's doing something in the sentence. The damma (ُ) shows it's the subject of كَانَ (was).

steps.m: to extend, spread out, or supply continuously

steps.n: Changes the concept of extending/spreading into the liquid tool that does this action. Ink spreads across paper and extends writing.

steps.m: ink (as the supply/resource for كَلِمٰتِ رَبِّى - the words of my Lord)
steps.n: The ًا ending makes 'ink' into a direct object - showing it's being used for something (in this case, for writing God's words)
Changed from action to thing by adding specific vowel pattern and ة
Regular feminine plural formation by adding ات suffix
Changed alif to alif maqsurah (ٰ) for writing style, no meaning change
Added kasra at end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Added لِ at start to show these words are being acted upon or directed towards something
The letter ل has a shadda (double letter) to show it's merged with another ل that came before it
The doubled ب shows this is someone who takes complete care of others
The ِ shows this word is connected to كَلِمٰتُ (words) in the phrase 'words of my Lord'
Added ى (ya) at the end to mean 'my' - like saying 'my Lord' instead of just 'Lord'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added لَ to emphasize that this definitely happened

steps.m: to split open, to spread out wide

steps.n: Forms the noun meaning 'sea' from the concept of something vast and spread out wide
Added 'the' to show this is talking about a specific sea. This word is what 'was' (كَانَ) in the sentence.
Changes from root to basic noun meaning 'before'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it. It tells when something happens - before the words run out.

This is a particle that connects two parts of the sentence. Here it introduces تَنفَدَ (run out/be exhausted) and shows what would happen before something else. In English, we might say 'before the sea would run out'.
Changed from root letters to a complete past tense verb meaning 'it ran out'

steps.m: it would run out/become depleted (before كَلِمٰتُ)
steps.n: Added ت at beginning to show present/future tense. This word describes what would happen to Allah's words, which is mentioned right after (كَلِمٰتُ).
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Added ة to make it into a noun meaning one single word
Added ات to make it plural (many words)

steps.m: words (subject of تَنفَدَ - to run out)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the doer of the action in the sentence. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this style.
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly
The kasra shows this word belongs to or is connected to 'words' (كَلِمٰتِ) in the phrase 'words of my Lord'
Adds ي to show this is 'my' Lord. The word is part of the phrase 'words of my Lord' where 'Lord' belongs to 'words'
This is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical scenarios - like saying 'if something were to happen' (but it hasn't)
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. Together they mean 'and even if' - showing that something would still be true even in an unlikely situation.
Changed from basic root letters to past tense verb by adding an alif between ج and ء
Adding تُ changes it from 'he came' to 'I came'
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Root letters got vowels to make a noun meaning 'like' or 'similar to'
Added بِ at the beginning to show 'with' and kasra at the end because it's connected to what comes after it

steps.m: with its like (referring to the sea mentioned before)
steps.n: Added هِۦ at the end to show 'its', referring back to the sea (البحر) mentioned earlier in the verse
Adding vowel marks makes it a proper past tense verb

steps.m: as additional support/aid (object of جِئْنَا/we brought)
steps.n: Changed from a verb (he extended) to a noun (additional support) by adding tanween at the end. This shows it's the thing being brought in the sentence.
Added damma and sukun to make basic command form
The weak letter و is dropped to make the command shorter and easier to say
This is an emphasis particle that makes what follows more certain

steps.m: only/just (restricting what follows in أَنَا۠ بَشَرٌ)
steps.n: Adding مَآ changes the meaning from emphasis to restriction. This combination is used to limit or specify what follows, like saying 'nothing but' or 'only'
This is the basic form of the first person singular pronoun meaning 'I'
Adding alif at the end makes this a complete standalone pronoun that can be used by itself
The small mark (۠) above the final alif adds emphasis to show this is definitely referring to 'I' as a complete standalone pronoun
Added fathas to connect the letters and make a noun meaning 'human'

steps.m: a human being (predicate of إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠)
steps.n: The double damma (tanwin) at the end shows this is indefinite (any human being). This word describes what 'I am' (أَنَا۠) in the phrase 'I am only a human being'
Changed the root letters into a noun by adding the vowel marks 'i' and 'sukoon' to mean 'like' or 'similar to'
Added shadda for emphasis
Added damma to show this describes the subject of the sentence (referring to the prophet ﷺ in بَشَرٌ)
Added كم to show this means 'like you (plural)' - referring to the people being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Changed to passive present tense showing something is being revealed. The ي at the end changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
The ى (alif maqsurah) at the end shows this is a passive verb
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. It has no root since it's a particle.

steps.m: to me (connected to يُوحَىٰٓ - is revealed)
steps.n: Added shadda with ya to show this means 'to me'. This word connects to يُوحَىٰٓ (is revealed) to say 'is revealed to me'.
This is a particle of emphasis that introduces and emphasizes what comes after it

steps.m: that only/merely (emphasizes limitation in the statement 'I am only a human')
steps.n: Adding ما changes the meaning from simple emphasis to restrictive emphasis, showing limitation or exclusivity

steps.m: that only/merely (emphasizes limitation in the statement 'I am only a human')
steps.n: The alif is written with an elongation mark (مد) to show proper Quranic pronunciation with a longer sound

steps.m: to worship or take as a deity

steps.n: Changes from a root meaning 'worship' into a noun meaning 'something that is worshipped'
The small mark above shows to make the 'aa' sound longer

steps.m: your (plural) god (the one you all worship)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this god belongs to or is worshipped by multiple people

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changed first letter to إِ and added vowels to make it into a noun meaning something that receives worship
The small alif above makes the 'aa' sound longer
Added double damma (ٌ) to show this is a complete noun meaning 'a god' (like saying 'a book' instead of just 'book')
Adding vowels to make it a proper word

steps.m: One (as a complete description of Allah)
steps.n: The alif (ا) was made into a long alif (ٰ) and a double-damma (ٌ) was added at the end to make it a complete standalone description
This is a question word that can also mean 'whoever'. Here it's the subject (doer) of the verb يَرْجُوا (hopes for)
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before, like saying 'so' or 'then' in English
Added fathas to make it past tense. This is the theoretical form before the weak letter change.

The middle letter و changed to ا because it has a fatha and comes between two fathas. This is a common change with weak letters to make words easier to say. This word describes the person who hopes/يَرْجُوا in this ayah.

steps.m: to hope for or expect something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle letter's vowel to sukun, and last letter's vowel to damma. The ا changes back to و.

steps.m: to meet or come face to face with someone/something

steps.n: Changed from action to the name of the action, like changing 'meet' to 'meeting' in English
The word 'meeting' got a special mark (آ) and ending (َ) because it's what someone 'hopes for' in this sentence
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this an intensive form, emphasizing the complete nature of the nurturing
Adding a kasra (i) sound at the end shows this means 'my Lord' instead of just 'Lord'
Added هِ to show the Lord belongs to him (referring back to whoever seeks the meeting mentioned earlier in the verse)
Added ۦ after هِ which helps with pronunciation when connecting to next word
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the past tense verb form
Added يَ at beginning and sukun marks to show present tense action (this word relates to لِقَآءَ 'meeting' in the ayah)
Added ل of command/emphasis at the beginning to create the jussive command form

steps.m: then let him do/perform (in response to hoping to meet his Lord)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is the result or response to hoping to meet Allah mentioned earlier in the ayah
Adding fathas creates the basic past tense verb form

steps.m: a righteous work (object of فَلْيَعْمَلْ - let him do)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is the thing being done (object). In this ayah, it's what Allah is commanding people to do - righteous works.
Basic past tense verb meaning 'he' did the action
Changes from verb (doing) to noun (person who does)
Added tanween alif to show this is the object (thing being done) in the phrase 'let him do righteous deeds'
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates يُشْرِكْ (associate/join partners with)

Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects to the previous command فَلْيَعْمَلْ (then let him do), making the full meaning 'then let him do... and not associate...'
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb

steps.m: he associates partners (present tense, in context of لا - meaning 'not')
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start, changing vowels, and adding sukun at end because it follows لا (not)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to create noun showing the action itself
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by رَبِّهِ (his Lord)
Added بِ to show how the worship is done

Adding shadda (ّ) doubles the middle letter 'ب' to show this is someone who does a lot of caring and developing. Like how a teacher isn't just someone who teaches once, but does it as their main role.
The kasra (ِ) shows this lord belongs to or is connected to something else that comes after it
Adding هِ (hi) at the end shows this Lord belongs to 'him'. This word follows لِقَآءَ (meeting) in the ayah, forming the phrase 'meeting of his Lord'
The ۦٓ is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'one'
Added ًا at the end to show this word is receiving the action of the verb يُشْرِكْ (associates). The meaning becomes 'anyone' in the context of 'not associating anyone with the worship of their Lord'
